En: What can you tell me about Cindy?
Zh: “关于辛迪，你有什么能告诉我的吗？”

En: The *artist*? She scoffs. "Nothing I can do about her, I'm afraid. She ruins the walls faster than I can clean them. Still..." She leans on her broom.
Zh: “那个*艺术家*？”她奚落道。“恐怕我对她是无能为力了。她破坏墙壁的速度比我清洁的还要快。不过……”她靠在自己的扫帚上。

En: She leaves an old lady to her business. More than I can say for others...
Zh: “她不怎么打扰我做事。其他人我就不好说了……”

En: Thank you. He turns to you. "We should go check out his apartment on the balcony, see if he's home."
Zh: “谢谢。”他转向你。“我们应该去阳台上找找他的房间，看他在不在家。”

En: This woman's health is failing her. There's not much to do, not in this damp.
Zh: 女人的健康每况愈下。没什么可以做的，至少在这个潮湿的地方不行。

En: She's the cleaning lady. She knows the floor plan and the residents.
Zh: 她是清洁女工。她知道这里的平面图，也知道这里的住客。

En: And all she gets, too. The coastal wind beats down hard on the coal room door, outside. Splashes of waves make the balcony slippery...
Zh: 而且，她得到的也只有这些。海岸吹来的风在外面用力拍打着煤房的大门。飞溅的浪花让阳台变得湿滑……

En: She hasn't spoken to anyone for a while. Even her sentences feel rusty.
Zh: 她有一阵没跟人说过话了，就连她的词句都感觉有些生锈。

En: Oops... you really didn't get the joke there.
Zh: 哎呀……看来你真的没听懂那个笑话。

En: I thought it was obvious... He stops before he offends you. "Anyway, officer. We *don't* have the witness' name."
Zh: “我觉得已经很明显了……”在冒犯到你之前，他停了下来。“总之，警官。我们还*不知道*目击证人的姓名。”

En: What was so funny about that?
Zh: 这有什么好笑的？

En: They probably just moved -- or died, hopefully somewhere else.
Zh: 他们可能只是搬走了——或者死了，希望是在别处。

En: With how small these rooms are -- wouldn't you want to break the wall down?
Zh: 这些房间这么小——难道你不想把墙拆掉吗？

En: She really doesn't like those *nut jobs*. Bad blood there.
Zh: 她真的不喜欢那些*不正常的家伙*。感觉有些嫌隙。

En: C'mon people, try to keep up *any* standards here. It's not about stars, it's... forget it.
Zh: 拜托，试着保持这里的*任何*标准好吗。并不是关于那些星星的，而是……还是忘了吧。

En: Yes. Radiocomputer wizards are coming. They're gonna save the place -- and the economy.
Zh: “是的。无线电脑巫师要来了。他们会拯救这个地方——还有经济。”

En: No one is coming. There will be nothing but squalor unless we start killing real estate agents.
Zh: “没人会来。除非我们杀害那些房地产中介，不然除了道德败坏之外什么也没有。”

En: Lax women and sexual deviants. That's who'll come. (Spit.)
Zh: “不道德的女人，还有性变态。只有他们会搬进来。”（吐口水。）

En: I'm sure everything will be fine. This apartment building needs *slow* change. Imperceptibly slow.
Zh: “我确定一切都会变好的。这间公寓需要*慢慢*改变。慢的察觉不到。”

En: Yes. Some highly technological wizards are going to save this place. It'll be an visionary innovation utopia.
Zh: “是的。有些高级技术巫师会拯救这个地方。这里会变成一个梦想中的创新理想国。”

En: Oh, I do like wizards, she agrees wholeheartedly. "And people like that in general. They have a lot to tell us about our fates..."
Zh: “哦，我挺喜欢巫师的，”她全心全意地表示赞同。“一般人都挺喜欢的。他们有很多关于命运的话题要讲给我们听……”

En: She means clairvoyants.
Zh: 她的意思是有预知能力的人。

En: *We* won't be killing anyone. And *you* shouldn't say things like that. You're a police officer... there's been enough killing. I've *seen* it... She coughs.
Zh: “*我们*不会杀害任何人的。而且*你*也不应该说这种话。你是一名警官……杀戮已经够多的了。我亲眼*见过*……”她咳嗽起来。

En: She's seen it -- and known those who have been killed.
Zh: 她见过——也知道那些被杀的人。

En: I know those types, yes. There have been some here, but they never make their rent. So they have to move out. I know their type... She coughs.
Zh: “我知道那类人。这里曾经也有过一些，但是他们从来不付租金。所以又要搬出去。我知道这类人……”她咳嗽起来。

En: She appreciates your effort to curb the deviantry and laxness.
Zh: 她欣赏你为控制变态和不道德而做出的努力。

En: Yes, well... She doesn't know what to say so she just coughs and repeats: "I hope they're good people."
Zh: “是的，不过……”她不知道该说些什么，只是咳嗽着重复到：“我希望他们是好人。”

En: Your statements are too vague to comment on.
Zh: 你的言辞太含糊，没办法评论。

En: It's for loons who have no idea how economy works.
Zh: “这是为那些不明白经济运作规律的笨蛋准备的。”

En: Economy? Why should anyone care about economy?! She shrugs and returns to sweeping.
Zh: “经济？为什么每个人都在关心经济？！”她耸耸肩，又回到自己的清扫工作上。

En: It's a symbol for contributing to the demise of our noble state in the name of some made-up world of 'equality'.
Zh: “这是一种符号，它以某种捏造的‘平等’世界的名义，导致了我们荣耀国家的覆灭。”

En: I don't believe in equality. Never seen it work. She shakes her head and returns to sweeping.
Zh: “我不相信平等。从来就没什么用。”她摇摇头，又回到自己的清扫工作上。

En: The police has better things to do. (Nope.)
Zh: “警察还有更重要的工作。”（拒绝。）

En: Sure, I'll go see what I can find. (Take it on.)
Zh: “当然了，我去看看到底是怎么回事。”（接受。）

En: Great. Young people, they're worse than rats, you know -- always littering the hallways with trinkets and empty beer cans...
Zh: “很好。年轻人，他们比老鼠还要糟糕，你知道的——总是把一些小玩意随手丢在走廊里，还有空啤酒罐……”

En: Suit yourself, Mr. Important. No one cares about this building any way...
Zh: “那就请便吧，重要先生。反正也没人在乎这个地方……”

En: Do you know anything about a *secret meeting* that's happening tonight?
Zh: “今晚有场*秘密集会*，你有任何印象吗？”

En: What? She frowns. "No. Nobody tells me anything."
Zh: “什么？”她皱眉。“不。没人告诉我任何事。”

En: How about any unusual activity taking place after hours? Say after 22.00?
Zh: “那你有没有注意到夜里有任何不同寻常的活动迹象？比如说晚上十点以后？”

En: Would she understand if they did?
Zh: 就算有，她会注意到吗？

En: If it ain't my business I keep my nose out of it... She shakes her head and mutters to herself.
Zh: “我从来不多管闲事……”她摇摇头，自言自语。

En: Think we'd all be better off if people kept their noses to themselves.
Zh: “如果大家都各管各的，那可就好喽。”

En: How about a young male in his mid-twenties, dark hair, skinny build? A smoker on the balcony. Know where he lives?
Zh: “我在寻找一位年轻男性，大概25岁左右，黑发，身材纤瘦？会在阳台上抽烟。你知道他住在哪吗？”

En: I'm looking for a young male in his mid-twenties, dark hair, skinny build. A smoker on the balcony.
Zh: “我在寻找一位年轻男性，大概25岁左右，黑发，身材纤瘦。会在阳台上抽烟。”

En: I have a few questions about those apartments...
Zh: “关于这些公寓，我还有些问题……”

En: Thanks, I'm off. [Leave.]
Zh: “谢谢，我先走了。”[离开。]

En: Who are you?
Zh: “你是谁？”

En: I'm no one, just an old woman who cleans these hallways.
Zh: “我谁也不是，只是一个负责清扫走廊的老太婆。”

En: Do you live here?
Zh: “你住在这里吗？”

En: If you can call it living... She spits on the floor, before wiping it off with a broom. "I have a little room upstairs, right next to the coal room."
Zh: “如果你把这叫做住的话……”她朝地上吐了口唾沫，然后用扫帚擦拭干净。“我在楼上有个小房间，就在煤房旁边。”

En: I'm looking for Billie Méjean. Do you know where she lives?
Zh: “我在找比莉·梅让。你知道她住在哪里吗？”

En: I'm looking for Billie Méjean's apartment. Could you direct me?
Zh: “我在找比莉·梅让的公寓。你能帮我指一下吗？”

En: Yes-yes. I know who you mean. The scrawny boy who's always smoking like the devil, right? She looks at the other end of the hallway.
Zh: “是是是。我明白你的意思。那个嗜烟如命、骨瘦如柴的男孩是吗？”她看向走廊的另一端。

En: Ask away, policeman.
Zh: “随便问吧，警察。”

En: Do you know who lived in the foreclosed apartment?
Zh: “你知道那间被没收的公寓里住的是什么人吗？”

En: Why is there a hole in the wall in the abandoned apartment?
Zh: “为什么那间废弃公寓的墙壁上有一个洞？”

En: Is one of the residents on a vacation? Their mailbox is overflowing.
Zh: “是不是有住客出去度假了？他们的信箱都快漫出来了。”

En: That's all, thanks.
Zh: “就这些，谢谢。”

En: No trouble, I just want to talk to him. Do you know where he lives?
Zh: “没什么，我只想跟他聊聊。你知道他住在哪里吗？”

En: It's about Victor, isn't it? She narrows her pale grey eyes. "It's always about Victor with her... You'll find the Méjeans in apartment #20 upstairs. It's one of the doors on the balcony."
Zh: 她眯起浅灰色的眼睛。“是关于维克托的，对吗？永远都是维克托和她的事……梅让就住在楼上20号公寓。大门在阳台上。”

En: Talk! The cleaning lady starts laughing, but it turns into a violent coughing spasm. She squeezes her broom, trying to catch her breath.
Zh: “聊聊！”清洁女工笑了起来，结果却变成了一阵剧烈的咳嗽。她用力握紧了扫帚，想要喘口气。

En: He lives upstairs in room #28. Go to the balcony. It's one of those doors there, she points east. "He's usually home in the evening."
Zh: “他住在楼上28号房间。去阳台上看看，房门就在那附近，”她指向东边。“他一般晚上在家。”

En: She mumbles some kind of a response, then hacks something into her handkerchief.
Zh: 清洁女工嘴里嘟囔着什么作为回应，然后往手帕里咳出了什么东西。

En: I am looking for the parents of a kid named Cuno.
Zh: “我正在找一个小孩的父母，他叫坤诺。”

En: De Ruyters are at the end of this hallway, right next to the communal bathroom.
Zh: “德鲁伊特住在这条走廊的尽头，就在公共浴室的旁边。”

En: The hell am I supposed to know? The cleaning lady shrugs, her mouth puckered like a dried fig. "Another nut job, I assume."
Zh: “见鬼了，我怎么知道？”清洁女工耸耸肩，嘴巴像无花果干一样皱巴巴的。“我猜，又是个不正常的家伙吧。”

En: People come and go, I don't keep an eye on everyone.
Zh: “大家都进进出出的，我总不能盯着每一个人吧。”

En: Who lives in apartment #10?
Zh: “10号公寓住的是什么人？”

En: Know anything about the young communist who lives behind the padlocked door?
Zh: “你认识那个住在上锁房间里的康米主义青年吗？”

En: Who lives behind the padlocked door?
Zh: “那扇有挂锁的门后住着什么人？”

En: Tell me about the man who lives at the end of the hallway -- Uuno de Ruyter.
Zh: “跟我说说住在走廊尽头的那人——乌诺·德鲁伊特。”

En: I don't want to talk about de Ruyters, the cleaning lady mutters. She starts digging for something in her pockets.
Zh: 清洁女工开始在口袋里翻找着什么。“我不想提德鲁伊特，”她嘟囔着。

En: Oh, that one is a scientist, a future scholar! Suddenly the old lady's face is beaming.
Zh: “哦，那是个科学家，一个未来的大学者！”老妇人的脸突然亮了起来。

En: Astrology?
Zh: “占星术？”

En: Something to do with all those stars around his door. He asked me to leave his drawings up on the wall.
Zh: “跟他家大门周围那些星星有关。他让我不要擦掉墙上那些画。”

En: You mean the star-and-antlers? I think that's the symbol of communism.
Zh: “你是说星星和鹿角吗？我觉得那个是康米主义的符号。”

En: Some lunatic lost his mind... All kinds of morons pass through these halls.
Zh: “有些疯子脑子不好使了……各种各样的傻瓜都会经过这些走廊。”

En: Addicts, all of them... And sometimes I hear them screaming. She winces.
Zh: “有瘾的人，全都是……有时候我还会听见他们尖叫。”她有些畏缩。

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不呢？”

En: The symbol of what now?
Zh: “你说是什么符号？”

En: You know, the ones who support the working class against Capital.
Zh: “你知道的，就是那些支持工人阶级，对抗资本家的人。”

En: Never heard of it, the cleaning lady replies, before another coughing spell takes hold of her.
Zh: “从没听说过，”清洁女工回答着，然后又咳嗽了起来。

En: They don't like me cleaning behind their door, think I'm listening in on their fights.
Zh: “他们不喜欢我在门口打扫，以为我在偷听他们吵架。”

En: No one lives there, it's been empty for months.
Zh: “没人住，已经空了好几个月。”

En: But I talked to someone through the door.
Zh: “但是我刚跟里面的人说过话。”

En: Impossible, the cleaning lady says, shaking her head, "I would know if someone had moved in there." She pauses, eyeing the hallway.
Zh: “不可能，”清洁女工摇头回答到，“如果有人搬进来的话，我肯定会知道的。”她停顿了一下，眼睛直勾勾地盯着走廊。

En: But I heard someone walking inside.
Zh: “但是我听见有人在里面走动。”

En: Give me a moment. An elderly woman is leaning on her broom, her knuckles white as bone. She seems to be having difficulty breathing.
Zh: “让我喘口气。”一个老妇人倚靠在扫帚上，指关节像骷髅一般惨白。她好像呼吸起来比较困难。

En: The cold never does any good for my bronchitis... She sneezes into a dirty handkerchief.
Zh: “这冷天对我的支气管炎一点好处都没有……”她对着一条肮脏的手帕打了个喷嚏。

En: Are you alright? Should I call a doctor?
Zh: “你还好吗？要不要我帮你叫个医生？”

En: I'm fine, fine, don't you worry about me! She starts coughing, red spots appearing on her cheeks.
Zh: “我很好，没事的，不用担心我！”她开始咳嗽，脸颊上浮现出一些红斑。

En: You're still worried. It's very worrying.
Zh: 你还是有点担心。这种状况本来就很让人担心。

En: What's he in trouble for?
Zh: “他遇到什么麻烦了？”

En: I think he studies astrology at the community college. Education's good, I always tell them to study.
Zh: “我觉得他应该是在社区大学里学习占星学。教育很好，我总是告诉他们要多学习。”

En: I am looking for Martin Martinaise.
Zh: “我正在找马丁·马丁内斯。”

En: Oh, you'll find plenty of Martins here, don't you worry! She smiles a gap toothed smile when she hears you mention the name.
Zh: 清洁女工听你说出这个名字的时候，她咧开嘴笑了。“哦，你会在这里找到很多个马丁的，不用担心！”

En: What do you mean? I wasn't joking.
Zh: “你什么意思？我没有开玩笑。”

En: No, you don't understand. I'm looking for a real person named Martin Martinaise. He told me he's Martin Martinaise.
Zh: “不，你不明白。我正在找一个叫马丁·马丁内斯的真人。他告诉我他叫马丁·马丁内斯。”

En: Pea-brain, someone played a trick on you. Martin Martinaise is a name for *anyone* from Martinaise. Like Jim Jamrock or Raoul Revachol.
Zh: “傻瓜，有人捉弄你呢。马丁·马丁内斯是*所有*马丁内斯人的统称。就像吉姆·加姆洛克或者拉乌尔·瑞瓦肖。”

En: Somewhere in the building a child starts crying. You hear a radio tuned to a talk show and someone taking a shower.
Zh: 这栋建筑的某个地方传来一阵小孩的哭声。你听见收音机调到脱口秀的频道，还有人正在洗澡。

En: Give me a moment... The cleaning lady still seems to be having difficulty breathing.
Zh: “稍等一下……”清洁女工似乎还是有些呼吸困难。

En: Let's do it later... [Leave.]
Zh: 以后再说吧……[离开。]

En: This won't take long, I only have a few questions.
Zh: “不会很久的，我只有几个问题。”

En: First, I'm the policeman that asked you to open the door. Are you sure you don't want to see my badge?
Zh: “首先，我是一个警察，要求你开门。你确定不想看看我的警徽吗？”

En: Go ahead then. What do you want to know, policeman?
Zh: “那就继续吧。你想知道什么，警官？”

En: I already told you I don't care about any badges.
Zh: “我已经说过了，我不在乎什么警徽。”

En: Now, what do you want from me, policeman?
Zh: “那么，你有什么事情吗，警官？”

En: He's wanted for murder. He's goin' away for life.
Zh: “他是个被通缉的杀人犯。他就要离开人世了。”

En: Murder! The cleaning lady starts laughing, but it turns into a violent coughing spasm. She squeezes her broom, trying to catch her breath.
Zh: “杀人犯！”清洁女工笑了起来，结果却变成了一阵剧烈的咳嗽。她用力握紧了扫帚，想要喘口气。

En: I'd wager you've got the wrong man. But even so, you're out of luck, policeman -- I saw him leave the building just before you came in.
Zh: “我敢打赌，你找错人了。不过即便是这样，你也不太走运啊，警官——在你还没进来之前，我就看见他出去了。”

En: Even so, I'd like to talk to him.
Zh: “就算是这样，我也想跟他聊聊。”

En: Are you sure you don't mean astronomy?
Zh: “你确定你的意思不是天文学吗？”

En: That's what I said, astrology.
Zh: “我说的就是这个，占星学。”

En: Communism. It's a political ideology.
Zh: “康米主义。是一种政治上的意识形态。”

En: It's an ideology. It has some noble aims, but it's also been used to justify terrible bloodshed.
Zh: “是一种意识形态。它有一些崇高的目标，但也曾经被用来为可怕的流血杀戮做辩护。”

En: Communism. You know, like in the world revolution?
Zh: “康米主义。你知道的，就像世界大革命那样？”

En: Huh, the cleaning lady says, "I thought Revachol *was* the capital."
Zh: “啊，”清洁女工说到，“我以为瑞瓦肖*是*首都呢。”

En: The cleaning lady shrugs and returns to sweeping.
Zh: 清洁女工耸耸肩，又回到自己的清扫工作上。

En: The clash of ideas and atrocities are as far from her world as fairies and wizards.
Zh: 在她的世界里，思想和暴行的冲突离她很远，就像仙女和巫师一样。

En: Ah! the cleaning lady says, leaning on her broom. "All I know about politics is that it has brought us more harm than good."
Zh: “啊！”清洁女工靠在自己的扫帚上说到。“我只知道政治给我们带来了更多伤害，而不是好处。”

En: The lieutenant shakes his head, as though to say 'let it go.'
Zh: 警督摇摇头，像是在说“别管了。”

En: He's actually *not* wanted for murder. We just want to talk with him.
Zh: “他其实*不是*被通缉的杀人犯。我们只想跟他聊聊。”

En: The de Ruyters... She frowns. "He's chained it and fallen into a stupor again."
Zh: 清洁女工皱起眉头。“德鲁伊特……他在门上挂了链条，又昏睡过去了。”

En: So she doesn't. Not after *that one time*. There's a *that one time* here.
Zh: 所以她没有。*那一次*之后就没有了。这里的关键点是*那一次*。

En: It didn't get physical. She heard something she doesn't want to hear again.
Zh: 并没有到动手动脚的地步。她听到了某些不想再听到的东西。

En: It was dirtier there. After the janitor's closet. She avoids the end of the hallway.
Zh: 这里更脏了。就在清洁工具柜的背后。她避开了走廊的尽头。

En: I saw unpaid utility bills -- aren't they going to be thrown out?
Zh: “我看见一些没有支付的物业账单——他们不会被赶出去吗？”

En: We saw unpaid utility bills -- aren't they going to be evicted?
Zh: “我们看见一些没有支付的物业账单——他们不会被赶出去吗？”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: I don't like talking about them, she says mostly to herself.
Zh: “我不想提他们的事，”这句话她几乎是说给自己听的。

En: No one likes them in this building. She shakes her head. "It's only because of the kid they haven't been thrown out."
Zh: “这栋楼里没人喜欢他们。”她摇摇头。“他们还没被赶出去，只是因为那个孩子。”

En: I don't like talking about those people, she looks down the hallway.
Zh: “我不喜欢提那些人，”她看着走廊的方向。

En: The less said about those people, the better.
Zh: “越少提那些人越好。”

En: Sounds like he has.
Zh: “听起来他是这样的。”

En: She's scared of room #12.
Zh: 她很害怕12号房间。

En: Who lives in apartment #12? I heard snoring.
Zh: “谁住在12号房间？我听见里面有鼾声。”

En: Maybe it's those *counterculture people* again? Breaking into our house like it's a public space... You're a policeman, be good and take a look, will you?
Zh: “也许又是那些*反主流文化分子*？闯入我们的房子，当这里是公共场所一样……你是一个警察，那就当个好人，帮着去看一眼，好吗？”

En: I didn't find any counterculture people in apartment #10 -- it was just a real estate agent setting up the room for new tenants.
Zh: “我没在10号房间发现反主流文化分子——只有一个房地产中介在布置房间，准备招揽新租客。”

En: I see... She takes out her handkerchief and wipes her nose. "I hope some good people are finally going to move in. This place needs them."
Zh: “明白了……”她掏出手帕，擦了擦鼻子。“希望能有些好人搬进来。这个地方需要他们。”

En: It's barely bigger than a closet, but I don't complain, no... She juts out her chin, eyes shining. "I have my bed and my aching bones to keep me company and that's all I need from this world."
Zh: “它比壁柜大不了多少，但我不是想抱怨，不是的……”——她伸长下巴，眼睛闪闪发光——“我有自己的床，还有这把疼痛的老骨头陪着我，在这个世界上，我只需要这些就够了。”

En: You can hear animated voices coming from the other side of the grille.
Zh: 你能听到从铁窗另一边传来的的活力十足的声音。

En: As you approach the metal grille you can hear several voices having what appears to be an animated discussion.
Zh: 你走向铁窗，逐渐能够听出里面有几个人正在激烈地争论着什么。

En: This must be it. Beyond this door lies the beating heart of radical communism in Martinaise.
Zh: 一定就是这里了。在这扇门的背后，马丁内斯仅存的激进康米主义者的心脏正跳动着。

En: Somehow the night air softens the smell of trash and sea brine. As the breeze pulls through the canvas like a shuttle through a loom you catch a hint of something unexpected, something earthy, warm, and burnt...
Zh: 不知怎么，垃圾与海腥味在夜晚的空气中，也变得不那么冲鼻了。你听着晚风如梭般吹拂帆布，随之注意到了某种出乎意料的东西，温暖、带着泥土芬芳与焦味的……

En: The acrid smell of failure...
Zh: 象征着失败的苦涩气味……

En: No, that's just slightly burnt coffee. A smell you would recognise anywhere.
Zh: 不，那只是有点烧糊的咖啡而已。你在哪里都能闻到的味道。

En: Just look at these pigs, sniffing about after-hours...
Zh: “瞧瞧这对猪猡，下了班还跑来这里乱闻。”

En: Just look at this pig, sniffing about after-hours...
Zh: “瞧瞧这只猪猡，下了班还跑来这里乱闻。”

En: Why didn't you say the secret door was *right behind you*?
Zh: “你为什么没说那扇暗门*就在你身后*？”

En: Hey, it's past curfew! Get to bed, Cindy!
Zh: “嘿，已经是宵禁时间了！回去睡觉，辛迪！”

En: (Ignore her.)
Zh: （无视她。）

En: Must have slipped my mind. You know how it goes. She gives you a lackadaisical shrug.
Zh: “那我一定是忘了。忘性大，没办法……”她无动于衷地耸耸肩。

En: Go ahead and arrest me, officer. I swear I'll go peacefully. You can feel her smirk through the dark.
Zh: “上来逮捕我啊，警官。我保证不反抗。”你能透过夜色想象出她的冷笑。

En: The metal grille is cool to the touch.
Zh: 铁栅格摸起来冷冰冰的。

En: You notice the lieutenant is looking uncustomarily anxious. His posture is rigid, his right hand hovers near the zipper of his jacket.
Zh: 你注意到警督看起来有些不同寻常地焦虑。他的姿势紧绷着，右手悬在夹克拉链附近。

En: He wants so badly to draw his Armistice, but he also doesn't *want* to want to draw it.
Zh: 他很想拔出止战者，但同时又很*不希望*自己想拔出它。

En: You okay, Kim?
Zh: “你还好吗，金？”

En: Relax, they're only communists.
Zh: “别紧张，他们只是康米主义分子而已。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Oh, I'm fine. I was practically *born* to infiltrate underground communist cells... He takes a quick look over his shoulder.
Zh: “哦，我没事。我*天生*擅长混入地下康米主义组织。”他快速地瞥你一眼。

En: Something's not right here. The equation doesn't quite balance... He taps his foot and takes a deep breath.
Zh: “这里有点问题。公式没有配平……”他用脚尖点了点地面，深呼吸一口。

En: Well, detective. It seems we've located your underground cell... He looks over the balcony, down toward the water.
Zh: “警探，我们似乎发现了你的地下组织……”他越过阳台，向下对着海水说道。

En: Which is just to say: We should be prepared for *any* eventuality.
Zh: “换句话说：我们可能会遇到*任何*事。要做好心理准备。”

En: The metal grille is cool to the touch. The long stretches of canvas billow like sails in the night breeze.
Zh: 铁栅格摸起来冷冰冰的。帆布在晚风中如船帆般鼓起。

En: Try to listen.
Zh: 试着听里面的动静

En: Hey, it's me again! (Knock on the grille.)
Zh: “嘿，我又来了！”（敲敲铁栅格。）

En: Bang on the grille.
Zh: 猛拍铁栅格。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The clang of metal reverberates all along the scaffolding. The voices coming from the other side fall silent.
Zh: 金属的当啷声在整个脚手架内回荡。铁窗另一边的声音消失了……

En: You can make out at least two separate voices.
Zh: 你能听出至少两道不同的声音……

En: You hear the voices conferring once again.
Zh: 你听到里面的人又交谈起来……

En: A pair of frightened hares cowering in some dark crevice of their burrow.
Zh: 一对受惊的小兔子，躲在它们的地洞里瑟瑟发抖。

En: The voices are male, you think. Beyond that, you can't tell...
Zh: 你觉得里面是男人的声音。除此之外，你什么也听不出……

En: He came back? There's a note of surprise in the voice. "Do you have the passphrase this time?"
Zh: “他又来了？”声音听起来有些惊讶。“这回你知道暗号了吗？”

En: Who's there? Is that you, Maurice?
Zh: “谁在外面？是你吗，莫里斯？”

En: It's not Maurice, it's the police!
Zh: “不是莫里斯，是警察！”

En: That's right. It is I... Maurice.
Zh: “没错，是我……莫里斯。”

En: Are you all communists? I'm looking for some communists.
Zh: “你们都是康米主义者吗？我在找几个康米主义者。”

En: Ever hear of the Wayfarer Act, you Coalition pawn? Come back when you've got a warrant, or when you've woken up to your own class interests.
Zh: “听说过旅人法案吗，联盟的走狗？除非你弄来了搜查证，或者对你的阶级利益觉醒，否则别来烦我们。”

En: That's exactly why I'm here! I'm trying to get organised. Can you help me?
Zh: “我就是为了这个来的！我想加入组织。你们能帮我吗？”

En: Come crawling back already? Get out of here, traitor!
Zh: “这么快就爬回来了？滚出去，叛徒！”

En: Uhh...
I'm not actually Maurice. I'm trying to get organised. Can you help me?
Zh: “其实我不是莫里斯。我想加入组织。你们能帮我吗？”

En: That's right! Another voice jumps in. "Take your miniscule, bourgeois turnips and get lost!"
Zh: “就是这样！”另一道声音插进来。“拿着你的迷你资产阶级小萝卜滚蛋！”

En: Face it, sire, your little subterfuge isn't leading anywhere.
Zh: 承认吧，大人，您的托词一点用都没有。

En: Who said communist? Did *we* say communist? Get out of here.
Zh: “谁说了康米主义？*我们*说了康米主义吗？滚出去。”

En: Listen, I've already built like .0001% of communism but now I need to get organised. Can you help me?
Zh: “听着，我已经建设了大概0.0001%的康米主义，但现在我需要加入组织。你们能帮我吗?”

En: For a moment, silence.
Zh: 里面沉默着……

En: A slight sensation of static electricity, as before a storm. A miniature conference is taking place inside, and you are the subject.
Zh: 如同风暴前积累的静电，铁窗之内正进行着一场微型会议，而你就是议题。

En: What's the passphrase?
Zh: “暗号是什么？”

En: It's 'We await the silent mole empire'.
Zh: “是‘我们等待着寂静的鼹鼠帝国’。”

En: It's 'The end times cometh'.
Zh: “是‘末日将至’。”

En: It's 'Disco Supernova'.
Zh: “是‘迪斯科超新星’。”

En: It's 'Blessed are the sausage grinders'.
Zh: “是‘赞美绞肉机’。”

En: It's 'Remember Dobreva and Abadanaiz'.
Zh: “是‘不要忘记多布列娃与阿巴达奈斯’。”

En: Can't I get a hint at least?
Zh: “你们能提示我一下吗？”

En: Look, I don't know your passphrase. Could you just let me in?
Zh: “我实在不知道你们的暗号是什么。你们就不能直接放我进去吗？”

En: Not a chance. We can't afford to compromise our operation, not at this point. Come back when you know the passphrase.
Zh: “想都别想。我们可不会冒着破坏活动的风险，至少现在不行。等你知道暗号了再过来。”

En: Look at that, the pig's trying to break in without the passphrase. Tsk, tsk. The girl shakes her head slowly.
Zh: “瞧瞧，猪猡不知道暗号就想闯进去呢。啧啧。”女孩缓缓摇了摇头。

En: Let's go, detective. We'll get nowhere standing around in the rain. The lieutenant wipes back a wet lock of hair.
Zh: “我们走吧，警探。这样站在雨里是没用的。”警督将一绺打湿的头发抹回脑后。

En: Let's go, detective. We'll get nowhere standing around here in the dark. The moonlight reflects white in the lieutenant's glasses.
Zh: “我们走吧，警探。这样站在夜里是没用的。”警督的眼镜折射出皎洁的月光。

En: Let's go, detective. We'll get nowhere standing around in the snow. The lieutenant brushes a few scales of snow off his jacket.
Zh: “我们走吧，警探。这样站在雪里是没用的。”警督从夹克上掸下几片雪花。

En: There's no response. You begin to wonder whether they've slipped out some back way...
Zh: 没有回答。你疑心他们是不是已经从什么秘密出口溜走了……

En: No! They're still there. You can *feel* them back there.
Zh: 不！他们还在里面。你能*感觉到*他们的存在。

En: Alright. The key's taped to the back of the doorframe. Just make sure you put it back when you're done or we'll all be locked out...
Zh: “好吧。钥匙贴在门框后面。等你走的时候记得贴回去，否则我们都要被锁在外面了。”

En: And do watch the concrete. It just kind of... falls away... in places.
Zh: “注意混凝土。它有点……四分五裂……”

En: *Charmant*. After you, detective.
Zh: “*迷人*。你先请，警探。”

En: Have fun at your *underground meeting*, pig. Hope it's a *blast*.
Zh: “好好享受你的*地下集会*吧，猪猡。希望它*激动人心*。”

En: Are you locked out of your radiocomputer or something? No, it's not '1234'.
Zh: “你忘了自己的无线电脑的密码了？不，不是‘1234’。”

En: No, it's not. We don't do ironic passphrases here.
Zh: “不，不是它。我们不用讽刺性暗号。”

En: Not even close.
Zh: “离谱。”

En: He said something about moles. Perhaps he's just a night-wandering lunatic.
Zh: “他说了鼹鼠什么什么。也许他只是个大半夜闲逛的疯子。”

En: Did he say something about sausages? The voice appears completely confused by your response
Zh: “他刚说了绞肉机什么？”门里的声音似乎对你感到十分困惑。

En: Perhaps he's drunk? the other voice asks.
Zh: “也许他喝醉了？”另一道声音说。

En: Two voices, both male. Approximately early 20s.
Zh: 两道声音，都是男性。约20岁上下。

En: ... Careful with those now...
Zh: “……现在得小心点……”

En: I have it. It's going to work, I can feel it...
Zh: “有我在呢，会成功的，我有这种预感……”

En: ... years? Months? Weeks?
Zh: ……几年？几个月？几个星期？

En: He's trying to call you a pig.
Zh: 他是想说你是猪。

En: Pig, he says.
Zh: 他说，‘猪头’。

En: Don't forget his face: the bloated and reddened cheeks, the bulbous nose... This would be Cuno after 30 years of substance abuse, if he drank too.
Zh: 别忘了他的脸：浮肿而发红的面颊，蒜头鼻……这就是滥用药物30年后的坤诺，如果他也喝酒的话。

En: The sight you're beholding right now is probably the reason he doesn't.
Zh: 你现在看到的景象可能就是他看不到的原因。

En: A bundle of clothes heaped on the bed. A stained parka, some towels and a duvet. Some socks even.
Zh: 一摊衣服堆在床上。一件沾有污渍的派克大衣，几条毛巾还有一条羽绒被。甚至还有些袜子。

En: Figure out what he's trying to say.
Zh: 弄清楚他想说什么。

En: Dresscode: RCM official.
Zh: 着装要求：RCM警官。

En: He's trying to call you pigs.
Zh: 他想说你是猪。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Kim, is this thing even *alive*?
Zh: “金，这东西还是*活着*的吗？”

En: I'm afraid it is. Look, it *moves*. He points to a fleshy lump sticking out from the other end of the blanket. The limb seems to be twitching from time to time.
Zh: “恐怕是的。看，它*动了*。”他指着毛毯另一端伸出的肉块。肢体似乎时不时地在抽搐。

En: And look, the other foot is camouflaged by a striped sock bearing the name MAXTOR on the sole. Three toes are poking out of a hole.
Zh: 看啦，另一只脚被底部印着*迈拓*字样的条纹袜子伪装了起来。三只脚趾头从一个破洞里戳了出来。

En: Maxtor is a gas company. He's wearing free socks from a gas company. They probably came with the bills.
Zh: 迈拓是一家燃气公司。他穿着燃气公司提供的免费袜子，可能跟账单一起来的。

En: Is this.... Cuno's father we're seeing?
Zh: “我们看到的……真是坤诺的父亲吗？”

En: (Whisper.) "I took your amphetamine, old man."
Zh: （低语。）“我拿走你的安非他命了，老家伙。”

En: Silence... Only heat emanates from the sleeping body.
Zh: 沉默……只有热量从睡着的人身上发散出来。

En: Well, judging by the colour of his hair I would say yes, it is.
Zh: “从他的发色来判断，我会说是的，没错。”

En: Doesn't look like he's going fuck anyone up any time soon.
Zh: “看起来他近期不会给别人带去什么麻烦。”

En: I was expecting something... worse.
Zh: “我期望的是某种……更糟糕的东西。”

En: No, it really doesn't. The lieutenant tilts his head. "This looks very hard to recover from."
Zh: “不，真不会。”警督歪着头。“这个看起来很难恢复。”

En: The lieutenant's right -- the man's unwashed hair bears a familiar ginger tone. Even the hair on his chest is coppery. The light from the window falls into his half-open eyes.
Zh: 警督是对的——男人肮脏又乱蓬蓬的头发带着一种熟悉的姜黄色调。就连胸口的毛发都是紫铜色的。灯光从窗外照射进来，落在他半睁的双眼上。

En: I can't believe it, Cuno's dad isn't a loser! It *must* be someone else.
Zh: “我简直不敢相信，坤诺的爸爸可不是什么失败者！这*肯定*是别人。”

En: I think he's still *quite* bad... I mean, what he has come to. The lieutenant tilts his head.
Zh: “我觉得他还是*挺糟糕*的……我是说，他这副样子。”警督歪着头。

En: A pair of half-open bug eyes is staring back at you from the dark, empty and frozen. It's clear that the person behind them is not awake.
Zh: 一双半睁的圆眼在黑暗中回望着你，空洞而僵滞。显然，那双眼背后的男人没有醒来。

En: This is serious damage, I'm still not sure he's not dead.
Zh: “伤的挺严重的，我还不确定他到底死没死。”

En: Hold on, what happened to his eyes?
Zh: “等一下，他的眼睛怎么了？”

En: Fuckn... k... f...pi... Suddenly the man starts growling. Three words manage to escape his mouth, along with a strong stench of alcohol.
Zh: 突然间，男人开始咆哮。伴随着一阵浓烈的酒臭味，他的嘴里好容易才蹦出三个词：“操……吉……皮……”

En: A groan rises from the man's throat, dry like a death rattle... He's trying to say something in his sleep.
Zh: 男人的喉咙里发出一声呻吟，就像濒死的哀鸣一般干枯……像是想在睡梦中说些什么。

En: Look, he's trying to communicate.
Zh: “看，他想试着交流。”

En: Can't you tell? It happens to exceptionally *committed* substance abusers. They fall asleep with their eyelids still open. Not a pretty sight.
Zh: “你看不出来吗？这种情况只会发生在那些特别*忠诚的*滥用药物者身上。他们睡着的时候眼皮也是张开的。那副光景可算不上是赏心悦目。”

En: The man groans once again, but his tongue keeps failing him: It's impossible to make out the syllables. A hand emerges from the blankets, trying to gesticulate something...
Zh: 男人又发出一声呻吟，但他的舌头总是不听使唤：想辨认出音节是不可能的。他从毛毯下伸出一只手，试着比划着什么……

En: The bear is trying to wake up!
Zh: 这头熊想醒过来！

En: What did you just say?!
Zh: “你刚说什么？！”

En: You're awake!
Zh: “你醒了！”

En: You're awake... He's awake!
Zh: “你醒了……他醒了！”

En: Maybe we should help him somehow?
Zh: “也许我们应该想办法帮帮他？”

En: Pigs, he says.
Zh: 他说，‘猪头’。

En: Fuck you. Die.
Zh: “他妈的。去死吧。”

En: At least he got to say his piece.
Zh: “至少他还能表达自己的意见。”

En: What is there to do? We could turn him on his side, so he doesn't choke on his own vomit. But he's already on his side...
Zh: “有什么办法呢？我们可以帮他翻到侧面，这样就不会被自己的呕吐物噎死。不过他已经侧过来了……”

En: We could take him to Remedie or Saint Batiste, but he doesn't have money for medical services. The Almshouse would turn him down...
Zh: “我们可以把他送去救济站或者是圣巴蒂斯特，不过他应该没钱支付医疗服务。公立救济院会拒收……”

En: And then falls back again, limp and defeated by sleep. A loud snore escapes his mouth.
Zh: 然后又翻了回去，软弱无力，被睡眠打败了。一阵响亮的鼾声从他嘴里跑了出来。

En: The man groans once again, but his tongue keeps failing him: It's impossible to make out the syllables. A hand emerges from the blankets, trying to gesticulate something...
Zh: 男人又发出一声呻吟，但他的舌头总是不听使唤：想辨认出音节是不可能的。他从毛毯下伸出一只手，试着比划着什么……

En: And then it dawns upon you, clear and surreal...
Zh: 然后你突然恍然大悟，犹如醍醐灌顶、有如神助……

En: His hand falls back on the bed, limp and defeated. A loud snore escapes his mouth... He's asleep again.
Zh: 他的手又落回到床上，挫败无力。一阵响亮的鼾声从他嘴里跑了出来……他又睡着了。

En: The pile of blankets grunts miserably.
Zh: 那堆毛毯发出痛苦的呼噜声。

En: He wouldn't be too thrilled to learn you stole his stash. It was the last thing keeping him functional.
Zh: 如果他知道你偷了他的私藏，也不会太激动。这是最后能让他保持正常的东西。

En: Slowly reach out your hand.
Zh: 慢慢伸出你的手。

En: Nothing to see here then. [Leave.]
Zh: 这里没什么好看的。[离开。]

En: The ageing alcoholic is still there, breathing... The air in the room is stale.
Zh: 那个上了年纪的老酒鬼还在这里，呼吸着……房间里的空间充满了陈腐的味道。

En: A lump of flesh is sticking out from under the blanket. It seems to be twitching from time to time, like the paw of an animal who's having a bad dream.
Zh: 一个肉块从毛毯下面伸了出来。有时还会有些抽搐，就像一只陷入噩梦的动物的爪子。

En: Are you saying a man who looks like Cuno broke into his father's apartment and passed out in his father's bed?
Zh: “你是说一个像坤诺的人闯进了他父亲的公寓，然后在他父亲的床上晕过去了？”

En: Somewhere, just out of earshot, a glass bottle explodes. A man's voice shouts "You fucking kids!" And then the screech of sneakers pounding the pavement, a girl's voice yelling "Got him!" and a painfully shrill laugh piercing the air...
Zh: 某个地方，就在听力所不及的范围内，一个玻璃瓶爆裂开来。一个男人的声音大喊到“你这个该死的小屁孩！”接着，是运动鞋撞击路面发出的尖锐声响，接着是一个女孩在喊“抓住他！”，还有一阵刺耳的笑声划破长空……

En: Excellent form.
Zh: “状态极佳。”

En: Poor guy.
Zh: “可怜的家伙。”

En: Are you going to let a semi-conscious degenerate disrespect you?
Zh: 你打算让一个半梦半醒的败类对你无礼吗？

En: Hey, talk to me! Are you even alive?
Zh: “喂，跟我说说话！你还活着吗？”

En: Hey, are you really Cuno's father?
Zh: “喂，你真是坤诺的父亲吗？”

En: The light from the window is weak, but it seems like he is. His wild, unwashed mane bears a familiar ginger tone. Even the hair on his chest is coppery.
Zh: 窗外的灯光很微弱，不过看起来他就是。他那肮脏又乱蓬蓬的头发带着一种熟悉的姜黄色调。就连胸口的毛发都是紫铜色的。

En: No response. A pair of half-open bug eyes is staring back at you from the dark, empty and frozen. It's clear that the person behind them is not awake.
Zh: 没有回应。一双半睁开的圆眼在黑暗中回望着你，空洞而僵滞。显然，那双眼背后的男人没有醒来。

En: His half-open eyes give him the look of a dead man. But he is in there... and not enjoying himself.
Zh: 那双半睁开的眼睛让他看起来就像死人一样。但他还活着……并不是很享受。

En: Suddenly the man starts growling. Three words manage to escape his mouth, along with a strong stench of alcohol: 'Fucking... kipt... f****ts...'
Zh: 突然间，男人开始咆哮。伴随着一阵浓烈的酒臭味，他的嘴里好容易才蹦出三个词：“操……吉皮特……我操……”

En: Don't forget his face: the bloated and reddened cheeks, the bulbous nose... This would be Cuno after 30 years of alcohol and substance abuse.
Zh: 别忘了他的脸：浮肿而发红的面颊，蒜头鼻……经过30年的酗酒和滥用药物，坤诺也会变成这个样子。

En: There's still plenty to be scared of here. Just not what you thought.
Zh: 这里还有很多可怕的东西。只是和你想的不太一样。

En: Do you need help?
Zh: “你需要帮助吗？”

En: The pile of blankets grunts. It's hard to say if there's anything left you could do.
Zh: 那堆毛毯咕哝着。很难说你还有什么能做的。

En: I can't believe it, Cuno's dad can't be a loser! It *must* be someone else.
Zh: “我简直不敢相信，坤诺的爸爸可不是什么失败者！这*肯定*是别人。”

En: I was expecting something... scarier.
Zh: “我期望的是某种……更吓人的东西。”

En: Doesn't look like you're going to fuck anyone up any time soon, little man.
Zh: “看来你近期内不会给别人带去什么麻烦，小伙子。”

En: Nocturnal lagophthalmos. The likelihood of falling asleep with partially open eyelids rises after stimulant use and heavy drinking.
Zh: 夜兔眼。在服用思必得和大量饮酒后，半睁眼皮睡着的可能性就会增加。

En: You see a 60-year-old fat red-headed man passed out from large amounts of alcohol. And god knows what else. The smell of shit rises from his mouth.
Zh: 你看见一个60岁左右的红发老男人，由于严重酗酒而不省人事。天知道还有什么其他的。一阵恶臭从他的嘴里散发出来。

En: Hold up, lieutenant. Look at that pile of clothes.
Zh: “等一下，警督。看看这堆衣服。”

En: Something underneath there is breathing.
Zh: 下面有什么东西在呼吸。

En: It doesn't give a shit that you're a cop! Stop your hand now or you're gonna die!
Zh: 它根本就不在意你是不是警察！现在赶快停手，不然你会死的！

En: Keep extending your hand towards the pile.
Zh: 继续把手伸向那堆东西。

En: Fuck this, I'm out. [Leave.]
Zh: 去他妈的吧，我不干了。[离开。]

En: Your hand touches a greasy duvet, covered in cigarette burns and ketchup stains. You hear a growl. There is something *alive* underneath it.
Zh: 你的手碰到一床油腻的羽绒被，上面沾满香烟的烙印和番茄酱的污渍。你听见一声咆哮。下面有什么*活着*的东西。

En: Pull the blanket off.
Zh: 把毛毯掀开。

En: In the dark it looks like a nest.
Zh: 在黑暗中，它看起来就像一个巢穴。

En: Mhmh... The lieutenant has covered his nose.
Zh: “唔……”警督掩住鼻子。

En: It's not too late, no one's going to blame you for backing out. You don't have to do this. Just get out.
Zh: 现在还不迟，如果你退出的话，没人会责怪你。你没必要这么做的。出去吧。

En: It's deep, in suffering somehow.
Zh: 它很深，正在承受某种痛苦。

En: You don't have to take him down. He's already down.
Zh: 你不用搞垮他，他已经垮掉了。

En: Nocturnal lagophthalmos. The likelihood of falling asleep with partially open eyelids rises after stimulant use and heavy drinking.
Zh: 夜兔眼。在服用思必得和大量饮酒后，半睁眼皮睡着的可能性就会增加。

En: There's still plenty to be scared of here. Just not what you thought.
Zh: 这里还有很多可怕的东西。只是和你想的不太一样。

En: This man won't be feeding his family any time soon. Not that he was, but... The lieutenant thinks to himself. "At least he won't be beating his son."
Zh: “这个男人近来是没法养家了。并不是说他之前有养过，但是……”警督想到。“至少他这样就没办法打儿子了。”

En: His half-open eyes give him the look of a dead man. But he is in there... and not enjoying himself.
Zh: 那双半睁开的眼睛让他看起来就像死人一样。但他还活着……并不是很享受。

En: He's gonna sleep it off, I know this shit.
Zh: “他会睡过去的，我知道这档子破事。”

En: A coronary artery bypass graft? Take out his liver and replace it with a new one? Somehow undo 20 years of neurological damage from stimulants...
Zh: 冠状动脉旁路移植术？取出他的肝脏，然后替换一个新的？莫名其妙地修复了服用20年思必得带来的神经损伤……

En: A change in the genetic make up wouldn't hurt. Ginger degenerate.
Zh: 遗传组合的改变不会带来伤害，但精力会退化。

En: A change in the socio-economic circumstances wouldn't hurt.
Zh: 社会经济环境的变化不会带来伤害。

En: Nod.
Zh: 点头。

En: They don't do charity for people who're trying to kill themselves. Besides, he'll be dead in a few... The lieutenant stops, listening to him.
Zh: “他们不会为试图自杀的人做慈善。再说了，他很快就会死……”警督停了下来，听着他的动静。

En: This is not something you should be attempting to do with your bare hands. You're just going to get hurt.
Zh: 这可不是空手能尝试的事情。你这样只会受伤的。

En: You can just go in now...
Zh: 现在你可以进去了……

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Like a goddamn maniac, you rip the chain off the door. The links lie on the floor, scattered.
Zh: 你就像个该死的疯子，直接把链条从门上扯了下来。链条凌乱地掉落在地上。

En: Like a goddamn maniac, you rip the chain off the door. The links lie on the floor, scattered.
Zh: 你就像个该死的疯子，直接把链条从门上扯了下来。链条凌乱地掉落在地上。

En: The door is locked by a chain. If only you had some kind of standard police device for the cutting of chains. Something, perhaps, that could be found in an RCM motor carriage...
Zh: 大门被一根链条锁住了。如果你有某种可以剪断链条的标准警用设备就好了。那种东西，也许可以在RCM的汽車里找到……

En: You'll need to equip the chaincutters to enter. Snip right through the metal!
Zh: 你必须装备切链钳才能进去。直接把金属剪断！

En: Good thing you already have the chaincutters in your hand! Nothing left to do now but *snip-snip*!
Zh: 还好你已经有切链钳了！现在只需要*咔擦-咔擦*一下就行了！

En: A shabby door hangs oddly on its hinges, secured to the doorframe with a safety chain. An unpaid energy bill is attached, threatening to cut off the electricity. It's addressed to Mr. Uuno de Ruyter.
Zh: 一扇破旧的大门古怪地挂在合页上，由一条安全链固定在门框里。上面贴着一张还未支付的电费账单，威胁说要切断电源。它是寄给乌诺·德鲁伊特先生的。

En: The apartment numbers have fallen off the door, leaving the panel with a sticky '1'-shaped shadow and a marker-drawn '2'.
Zh: 公寓的门牌号已经从门上掉了下来，牌子上只留下一个粘粘的“1”形阴影，还有马克笔画上的“2”。

En: Knock lightly.
Zh: 轻轻敲门。

En: Looks like we found where Cuno's dad lives. (Point to the bill on the door.)
Zh: “看来我们找到坤诺爸爸住的地方了。”（指着门上的账单。）

En: Your heartbeat quickens, palms go sweaty... The siren of amphetamine is singing you her song.
Zh: 你的心跳开始加速，掌心也汗津津的……安非他命女妖正在吟唱她的歌谣。

En: The lieutenant looks worried. "I know there's no stopping you -- but let's at least make this quick."
Zh: 警督看起来有些担心。“我知道是没办法阻止你的——所以至少麻烦你快点。”

En: Nothing happens?! It must be an ambush... Let's get your adrenaline pumping, just in case.
Zh: 什么也没发生？！肯定有埋伏……为了以防万一，让你的肾上腺素飙升起来吧。

En: And the place comes with three months' worth of utility bills.
Zh: 警督点点头。“而且这个地方有三个月没交物业费了。”

En: No response.
Zh: 没有回应。

En: Snip! The cutter goes through them like dead leaves. The links fall to the ground on the other side of the door.
Zh: 咔擦！切链钳像枯叶一样剪开它们。链条掉落在大门的另一侧。

En: Don't let the quiet fool you -- the beast is in there somewhere, ready to rip you to shreds with a broken bottle...
Zh: 别被这份宁静欺骗了——那头野兽就藏在这里的某个地方，随时准备用一只破碎的瓶子把你撕成碎片。

En: Use the cutters on the chain.
Zh: 用切链钳剪开链条。

En: Tool not in hand.
Zh: 手上没有工具。

En: Won't be beat by a goddamn door.
Zh: 你才不会被一扇该死的门给难倒呢。

En: Cursed Iilmaraan Die.
Zh: 诅咒伊尔玛骰子。

En: One More Door.
Zh: 还有一扇门。

En: It should be easy, but... it's just an awkward angle, okay? The door doesn't *really* open wide enough to really get the cutters through. You can't get the leverage you need. And the cutters are blunt. AND the chain is... just super strong, okay? It's not your fault you can't cut it!
Zh: 应该挺容易的，但是……这个角度实在有点尴尬，好吗？门敞开的宽度*真的*不太够，没办法把切链钳伸进去。这样就没办法得到必须的杠杆力。这个切链钳不太锋利。但是链条又……特别结实好吗？剪不开不是你的错！

En: The handle is loose from violent, fruitless yanking. Paint at the door's bottom is chipped from kicking. People have tried often, and apparently without success, to enter.
Zh: 把手在暴力而徒然的猛拉中有些松动。大门底部的油漆因为踢踹而剥落。大家经常尝试进去，但是显然没有成功。

En: The lieutenant shakes his head. "We've no business here. Let's get back to the investigation."
Zh: 警督摇摇头。“这里不关我们的事。还是继续调查吧。”

En: The lieutenant smiles. "Trouble with the chain, officer?"
Zh: 警督笑了。“被链条难住了吗，警官？”

En: These are no ordinary chains... they are *titanium* chains. *Supreme-titanium*, Kim.
Zh: “这些不是普通的链条……它们可是*钛合金*链条。*至高无上的钛合金*，金。”

En: Hey, don't look at me! You're the one packing shitty chaincutters.
Zh: “喂，别看我啊！你才是打包那个劣质切链钳的人。”

En: I'll be honest: I'm just not that good with my hands.
Zh: “老实说：我的手不是很灵活。”

En: The lieutenant arches his eyebrows, surprised. "Well... no. I guess not."
Zh: 警督惊讶地扬起眉毛。“呃……不。我猜没有。”

En: Of course, officer. The lieutenant smiles obligingly. "My RCM regulation, recently distributed cutters are the problem."
Zh: “当然了，警官。”警督露出亲切的笑容。“RCM的惯例，最近分发的切链钳才是问题。”

En: *Supreme-titanium*? The lieutenant blinks. "Sure. A door inside this complex has super-hard, super-expensive chains."
Zh: “*至高无上的钛合金*？”警督眨眨眼。“当然。这种公寓内的门会用一条超级牢固、超级昂贵的链条。”

En: Fabric rustles. The lieutenant's chin catches against his collar. He is looking on, shaking his head in disappointment.
Zh: 织物在沙沙作响。警督的下巴抵在了自己的衣领上。他在旁边看着，失望地摇摇头。

En: Stupid chain... Come on, try it again. We won't be defeated so easily!
Zh: 愚蠢的链条……来啊，再试一次。我们是不会这么轻易被打败的！

En: You can't stand for that! Come on, show him what you can REALLY do!
Zh: 这你怎么能忍得了！来吧，让他看看到底有多大能耐！

En: How... how can this be?! How can such a simple chain defy our best efforts? Are you *that* bad with your hands? I do not understand...
Zh: 这……这怎么可能？！你的最大努力怎么可以被这样一条简单的链条公然蔑视呢？你的双手*真的*有这么不灵活吗？我理解不了……

En: Snip! So much for that goddamn chain. The links fall to the ground on the other side of the door. You have bested the titanium menace.
Zh: 咔擦！受够这个该死的链条了。链条掉落在大门的另一侧。你克服了钛合金的威胁。

En: The communards didn't commit *enough* atrocities.
Zh: “公社社员们干掉的人还*不够*多。”

En: Yes, exactly.
Zh: “是的，没错。”

En: Completely right, Steban.
Zh: “一点不错，史蒂文。”

En: This new translation of 'Reál and Reality' is garbage.
Zh: “‘雷亚尔与现实’的这个新译本简直就是垃圾。”

En: University is a lot better once you stop going to class.
Zh: “上大学还是不去听课最好。”

En: I'll spit in Maurice's face when he comes crawling back.
Zh: “等莫里斯爬回来，我要往他脸上吐痰。”

En: Eight cups a day is perfectly healthy.
Zh: “一天喝八杯咖啡很健康。”

En: I would commit war crimes for some real Saramirizian coffee.
Zh: “现在再喝不到正宗的萨拉米尔泽咖啡，我就要犯罪了。”

En: Another coffee, Steban?
Zh: “再来杯咖啡吗，史蒂文？”

En: Don't worry, I'm sure your jacket will turn up.
Zh: “别担心，你肯定会找到那件夹克的。”

En: I can't believe someone just left these matchboxes here.
Zh: “我简直不敢相信有人居然就把这些火柴盒落在这里了。”

En: Sounded like a woman. A woman's shoes.
Zh: 听起来是个女人，有女性鞋子的声音。

En: Later then. Entering this door seems a physical *challenge*.
Zh: 之后再说吧。进入这扇门像是一次身体上的*挑战*。

En: It's generally easier to do things if you have literally *any* reason.
Zh: 一般来说，如果你有*任何*理由的话——字面上的意义——那做起事来就容易的多。

En: Scare them. Suspected of some *big crime*!
Zh: 吓吓他们。就说怀疑有*重大罪行*！

En: There's no sweet-talking your way in there. Be official.
Zh: 花言巧语可是进不去的。还是官方一点吧。

En: You have *plenty* of reason to enter.
Zh: 你有*很多*需要进去的理由。

En: Are you cooking *morphine* in there?!
Zh: “你在里面制造*吗啡*吗？！”

En: You hear someone walking around inside, rearranging the furniture. The number on the panel says #10.
Zh: 你听到里面有人走动的声音，像是在重新摆放家具。门牌上的数字写着10。

En: Knock.
Zh: 敲门。

En: Knock again.
Zh: 再敲一次。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: This time the steps come closer. "Who is this?" demands a female voice, wary and tense.
Zh: 这次脚步靠近一点。“是谁？”一个女性的声音响起，谨慎而紧张。

En: The walking stops abruptly, but no one comes to the door.
Zh: 脚步声突然中止了，但是没有人来开门。

En: You can feel tension on the other side.
Zh: 你能感觉到另一侧的紧张感。

En: This is the police. Open up.
Zh: “我是警察。请开门。”

En: Do I *have to* open the door? You hear the clacking of heels again, as the other side walks right up to the door. Her tone is now getting a defensive edge...
Zh: “我*必须*开门吗？”你再次听见高跟鞋的咔哒声，好像另一边有人正朝门口走来。现在她的语气里多了几分防备的意味……

En: Let's go, the lieutenant says, "we don't have a reason to get inside that apartment."
Zh: “我们走吧，”警督说到，“我们没有进入那个房间的理由。”

En: Do you have a warrant? I'm not obligated to open the door if you don't have a warrant.
Zh: “你有搜查令吗？如果没有的话，我没有义务这么做。”

En: This apartment is supposed to be empty. Did you break in here?
Zh: “这个房间应该是空的。你是闯进去的吗？”

En: Excuse me? Of course not!
Zh: “什么？当然不是！”

En: I don't need a warrant if I suspect there's been a *break-in*.
Zh: “如果我怀疑有*非法闯入*事件的话，就不需要搜查令了。”

En: Oh, come on! There's a pause, before you hear the door being unlocked.
Zh: “拜托！”短暂的停顿过后，你开始听到大门解锁的声音。

En: That was smart, the lieutenant says, nodding toward the unlocked door.
Zh: “这招挺聪明的，”警督一边说着，一边朝打开的大门点了点头。

En: Well, that was easy.
Zh: 哎呀，还挺简单的嘛。

En: Then open the door, so I can *verify* your identity.
Zh: “那就开门，我要*核查*你的身份。”

En: The door number #10 is closed again.
Zh: 10号门又关上了。

En: Knock.
Zh: 敲门。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Nothing happens. The real estate agent must have left.
Zh: 什么也没发生。房地产中介一定是离开了。

En: Why does this tenant have a bust of Kras Mazov in their bedroom?
Zh: “为什么这个租客会在卧室里放一尊卡拉斯·马佐夫的半身像？”

En: The white star... the photos on the wall... I think we have broken into the apartment of a young communard. He looks around, before mumbling to himself: "How fitting."
Zh: “那颗白星……还有墙上的照片……我想我们可能闯入了一位年轻公社社员的房间。”他环顾四周，然后喃喃自语到：“真是太恰当了。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Father Mazov, the hero of the working class! (Salute the statue.)
Zh: “伟大的马佐夫，工人阶级的英雄！”（向雕像行礼。）

En: Whoever lives here needs to learn how the economy *actually* works. (Stare at the bust.)
Zh: “不管是谁住在这里，都需要学习经济*实际*上是怎么运作的。”（盯着半身像。）

En: Kras Mazov was a mass murderer. There's nothing *cool* about keeping his bust on your night stand. (Stare at the bust.)
Zh: “卡拉斯·马佐夫是害死很多人的凶手。把他的半身像放在床头柜上可一点都不*酷*。”（盯着半身像。）

En: Kras Mazov was a filthy homo-sexual. (Stare at the bust.)
Zh: “卡拉斯·马佐夫是个猥琐的同性恋。”（盯着半身像。）

En: A warm feeling fills you as you look into Mazov's marble eyes. The hallway behind the door is empty; no one else heard your courageous salute.
Zh: 你看向马佐夫的大理石眼睛，浑身上下突然被一种温暖的感觉包围了。门后那条走廊空荡荡的；没有其他人听见你勇敢的致敬声。

En: The bust doesn't reply. The hallway behind the door is empty; no one heard your words.
Zh: 半身像没有回应。门后那条走廊空荡荡的；没有人听到你的话。

En: A communard lives in this room. (Look around.)
Zh: 这里住了个公社社员。（环顾四周）

En: A *poor* communard, from the looks of it. The room is barely bigger than a closet.
Zh: 从外观来看，是一个*贫穷*的公社社员。这个房间只比橱柜略大一点。

En: Yeah -- I have a problem. A problem with alcohol.
Zh: “是啊——我有个问题。与酒精有关的问题。”

En: You do -- but that's not the point. The point is, he doesn't look like you.
Zh: “没错——但这不是关键。问题是，他看起来不像你。”

En: Because he was totally a gangster and a bank robber who went for *all* the cash in the world. That's *me*.
Zh: “因为他完全就是个流氓，还是个银行抢劫犯，是以全世界*所有*现金为奋斗目标的。这就是*我*。”

En: I think you have misunderstood who he was, but... the lieutenant sighs. "If you say so."
Zh: “我觉得你误解他的身份了，但是……”警督叹了口气。“随你怎么说吧。”

En: There aren't many communists around -- not after the Revolution. Some youths still keep the ideology going, it seems.
Zh: “现在公社社员可不多了——特别是大革命之后。看来有些年轻人还传承着这种意识形态。”

En: That's right. (Nod.) "Filthy."
Zh: “没错。”（点头。）“猥琐。”

En: A plaster cast bust depicts a middle-aged man with impressive sideburns. The name on the plinth reads 'Kras Mazov'.
Zh: 一座石膏半身像描绘出一个中年男子的形象，他的连鬓胡子让人印象深刻。底座上的铭牌上写着“卡拉斯·马佐夫”。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Kim, who is this Kras Mazov?
Zh: “金，卡拉斯·马佐夫是谁？”

En: Father Mazov, the hero of the working class! (Salute the statue.)
Zh: “伟大的马佐夫，工人阶级的英雄！”（向雕像行礼。）

En: Whoever lives here needs to learn how the economy *actually* works.
Zh: “不管是谁住在这里，都需要学习经济*实际*上是怎么运作的。”

En: Kim, you have to admit, this Kras Mazov bears a striking resemblance to me.
Zh: “金，你必须承认，这个卡拉斯·马佐夫跟我有着惊人的相似。”

En: Kras Mazov (nom de guerre), was an economist and theoretical historian. He was a leading figure on the Graad side of the Turn-of-the-Century Revolution, where he headed the Eleven Day Government. Mazov is considered the father of scientific communism (Mazovian thought or Mazovianism).
Zh: 卡拉斯·马佐夫（化名），是一名经济学家以及理论历史学家。在那场世纪之交的大革命中，他是格拉德革命阵营的领军人物，并在之后建立了十一日政府。马佐夫被认为是科学康米主义（又称马佐夫思想或马佐夫主义）之父。

En: Kras Mazov was a mass murderer. There's nothing *cool* about keeping his bust on your night stand.
Zh: “卡拉斯·马佐夫是害死很多人的凶手。把他的半身像放在床头柜上可一点都不*酷*。”

En: Kras Mazov was a sexual deviant. A zoophile probably.
Zh: “卡拉斯·马佐夫是个性变态。还可能是个嗜兽癖。”

En: Who is Kras Mazov?
Zh: 谁是卡拉斯·马佐夫？

En: Seems like a good guy judging by the moustache. By the way... doesn't he kind of look like *you*?
Zh: 从胡子来判断，好像是个好人。顺便说一句……不觉得他有点像*你*吗？

En: He's known as the father of scientific communism, also known as Mazovianism. His theories about economic history greatly influenced -- some would even say *sparked* -- the Antecentennial Revolution.
Zh: “他被认为是科学康米主义，或者是马佐夫主义之父。他关于经济历史的理论极大地影响了——甚至有人说是*点燃*了——世纪前大革命。”

En: Situated between a poster of a white star and a photo of revolutionaries posing with their guns... This can't be a coincidence. There can be only one explanation.
Zh: 位于一副白星海报和一张革命者举枪的照片中间……这绝对不是个巧合。只有一种解释。

En: Kras Mazov is a *communard*.
Zh: 卡拉斯·马佐夫是一名*公社社员*。

En: Yes, Kras Mazov (nom de guerre), was an economist and theoretical historian. He was a leading figure on the Graad side of the Turn-of-the-Century Revolution, where he headed the Eleven Day Government. Mazov is considered the father of scientific communism (Mazovian thought or Mazovianism).
Zh: 是的，卡拉斯·马佐夫（化名），是一名经济学家以及理论历史学家。在那场世纪之交的大革命中，他是格拉德革命阵营的领军人物，并在之后建立了十一日政府。马佐夫被认为是科学康米主义（又称马佐夫思想或马佐夫主义）之父。

En: The lieutenant raises a brow.
Zh: 警督扬起眉毛。

En: I hate deviants.
Zh: “我讨厌变态。”

En: Whoever lives here definitely shares your enthusiasm. He leans closer to inspect the photos of revolutionaries on the wall.
Zh: “不管是谁住在这里，肯定跟你有相同的爱好。”他靠近墙边，检查着革命者的照片。

En: I suspect that's exactly what they're trying to do. He leans closer to inspect the economics book on the table.
Zh: “我怀疑这就是他们打算做的事。”他靠近桌子，检查着上面的经济读物。

En: He was definitely controversial. The tenant here seems to like him... He leans closer to inspect the photos of revolutionaries on the wall.
Zh: “他确实是个有争议的人。这里的租客好像挺喜欢他的……”他靠近墙边，检查着革命者的照片。

En: Thank you for sharing this wonderful opinion on human sexuality. He pretends to read the economics book open on the table.
Zh: “谢谢你分享自己在人类性行为学方面的看法。”他假装在看桌子上摊开的那本经济读物。

En: Honestly, he does kind of look like you after all.
Zh: 老实说，他确实跟你有点类似。

En: Hold on a second... Is this why you broke in here? To find out whether you're Kras Mazov?
Zh: “等一下……这就是你闯进来的原因？为了搞清楚自己是不是卡拉斯·马佐夫？”

En: I have to consider and investigate all possibilities.
Zh: “所有可能性我都要考虑到，还要调查清楚。”

En: No, it's purely coincidental.
Zh: “不，这纯属巧合。”

En: I'm not saying that I *am* Kras Mazov, I'm just saying that we look alike.
Zh: “我没说自己*是*卡拉斯·马佐夫，我只是说我们很像。”

En: Sure it is.
Zh: “当然。”

En: Well, you both do seem to share an affinity for sideburns. But it seems like old Kras here didn't drink nearly as much as you.
Zh: “呃，你们的连鬓胡子确实挺像的。不过这位老卡拉斯看起来没你喝的多。”

En: Except that Kras Mazov is dead. He's been dead for 50 years now.
Zh: “只是卡拉斯·马佐夫已经死了。到现在已经死去50年了。”

En: Just humour me for a moment... Don't you see the resemblance?
Zh: “你就稍微迁就我一下……难道你没看见相似之处吗？”

En: You're right, this is silly -- I'm no Kras Mazov.
Zh: “你说得对，这样太蠢了——我不是卡拉斯·马佐夫。”

En: The lieutenant doesn't even bother to reply to this.
Zh: 警督甚至都懒得回应。

En: How do you know? The bust is probably just a romanticized depiction anyway.
Zh: “你怎么知道？这座半身像可能只是用了一种传奇化的表现手法。”

En: Maybe this bust shows him *before* he started drinking.
Zh: “也许这尊半身像展示的是他开始喝酒*之前*的样子。”

En: Sure you are.
Zh: “当然是了。”

En: Very well. The lieutenant leans closer to the sculpture. "Let's look for identifying features then..."
Zh: “好吧。”警督又靠近了雕像一些。“那我们就找找相似的特征吧……”

En: He puts a finger on a pale dot embellishing the bust's cheekbone. "Doesn't he have a birthmark right here? What about you?"
Zh: 他伸出一只手指，放在半身像颧骨的一个小白点上。“这里是他的胎记吗？你有吗？”

En: I can't tell -- I can't see my face.
Zh: “我也不知道——我看不到自己的脸。”

En: I definitely don't have a birthmark on my left cheek.
Zh: “我的左脸颊肯定没有胎记。”

En: Then it's settled, says the lieutenant, straightening his back. "You're not Kras Mazov."
Zh: “那就有结果了，”警督挺直了背，回答到。“你不是卡拉斯·马佐夫。”

En: Alright. But here's the big thing -- Kras Mazov looks Samaran, and you don't.
Zh: “好吧。但是有件重要的事——卡拉斯·马佐夫看起来像是萨马拉人，但你不像。”

En: No, it's not settled -- I might still be Kras Mazov. It's just a birthmark.
Zh: “不，还不能确定——我仍然有可能是卡拉斯·马佐夫。这只是个胎记。”

En: You're right, I'm really not.
Zh: “你说得对，我真不是。”

En: No, he doesn't.
Zh: “不，他没有。”

En: I'm part Samaran myself.
Zh: “我自己也有部分萨马拉人的血统。”

En: The lieutenant closes his eyes. "Okay, you win. Be Kras Mazov then, I don't care..."
Zh: 警督闭上双眼。“好吧，你赢了。那就当你是卡拉斯吧，我不在乎……”

En: He opens his eyes again, tilting his head in a quiet wonder. "Why are you so hell-bent on proving that you're Kras Mazov anyway?"
Zh: 他再次睁开眼，歪头安静地沉思到。“话说回来，你为什么拼命要证明自己是卡拉斯·马佐夫？”

En: Yes, he does. Look at his eyes. Wasn't his mother a Samaran immigrant?
Zh: “是，他有。看看他的眼睛。他母亲不是萨马拉移民吗？”

En: You're right, that's something we definitely don't have in common.
Zh: “你说得对，我们确实有些不同的地方。”

En: Good, so you're not him.
Zh: “很好，那你就不是他。”

En: It's decided -- you're *not* the most famous philosopher of the modern period. The lieutenant nods, turning away to inspect the photo on the other wall.
Zh: “决定了——你*不是*当代最著名的哲学家。”警督点点头，转身检查另一面墙上的照片去了。

En: I might not look like him, but I know it in my heart -- Kras Mazov, that's me.
Zh: “我可能长得不像他，但是在心里我知道——

En: Because I'm a communard.
Zh: “因为我是一名公社社员。”

En: Because I believe a powerful nation-state is the only way to protect the working class from subhumans.
Zh: “因为我相信一个强大的单民族国家是唯一能保护工人阶级不受类人生物伤害的办法。”

En: Because *actually* he wanted stability and incremental progress -- and so do I.
Zh: “因为*事实上*，他想要稳定和循序渐进——我也一样。”

En: I'm not sure, it's just a feeling I have. But I trust my feelings.
Zh: “我不确定，只是一种感觉。但我相信自己的感觉。”

En: Alright, *Kras*, the lieutenant notes. "If you say so."
Zh: “好吧，*卡拉斯*，”警督记录着。“随你怎么说吧。”

En: Alright, *Kras*. You fought in the Revolution and everything, the lieutenant notes. "If you say so."
Zh: “好吧，*卡拉斯*，你参与过革命战斗，一切都是你做的，”警督记录着。“随你怎么说吧。”

En: What are you doing? You need to get your tools out, man.
Zh: 你在干什么？你需要把工具拿出来，伙计。

En: He's just trying to help, don't take it... bad. Relax.
Zh: 他只是想试着帮你的忙，别把它……往坏处想。放松点。

En: We could use some chaincutters here to cut the shackle.
Zh: “我们可以用切链钳把链条剪开。”

En: Why would we want to break into some random citizen's apartment?
Zh: “我们为什么要这样随机闯入普通市民的公寓？”

En: Why *wouldn't* we want to?
Zh: “为什么*不可以*呢？”

En: Snip-snip, my friend! (Do hand-scissors.)
Zh: “咔擦掉它吧，我的朋友！”（用手比划出剪刀的样子。）

En: I don't know, just seems like something we could do.
Zh: “我不知道，只是感觉像是我们可以做的事。”

En: The lieutenant looks at you for a long moment, then blinks once...
Zh: 警督盯着你看了好一会儿，然后眨了眨眼……

En: There's so much he could say right now, but he knows it wouldn't make much of a difference.
Zh: 他现在有很多话可以说，但他知道这样也改变不了什么。

En: The lieutenant doesn't say a word, his face wiped clear of any emotion.
Zh: 警督什么也没说，脸上没有任何表情。

En: He takes a closer look at the lock. "I supposed if one were *committed* to it, there's a pair of chaincutters in the Kineema."
Zh: 他仔细看了眼门锁。“如果某人真的*决心*要这么做的话，我的锐影里有一副切链钳。”

En: This door has been closed with a padlock. A chalk-drawn number on the board says #11.
Zh: 这扇门被一只挂锁锁上了。门板上用粉笔写着11号。

En: Knock.
Zh: 敲门。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: No reply.
Zh: 没有回应。

En: Use the chaincutters to cut through the padlock.
Zh: 用切链钳剪开挂锁。

En: Tool not in hand.
Zh: 手里没有工具。

En: Kim pointed out the shackle.
Zh: 金指了指上面的钩环。

En: Standard Anti-Wirrâl Die.
Zh: 标准的反威勒尔骰子。

En: One More Door.
Zh: 又一扇门。

En: The shackle snaps like a twig and the lock falls to the floor with a little thud. It should be possible to enter now...
Zh: 钩环像小树枝一样被剪了下来，锁砰的一声掉落在地板上。现在能进去了……

En: What are you doing? You're trying to cut the body of the lock with the chaincutters and it's really not working.
Zh: 你在干什么？你打算用切链钳剪开锁头吗，这样真的行不通。

En: Examine the padlock.
Zh: 检查挂锁。

En: It's a solid lump of metal, but the shackle is deeply corroded -- a solid pair of chaincutters would make short work of it.
Zh: 这是一块坚硬的金属，但是链条腐蚀地已经很严重——一副结实的切链钳最适合这类工作。

En: Time for a little snip-snip! (Do hand-scissors.)
Zh: “是时候来点咔擦-咔擦了！”（用手比划出剪刀的样子。）

En: You seem committed to it, so go on.
Zh: “你好像下定决心了，那就继续吧。”

En: Refrain from commenting.
Zh: 忍住，不要发表评论。

En: The lieutenant is concentrated on the hallway, scanning both ways for any approachers.
Zh: 警督的注意力都放在了走廊上，他正观察着两边有没有人接近。

En: After you, detective.
Zh: “你先吧，警探。”

En: I believe it's the *shackle* you mean to cut, detective. The lieutenant points to the corroded loop with a gloved finger.
Zh: “我觉得你应该剪开的是*钩环*，警探。”警督用戴着手套的手指指向被腐蚀的圆环。

En: Don't you think that's what I'm *trying* to do?
Zh: “你不觉得这就是我要*尝试*的事情吗？”

En: These chaincutters are broken, Kim.
Zh: “切链钳坏了，金。”

En: Perhaps you should give it another go?
Zh: “也许你应该再试一次？”

En: Better whip out those cutters... you won't get very far otherwise.
Zh: 最好把那些切链钳拿出来……否则你是走不了太远的。

En: A lock blocks your path! You might have use for one of those standard police devices for cutting chains. Something that may be found in an RCM motor carriage...
Zh: 一把锁挡住了你的去路！你可以用标准的警用设备剪开这些链条。某些可以在RCM汽車里找到的东西……

En: Yes! Get those cutters in there and *snip* away. This weak chain is no match for your might.
Zh: 没错！把切链钳来出来，直接*剪开*吧。这根脆弱的链条根本比不上你强大的力量。

En: Boy there are a lot of different keys there. More than *twenty* at least...
Zh: 老天，还真是有好多不同的钥匙。至少超过*20*把了……

En: Her voice is really cheerful despite her obviously *hating* you.
Zh: 她显然很*讨厌*你，但她的声音听起来还是很愉悦。

En: Be friendly.
Zh: 友好一点。

En: I fuck shit up too.
Zh: “我也搞砸了。”

En: Heh! She smiles aggressively. "Sounds like you've spent too much time undercover in some rock band."
Zh: “哈！”她的笑容很有攻击性。“听起来你在某个摇滚乐队卧底太久了。”

En: Who lived here?
Zh: “谁住在这里？”

En: It's not a compliment.
Zh: 这可不是一句恭维的话。

En: I glimpsed a *foreclosed* apartment down the hallway.
Zh: “我在下面的走廊瞥见一间*被没收*的公寓。”

En: So preppy! She's probably on some low grade performance enhancers. Like Preptide or Pericanine.
Zh: 真是太幼稚了！她可能在嗑某种低级的违禁药物，比如帕立太特，或者是地布卡因。

En: The sum must've been puny.
Zh: 总数肯定微不足道。

En: Oh, it irks her. The *incompetence*.
Zh: 哦，这让她感到烦恼。这种*无能为力*的感觉。

En: It's as if they're *real* wizards, able to resurrect dead real estate and breathe life into bank accounts.
Zh: 感觉他们就像是*真正的*巫师，能够复兴衰败的房地产业，为银行账户注入生命。

En: The *radiocomputer wizards* are invested with magical economic powers.
Zh: 这些*无线电脑巫师*被赋予了神奇的经济力量。

En: Isn't it a bit late to be working?
Zh: “这么晚了还在工作吗？”

En: It *is*. She breathes in and out to the count of three -- it looks like some special stress release technique -- before she continues...
Zh: “*是的*。”她吸了一口气，吐出来，数到三——看起来像是某种特别的减压技巧——在她继续之前……

En: Everyone says that real estate agents don't *do* anything, but here I am, in the middle of the night, cleaning up someone's 'crash pad'.
Zh: “大家都说房产中介什么也*没做*，但是我就在这里，大半夜的，清理某人的‘临时住所’。”

En: So, the sooner we get this over with, the better. She means you -- time for the next question.
Zh: “所以，我们越快结束这件事就越好。”她指的是你——是时候进入下一个问题了。

En: Come to think of it, she *does* look incredibly tired.
Zh: 细想一下，她*的确*看起来非常疲惫。

En: Satisfied? My name is Marielle Charpentier and I'm an agent with Martinaise Realty Associates. I am *not* breaking in, as I have every *right* to be here. The keys, see? She jingles a set of keys in her hand.
Zh: “满意了吗？我的名字叫玛瑞勒·卡朋特，我是马丁内斯地产联合公司的一名中介。我*没有*非法闯入，而且我完全*有权利*待在这里。看见了吗，这是钥匙？”她手中的一串钥匙叮当作响。

En: That's all, thank you. [Leave.]
Zh: “就这些，谢谢你。”[离开。]

En: Inspect the passport.
Zh: 检查护照。

En: I don't need your ID, I just need to ask you some questions.
Zh: “我不需要你的身份证明，我只想问你几个问题。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Very well, but please make it quick. She slips the passport back to her purse and checks her watch. "I need to be back in La Delta in an hour."
Zh: “很好，那就麻烦你快一点。”她把护照塞回自己的钱包，看了一眼手表。“我必须在一小时以内赶回德尔塔。”

En: Who lived in the foreclosed apartment down the hallway?
Zh: “走廊下面那间被没收的公寓里住的是什么人？”

En: What are you doing here?
Zh: “你在这里做什么？”

En: She sighs and looks around. "I need to get it ready for the next lease, but as you can see, the previous tenant completely trashed the place."
Zh: 她叹了口气，四下望了望。“我需要把这里准备好，这样才能再租出去，但是你也看到了，之前的租客把这里完全毁掉了。”

En: Reprehensible. Who lived here?
Zh: “确实应该受到谴责。谁住在这里？”

En: It was some kind of a moribund old man, who used to be a business owner. You'd think they'd make rent... She stops, hesitating.
Zh: “好像是个垂死的老人，以前是一个企业家。你觉得他们会付房租……”她停了下来，有些犹豫。

En: Do you want to see my ID as well? You can't *legally* ask for it, but why not? Want to see my residence permit, too? She fumbles through her purse, fishing out a light paper-clad passport.
Zh: “你还想看我的身份证明吗？你的要求不是*合法*的，但是为什么不呢？还想看我的居留证吗？”她在钱包里翻找着，掏出一本浅色的纸质护照。

En: It feels flimsy in hand, with the words 'Revachol Zone of Control' written under a nondescript municipal logo. There's a picture of her with shorter hair inside, along with all her personal details.
Zh: 你手里的它十分脆弱，毫不起眼的城市标志下印着“瑞瓦肖控制区”几个字。里面是她的一张短发照片，还有全部个人信息。

En: But -- that was months ago. Anyway. Was there anything you wanted, or is that it? I'm in a hurry.
Zh: “但是——那已经是几个月前的事了。总而言之，你还有什么需要吗，或者这样就行了？我赶时间。”

En: Oh, that's another huge mess. The former tenant owes us three months of rent, *three*! We closed the apartment and planned on auctioning off the valuables, but...
Zh: “哎，那又是一个巨大的麻烦。前任租客欠了我们3个月的房租，*三个月*啊！我们关上公寓，打算拍卖一些值钱的东西，但是……”

En: Of course, she replies with a smile, but her eyes remain glazed over -- she's been waiting for you to leave.
Zh: “当然，”她笑着回复到，不过目光依然呆滞——她在等你离开。

En: Don't ask me what happened with the wall, I have no idea how we're going to find the time or resources to fix it.
Zh: “别问我这面墙怎么了，我不知道该去哪找时间，或者是资源来修好它。”

En: *Both* apartments are now unrentable. Both! She's still shaking her head, manicured hands now crossed over the chest.
Zh: “现在*两间*公寓都没有出租。两间都是！”她还在摇头，把修剪整齐的手交叉抱在胸口。

En: And again, I have no idea how *stupid* mistakes like this can even happen, but Ron, when he came to close the door, didn't close the neighbouring door! And there's a hole in the wall!
Zh: “又来了，我不明白这种*愚蠢*的错误怎么会发生，但是罗恩，他来关门的时候，居然没把邻近的门关上！结果现在墙上多了个洞！”

En: A hole in the wall, can you believe it? She spreads her hands. "And then the tenant ran off with his stuff. He's gone, the *money's* gone, just like that!" She snaps her fingers.
Zh: “墙上多了个洞，你能相信吗？”她摊开双手。“然后租客带着东西跑了。他跑了，*钱*也没了，就是这么回事！”她打了个响指。

En: So wait, what happened with the wall? (Proceed.)
Zh: “等等，这面墙怎么了？”（继续。）

En: It couldn't have been that much money. These apartments look pretty shabby.
Zh: “应该没那么多钱吧。这些公寓本身看起来就挺破旧的。”

En: These apartments are perfectly fine! They have gorgeous architecture, a million reál view of the bay, good ventilation, neighbours, life, spark... And they are affordable.
Zh: “这些公寓好的不得了！建筑风格华丽，价值百万雷亚尔的海湾景观，通风良好，邻里和睦，生活气息浓，活力四射……而且价格实惠。”

En: My money has also disappeared, I think.
Zh: “我想，我的钱也消失了。”

En: Well, it does *not* disappear from my hands, no, I don't let it.
Zh: “呃，它*不会*从我手上消失的，不会，我绝对不会允许。”

En: I'll tell you, Martinaise has a future -- in a few years it's going to blossom with artists and creatives and those radiocomputer wizards.
Zh: “我告诉你，马丁内斯是有未来的——就在几年内，它会和那些艺术家，创意人员，还有无线电脑巫师一同绽放。”

En: A sudden serious look crosses her face. This story didn't have a happy ending.
Zh: 突然间，她的脸上露出一副严肃的神情。这个故事并没有一个快乐的结局。

En: Everything seems to be in order. (Hand back the passport.)
Zh: “一切好像都很正常。”（把护照还回去。）

En: Thank you. She slips the passport back in her purse. "Do you have any questions? I need to be back in midtown in an hour."
Zh: “谢谢。”她把护照塞进钱包里。“你还有别的问题吗？我一小时以内要赶回市中心。”

En: What now? She crosses her arms impatiently.
Zh: “现在又想怎么样？”她不耐烦地抱起双臂。

En: Nice haircut. (Hand back the passport.)
Zh: “发型不错。”（把护照还回去。）

En: Sounds like they knew how to have a good time.
Zh: “听起来他们知道怎么才能玩的尽兴。”

En: Number twenty eight... This is where the cleaning lady said the smoker on the balcony lives.
Zh: 28号……清洁女工说阳台上的吸烟者住的地方。

En: Let's see if anyone's home. Knock on the door.
Zh: “我们看看有没有人在家。敲门吧。”

En: Knock.
Zh: 敲门。

En: No one answers.
Zh: 没人回应。

En: Yes? A handsome young man hums with a cigarette in his hand. "Are you looking for me, gendarme?"
Zh: “什么事？”一个英俊的年轻男子哼了一声，手里还拿着一只香烟。“你在找我吗，警官？”

En: Looks like the young man we're looking for isn't home. I think our best chance to catch him is in the evening. He looks around, taking in the cold spring air.
Zh: “看来我们要找的年轻人不在家，我想最好抓到他的机会是在晚上。”他看了看周围，吸了一口春日料峭的空气。

En: Sounds good. Tomorrow 21.00.
Zh: “听起来不错。那就明天晚上9点吧。”

En: I don't think I can make it.
Zh: “我可能赶不到。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你什么意思？”

En: Tomorrow 21.00, right here, apartment #28. He writes it down into his little notebook. "Good. Let's go."
Zh: “明天晚上9点，就在这里，28号公寓。”他记在自己的小笔记本上。“好了。我们走吧。”

En: The smoker on the balcony. This is why we're here, right? He might know something about the murder. So, tomorrow 21.00?
Zh: “阳台上的那个吸烟者。我们就是为这个来的，不是吗？他可能知道些凶杀案的事。所以要不明天晚上9点吧？”

En: It's alright, we don't have to be here 21.00 sharp, I just need to write something down in my notebook. He has already opened the little blue book.
Zh: “没事的，没必要9点整赶到这里，我只要在笔记本里记下来就好。”他已经打开了自己的蓝色小笔记本。

En: Looks like the young man we're looking for isn't home. I think our best chance to catch him is in the evening. He looks around, taking in the cold spring air.
Zh: “看来我们要找的年轻人不在家。我想最好抓到他的机会是在晚上。”他看了看周围，吸了一口春日料峭的空气。

En: We should return tomorrow after we have finished with our day's work -- how about 21.00, sound good?
Zh: “我们应该明天再来，等完成白天的工作之后——晚上9点，怎么样？”

En: Tomorrow evening, right here, apartment #28. Good. Let's go.
Zh: “明天晚上，就在这里，28号公寓。好了，我们走吧。”

En: We should return tonight after we have finished with our day's work -- how about 21.00, sound good?
Zh: “我们应该今晚再来，等完成白天的工作之后——晚上9点，怎么样？”

En: We should talk to him. The lieutenant nods at the man standing right next to the door.
Zh: “我们应该跟他聊聊。”警督朝着站在门边的男人点点头。

En: We should return tomorrow -- tomorrow at 21.00, the lieutenant reminds you.
Zh: “我们应该明天再来——明天晚上9点，”警督提醒着你。

En: We should return in the evening -- at 21.00 the earliest.
Zh: “我们应该晚上再来——最早9点。”

En: What are you talking about?
Zh: “你在说什么呢？”

En: Sounds good. Tonight at 21.00.
Zh: “听起来不错。今晚9点。”

En: I don't think I can make it.
Zh: “我可能赶不到。”

En: The smoker on the balcony. This is why we're here, right? He might know something about the murder. So, tonight at 21.00?
Zh: “阳台上的那个吸烟者。我们就是为这个来的，不是吗？他可能知道些凶杀案的事。所以，要不今晚9点吧？”

En: Tonight, right here, apartment #28. Good. Let's go.
Zh: “今晚，就在这里，28号公寓。好了，我们走吧。”

En: It's alright, we don't have to be here 21.00 sharp, I just need to write something down in my notebook. He has already opened the little blue book.
Zh: “没事的，没必要9点整赶到这里，我只要在笔记本里记下来就好。”他已经打开了自己的蓝色小笔记本。

En: Tonight at 21.00, right here, apartment #28. He writes it down into his little notebook. "Good. Let's go."
Zh: “今晚9点，就在这里，28号公寓。”他记在自己的小笔记本上。“好了。我们走吧。”

En: Suddenly he's a little worried about your memory...
Zh: 突然，他有点担心你的记忆力……

En: Don't worry, it's (mostly) all still here. You can remember things. You *got* this.
Zh: 别担心，（差不多）已经记住了。你能想起来。你可以*做到*的。

En: Damn. Turns out it's quite tricky finding someone in a big apartment building... Don't worry. You'll get him!
Zh: 见鬼。没想到在这间大公寓里找个人居然这么棘手……别担心。你会找到他的！

En: Remember: tomorrow. He's *probably* gone for today.
Zh: 记住：明天。他今天*可能*已经走了。

En: Looks like he isn't home. You should come back later, after 21.00.
Zh: 看来他不在家。你应该晚点再来，晚上9点之后。

En: Looks like he isn't home. You should come back tomorrow evening, after 21.00.
Zh: 看来他不在家。你应该明天晚上再来，晚上9点之后。

En: Looks like he isn't home. You should come back tomorrow evening, after 21.00.
Zh: 看来他不在家。你应该明天晚上再来，晚上9点之后。

En: Looks like he isn't home. You should come back later, after 21.00.
Zh: 看来他不在家。你应该晚点再来，晚上9点之后。

En: The man you're looking for is standing right next to you. You should talk to him.
Zh: 你要找的男人就站在你身边。你应该跟他聊聊。

En: Let's go. There's nothing to do here for us anymore.
Zh: “我们走吧。这里没什么能做的了。”

En: Let's go. There's nothing to do here for us anymore.
Zh: “我们走吧。这里没什么能做的了。”

En: Nothing do to here anymore...
Zh: 这里没什么能做的了……

En: This door is made of metal and appears to be reinforced. Someone here really values their security.
Zh: 这扇门是金属做的，好像还加固过。有人真的很重视自己的安全。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Very well, I'll talk to him, but first I want to talk to *you*. I have so many questions. (Proceed.)
Zh: “很好，我会跟他谈谈的，但是首先，我想先跟*你*聊聊。我有很多问题要问。”（继续。）

En: Wait, who exactly is visiting you?
Zh: “等等，你的客人到底是谁？”

En: My Sunday friend, he repeats, his smile even wider than before. "He's brilliant, everyone wants to meet my Sunday friend. You'll see."
Zh: “我的周日朋友，”他重复着，笑容比之前更加灿烂。“他很聪明，所有人都想见见我的周日朋友。你会见到的。”

En: That's nice, but I don't have anything to tell you. It's my friend you're looking for, not me.
Zh: “那很好啊，但是我没什么可以告诉你的。你要找的是我朋友，不是我。”

En: Besides, I've got to run.
Zh: “再说我得走了。”

En: He takes another drag of his unfiltered cigarette and looks around. It's getting dark and the neighbouring windows have lit up one by one.
Zh: 他又抽了一口无滤嘴香烟，四下看了看。天色渐晚，临近的窗户一个接一个地点亮了。

En: Run where?
Zh: “去哪？”

En: Only if you promise that we'll talk again. It's *important*.
Zh: “如果你答应我们以后还有机会再聊的话，这很*重要*。”

En: To the city. He gestures idly towards distant motorways. "It's a beautiful night."
Zh: “去城里。”他漫不经心地朝着远处的高速公路指了指。“今晚的夜色很美。”

En: We'll talk, the smoker assures you, brushing his hand through the hair. "Just not tonight."
Zh: “会有机会再聊的，”吸烟者理了理自己的头发，向你保证着，“只是今晚不行。”

En: Take care, alright? he says with another disarming smile, before slipping off into the night.
Zh: “保重，好吗？”他又露出一个让人放松的笑脸，然后消失在茫茫夜色中。

En: Gendarmerie! You found me. The young man on the balcony gives you a bright smile, before taking another drag from his cigarette.
Zh: “宪兵！你找到我了。”阳台上的年轻人露出一个大大的笑脸，接着抽了一口手中的烟。

En: Yes, the cleaning lady let us in.\n
Zh: “是的，清洁女工放我们进来的。”

En: Yes, the cleaning lady let me in. She also led me to your apartment.
Zh: “没错，清洁女工放我进来的。她还带我找到了你的公寓。”

En: Beautiful, he replies, smiling. As he looks at you, something sparkles in his eyes.
Zh: “漂亮，”他笑着回答到。当他看着你的时候，眼睛里有什么东西在闪烁。

En: That's what I'm aiming for, yes.
Zh: “没错，这就是我的目的。”

En: I'm not going to make things just *right*, I'm going to make them *spectacular*.
Zh: “我不会只让事情*变好*，而是会让它们变得*好的不得了*。”

En: I have some good news for you. My Sunday friend is visiting me tonight. I told him about you and he'd like to say hello. Step in, he's already waiting. He nods towards door #28.
Zh: “我有个好消息要告诉你。我的周日朋友今晚会来看我。我跟他提了你，他想跟你打个招呼。进来吧，他已经在等你了。”他朝28号门点了点头。

En: We found a key hidden under a stone. Was it yours?
Zh: “我们在石头底下找到一把钥匙。是你的吗？”

En: I found a key hidden under a stone. Was it yours? (Show him the key.)
Zh: “我在石头底下找到一把钥匙。是你的吗？”（把钥匙拿给他看。）

En: It was mine. My friends use it from time to time to visit me. As he looks at you, something sparkles in his eyes.
Zh: “是我的。我的朋友偶尔会来看我。”当他看着你的时候，眼睛里有什么东西在闪烁。

En: Somewhere in the distance a baby cries over a blasting radio. There's a family argument reaching its crescendo in an upstairs apartment, while around the corner the air conditioners drone.
Zh: 远处的某个地方，婴儿被爆裂的收音机声吓得哭了起来。楼上的某间公寓里，一场家庭纠纷逐渐达到高潮，而附近角落里的空调还在嗡嗡作响。

En: It feels like a Friday. He seems to be in a good mood tonight.
Zh: 感觉像是星期五。他今晚的心情似乎不错。

En: There's something so *different* about him that I just can't put my finger on...
Zh: “他身上真的有些*与众不同*的东西，我实在是搞不清楚……”

En: Different, of course.
Zh: “与众不同，的确如此。”

En: And his shirt is still unbuttoned.
Zh: 而且他的衬衫还是没有扣上。

En: Why would I want to meet your friend?
Zh: “我为什么想见你的朋友呢？”

En: Trust me, you do.
Zh: “相信我，你想的。”

En: His shirt... why is his shirt always unbuttoned?
Zh: “他的衬衫……为什么他的衬衫总是不扣扣子呢？”

En: There's just something so *mysterious* about the way he talks and moves...
Zh: “他说话和行动的方式总是有种说不出的*神秘感*……”

En: He's such a good listener, I liked talking to him.
Zh: “他是个很好的倾听者，我喜欢跟他说话。”

En: Right after you. He nods at the apartment door before you.
Zh: “你先吧。”他朝着你面前的公寓大门点点头。

En: His shirt... The lieutenant squints his eyes, trying to hold back laughter.
Zh: “他的衬衫……”警督眯着眼，努力忍住不笑。

En: Honestly I'm just trying to not screw anything up.
Zh: “老实说，我只是不想把事情搞砸。”

En: Beautiful, he says again. A nearby street lamp casts shadows on his chin, drawing out the slender cheekbones.
Zh: “漂亮，”他又说了一遍。附近的街灯在他的下巴上投下阴影，让他纤细的颧骨显得更加修长。

En: So tell me, are you here to make things *right* again?
Zh: “告诉我，你来是为了让事情恢复*正常*吗？”

En: A man on high heels stumbles out of a basement club, music blasting over the entire district. It's chilly, and as the chemicals hit his nervous system a thousand shivers ripple through his body.
Zh: 一个穿着高跟鞋的男人跌跌撞撞地从一间地下俱乐部走了出来，震耳欲聋的音乐声响彻整个区域。天很冷，化学药品击中了他的神经系统，他全身上下止不住地颤抖着。

En: He smells good. Why on earth does he smell so good?
Zh: “他的味道很好闻。为什么他身上这么好闻？”

En: His shirt.
Zh: “他的衬衫。”

En: His shirt...
Zh: “他的衬衫……”

En: He's trying to dive deep into the mysteries of his *shirt*!
Zh: 他想要深入探讨他的*衬衫*之谜！

En: You liked talking to him... The lieutenant squints his eyes, trying to hold back laughter.
Zh: “你喜欢跟他说话……”警督眯着眼，努力忍住不笑。

En: It made me feel *special*.
Zh: “这让我感觉很*特别*。”

En: It made you feel *special*...
Zh: “这让你感觉很*特别*……”

En: Come on, detective. Let's go -- we've got a potential witness to interview -- his 'Sunday friend', remember? He nods at the apartment door before you.
Zh: “来吧，警探。我们走——我们还要走访一个可能的目击证人——他的‘周日朋友’，还记得吗？”他朝着你面前的公寓大门点点头。

En: Something so mysterious about the way he talks... The lieutenant squints his eyes, trying to hold back laughter.
Zh: “他说话的方式有点神秘……”警督眯着眼，努力忍住不笑。

En: He *smells* good... The lieutenant squints his eyes, trying to hold back laughter.
Zh: “他的味道很*好闻*……”警督眯着眼，努力忍住不笑。

En: *Very* mysterious.
Zh: “*非常*神秘。”

En: *Very*.
That's weird, right?
Zh: “这有点奇怪，是吗？”

En: He smells good and that's weird.
Zh: “他的味道很好闻，这很奇怪。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: And he's gone again. Looks like it's becoming a theme for him.
Zh: 他又离开了。看来这已经变成了他的主题。

En: Let's go, Kim. We have to interview his Sunday friend. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们走吧，金。我们还要走访他那位周日朋友。”[离开。]

En: He's always leaving... Why is he always leaving, Kim?
Zh: “他总是离开……他为什么老是离开，金？”

En: Who knows, detective? It's a... mystery, he says, turning his face away from you.
Zh: “谁知道呢，警探？这是一个……谜题，”他一边说着，一边别过脸不去看你。

En: Alright, go then.
Zh: “好吧，那就走吧。”

En: Something flutters in the corner of the lieutenant's mouth as you're saying those words.
Zh: 你说出这些话的时候，有什么东西在警督的嘴角跳动。

En: His shirt... No, I don't know why his shirt is always unbuttoned. His mouth tightens, as though trying to hold something back.
Zh: “他的衬衫……不，我不知道他为什么老是不扣扣子。”他紧闭双唇，像是在忍耐着什么。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: He's going to leave you alone again. That's sad.
Zh: 他又要留下你一个人了，真是太悲伤了。

En: It's laughter!
Zh: 是笑声！

En: There, he's laughing again!
Zh: 看，他又笑了！

En: But I just *found* you again!
Zh: “但我刚刚才又*找到*你！”

En: Go, if you must, I don't care. I don't care about people leaving me all the time.
Zh: “走吧，如果你必须这么做的话，我不在乎。我不在乎所有人总是离我远去。”

En: Just look at it... He gestures towards distant motorways, all leading to golden La Delta. "It's a beautiful night. Who's going to stay in on a night like this?"
Zh: “看看它……”他朝着远处的高速公路比了个手势，它们全都通往金色的德尔塔。“今晚夜色很美。这么美的夜晚，谁还会待在家里呢？”

En: Don't worry, we'll meet again. He gently rests his hand on your shoulder. "Come find me in the bar of the Whirling some evening."
Zh: “别担心，我们还会再见的。”他把手轻轻搭在你的肩膀上。“哪天晚上到飞旋旅社的酒吧来找我吧。”

En: Something tells you you're never going to talk to an individual this cool or mysterious ever again.
Zh: 有一种感觉告诉你，你再也不会遇到一个这么酷或者这么神秘的谈话对象了。

En: Downstairs a cat crosses the yard, disappearing into the bush.
Zh: 楼下，有一只小猫穿过后院，消失在灌木丛里。

En: He's right, only losers go to bed early. Time to break out the booze!
Zh: 他说得对，只有废物才会早睡。是时候开怀痛饮了！

En: His slender figure is backlit by city lights, its distant streets and motorways flashing like diamonds...
Zh: 城市的灯光从背后照亮他纤细的身影，远处的街道和高速公路像钻石一样闪闪烁烁……

En: We got your hint. Found the key right under that stone.
Zh: “我们收到了你的暗示，在石头下面找到了钥匙。”

En: I got your hint. Found the key right under that stone.
Zh: “我收到了你的暗示，在石头下面找到了钥匙。”

En: (Ignore his question.) "I was hoping to talk to a possible witness. Your balcony overlooks the murder scene."
Zh: （忽略他的问题。）“我希望能跟可能的目击证人聊聊。你家阳台正好可以俯瞰整个犯罪现场。”

En: Then I have some good news for you... His eyes narrow. A nearby street lamp casts shadows on his chin, drawing out the slender cheekbones.
Zh: “那我有好消息要告诉你了……”他眯起双眼。附近的街灯在他的下巴上投下阴影，让他纤细的颧骨显得更加修长。

En: Is it Friday tonight? It feels like Friday.
Zh: “今天是周五吗？感觉有点像周五。”

En: Yeah, it does feel like the end of the week. Such gentle weather...
Zh: “是啊，确实感觉像是周末。这么好的天气……”

En: By the way, I'm really digging the view here. (Point to the city skyline.)
Zh: “话说回来，我真的很喜欢这里的风景。”（指向城市的天际线。）

En: Mhmh, that's why I chose this place. He looks away, his cigarette end glowing in the dark. "Martinaise is special, isn't it?"
Zh: “嗯，所以我才会选了这里。”他看着远处，手中的烟蒂在黑暗中发出光芒。“马丁内斯挺特别的，不是吗？”

En: Wait, suddenly you're *digging* things? the lieutenant whispers to you, shaking his head.
Zh: “等等，你突然开始*喜欢*东西了？”警督摇摇头，对你小声说到。

En: My Sunday friend is visiting me tonight. I told him about you and he'd like to say hello. Step in, he's already waiting. He nods towards door #28.
Zh: “我的周日朋友今晚会来。我跟他提了你，他想跟你打个招呼。进来吧，他已经在等你了。”他朝28号门点了点头。

En: Even the rain feels nice. He leans over the railing and sticks out his hand to feel the rain.
Zh: “就连这雨也感觉很舒服。”他倚靠在栏杆上，伸出手去感受雨滴。

En: It feels like a Friday. He seems to be in a good mood today.
Zh: 今天感觉像是星期五。他好像心情很不错。

En: It does feel like the end of the week. It's so magical, the white falling against the black...
Zh: “感觉就像是周末。黑色与白色相互交映，真是充满魔力……”

En: He's barely holding it together. It's all he can do to keep from bursting out in laughter.
Zh: 他就快撑不住了。他唯一能做的，就是不要放声大笑。

En: Theory is the poetry of thought.
Zh: “理论是思想的诗歌。”

En: Maurice and Zuzanna will be back, I know it.
Zh: “我相信莫里斯和祖扎娜会回来的。”

En: Who the hell took my jacket?
Zh: “谁他妈的拿了我的夹克？”

En: Puncher and Wattmann should be required reading.
Zh: “庞奇和瓦特曼的著作应该变成必读书目。”

En: The only 'class' I care about is the working class.
Zh: “我唯一关心的‘阶级’就是无产阶级。”

En: If infra-materialism is wrong, I don't want to be right.
Zh: “如果亚唯物主义是错的，我情愿和它一起错下去。”

En: My grades are trash this semester.
Zh: “我这学期的绩点完蛋了。”

En: We're the only real communists left.
Zh: “我们是仅剩的真正的康米主义者了。”

En: An interesting point, Uli.
Zh: “有点意思，尤利。”

En: Kind of chilly tonight, Uli.
Zh: “今晚有点凉呢，尤利。”

En: That girl from my seminar *promised* she would stop by.
Zh: “和我一起上讨论课的那个女生*说了*她会来的。”

En: Would you mind? We've got *group business* to take care of.
Zh: “你介意……吗？我们有些*团队协作*需要完成。”

En: That means go home, gendarme.
Zh: “他的意思是请你回家，警官。”

En: The two young men are either oblivious to or ignoring your entrance. Their attentions are fixed on whatever it is they're stacking in the middle of the floor...
Zh: 这两个年轻人要么没注意到你进来了，要么根本不关心……他们全神贯注地在地板中央堆那个不知道什么东西……

En: I think it's holding, Ulixes...
Zh: “我觉得它稳住了，尤利西斯……”

En: It is, it's holding!
Zh: “没错，它稳住了！”

En: It's definitely not holding. Those matchboxes are stacked so haphazardly it's like they *want* them to collapse.
Zh: 它绝对没有稳住。这些火柴盒岌岌可危地堆在一起，简直像是他们就*想*让它倒塌似的。

En: Careful, careful!
Zh: “当心，当心！”

En: Damn. Hardly any difference... The young man shakes his head, and then looks up at you and the lieutenant with barely concealed irritation...
Zh: “该死。一点差别都没有……”年轻人摇摇头，然后带着不加掩饰的恼怒抬头看向你和警督。

En: Damn. Hardly any difference... The young man shakes his head, and then looks up at you with barely concealed irritation...
Zh: “该死。一点差别都没有……”年轻人摇摇头，然后带着不加掩饰的恼怒抬头看向你。

En: You two. You're late.
Zh: “你们两个迟到了。”

En: You're late.
Zh: “你迟到了。”

En: They should know the meeting starts at 22.00 *sharp*... His companion looks up at you and squints.
Zh: “他应该知道集会在22点*准时*开始……”他的同伴眯着眼打量你。

En: He should know the meeting starts at 22.00 *sharp*. His companion looks up at you and squints.
Zh: “他应该知道集会在22点*准时*开始。”他的同伴眯着眼打量你。

En: The gendarme returns. The young man turns to you. "What do you need?"
Zh: “警官回来了。”年轻人转向你。“你想干什么？”

En: You're back. The young man turns to you.
Zh: “你回来了。”年轻人转向你。

En: His eyes are tracing an invisible line back and forth from your jacket to his companion.
Zh: 他的目光在你的夹克和他的同伴的白色裤子之间来回逡巡。

En: What a coincidence! You two have the *same* jacket. What are the odds?
Zh: 真是个巧合！你们两个有*同一件*夹克。这几率得多小？

En: Based on the prevalence of white Saramirizian suits in Martinaise? Extremely low.
Zh: 考虑到萨拉米尔泽套装在马丁内斯的流行程度？可以忽略不计了。

En: Hey, Steban. Isn't that *your* jacket?
Zh: “嘿，史蒂文。这不是*你的*夹克吗？”

En: It certainly *looks* like my jacket, Ulixes. Where did you get that, gendarme?
Zh: “这*看上去*确实很像我的夹克，尤利西斯。你是从哪拿到它的，警官？”

En: I just found it... in a room.
Zh: “我就是从……一个房间里……看到它。”

En: Must be a coincidence. I see these jackets all the time.
Zh: “这一定是个巧合。这种夹克到处都是。”

En: This jacket is RCM property. It's been confiscated as part of an ongoing investigation.
Zh: “这件夹克现在是RCM的财产。它因为一起正在进行的调查被充公了。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: 'Confiscated'? Next he's probably going to say it was civil asset forfeiture...
Zh: “‘充公’？我看他接下来就要说这是没收公民财产……”

En: Unlikely. That's real Saramirizian twill. Only old Saramirizian communists and drug smugglers wear those anymore...
Zh: “这不太可能。那可是真正的萨拉米尔泽斜纹布。现在只有老萨拉米尔泽康米主义者和毒贩还穿这种衣服了……”

En: He is neither of those, of course. He is simply a poor student, putting on airs. Also, he doesn't have the shoulders to fill out such a jacket.
Zh: 当然，他并不属于这二者。他只是个自命不凡的穷学生。况且，他的窄肩可撑不起这样一件夹克。

En: See, Uli? It's just like Mazov wrote. How does it go again?
Zh: “你瞧，尤利？就和马佐夫写的一样。那句话是怎么说的来着？”

En: Damn right. If he keeps mouthing off, you should take his pants too.
Zh: 一点不错。要是他再喋喋不休，你就把他的裤子也扒了。

En: 'Those committed to the rights of property are those most apt to violate them'. His companion nods emphatically.
Zh: “‘那些致力于保护财产权的人也最容易侵犯它’……”他的同伴点头强调语气。

En: I assumed it was Maurice who broke into my room, to play a trick on me. I didn't think I'd *actually* been raided by the RCM!
Zh: “我还以为是莫里斯闯进我的房间搞恶作剧。谁能想到*居然*是RCM抢劫了我！”

En: Just a minute. Steban... Ulixes... *why* do those names ring the faintest of bells?
Zh: 等一下。史蒂文……尤利西斯……*为什么*这些名字给你一种隐隐的熟悉感？

En: Probably because they're the real names of 'Nasteb' and 'Exilus', the authors of that so-called essay about TipTop Tournée you read...
Zh: 可能因为它们就是‘凡史蒂’和‘西利尤斯’的真名，也就是你之前读过的那篇所谓的大师巡回赛批判的作者。

En: You should get to the bottom of this, when you have the chance.
Zh: 你应该抽时间仔细读一读那篇文章。

En: There is surprise in his voice, naturally, but is that a note of *excitement* you also detect?
Zh: 他听起来有些惊讶，这很正常，但为什么你还能听出来一丝*兴奋*？

En: So, do you want your jacket *back*?
Zh: “那你想*要回*你的夹克吗？”

En: Listen, comrade, it's not what it looks like...
Zh: “听着，同志，事情不是你想的那样……”

En: Why do you sound *excited* to be raided by the RCM?
Zh: “为什么你被RCM抢劫了，反而听起来有点*兴奋*？”

En: Look, man, it doesn't even fit you, anyway.
Zh: “你看，老兄，反正它对你来说根本就不合身嘛。”

En: Absolutely not. You have to keep it...
Zh: “绝不，请你留着它。”

En: You're right. It's *better*!
Zh: “没错。事情比我想得更好！”

En: Oh, gendarme, because this is perfect...
Zh: “哦，警官，因为它是完美的……”

En: You're right. It was a gift from my grandmother. But now you've *got* to keep it...
Zh: “你说得对。它是我祖母送给我的礼物。但你*得*留下它……”
