En: You're intimidated by all the books these kids have read, aren't you? Maybe a trip to the bookstore would do you some good.
Zh: 你在这些孩子读过的书面前感到自惭形秽了，是吧？也许去一趟书店能帮到你。

En: What is it? Are you scared because you can't remember a single book you've read?
Zh: 是什么？你是因为无法想起任何一本读过的书而感到恐慌吗？

En: Nod again.
Zh: 再点一次头。

En: Shake your head no.
Zh: 摇头表示你不好。

En: Is everything alright? The young man turns to the lieutenant. "Is he trying to say something?"\n
Zh: “你还好吗？”年轻人转向警督。“他是不是有什么想说的？”

En: Is everything alright? Is there something you're trying to say?
Zh: “你还好吗？你是不是有什么想说的？”

En: What is it? Are you scared because you can't remember a single book you've read?
Zh: 是什么？你是因为无法想起任何一本读过的书而感到恐慌吗？

En: You're intimidated by all the books these kids have read, aren't you? Maybe a trip to the bookstore would do you some good.
Zh: 你在这些孩子读过的书面前感到自惭形秽了，是吧？也许去一趟书店能帮到你。

En: What are you worried about? You've read plenty of books. Why don't you take a little pause, come back once your mind's clear?
Zh: 你在担心什么？你读了不少书了。何不暂停一会儿，等头脑清醒了再回来？

En: As in, 'No, everything is *not* in order'? Can you give me a negative, detective?
Zh: “意思是‘不，一切都*不*好’吗？你能否确认一下，警探？”

En: Shake your head no.
Zh: 摇头表示你不好。

En: Nod again.
Zh: 再点一次头。

En: It seems he's lost his words. A case of momentary aphasia, it looks like... The lieutenant does not appear overly concerned.
Zh: “看起来他无法说话了。似乎是暂时性的失语症……”警督看起来并不十分担心。

En: It wouldn't be the first neurological episode you've experienced on this case.
Zh: 你在这起案件里已经不是第一次经历这种神经性的突然发作了。

En: In any case, a change of subject might help.
Zh: “不管怎么说，或许换个话题能有点帮助。”

En: Momentary aphasia, it seems. Just one more neurological episode for the station lazareth to look into during your next check-up.
Zh: 似乎是暂时性的失语症。在你下次体检的时候，分局检疫所需要检查的神经性突然发作又多了一次。

En: He looks like he's about to puke.
Zh: “他看起来像是要吐了。”

En: There might be a little bile gathering at the back of your throat, but it's just a taste. The source of this issue is higher up the wire...
Zh: 或许你的嗓子眼里积蓄起了一种苦意，但那只是味道而已。这个问题的根源可在嗓子的上面……

En: Nod your head.
Zh: 点头。

En: These things happen with him sometimes. I recommend a change of subject.
Zh: “他有时候会经历这种事情。我建议我们换个话题。”

En: Would it help if we changed the subject?
Zh: “如果我们换个话题，会不会有点帮助？”

En: This isn't going to work. Maybe there's some other way you could prove yourself to them?
Zh: 这行不通。或许你可以用另外一种方式向他们证明你自己？

En: It's because I don't do well in interviews.
Zh: 这是因为我不擅长面试。

En: Definitely not sweating, no sir. (Dab the sweat with your shirt.)
Zh: 绝不淌汗，不，先生。（用上衣轻拭汗水。）

En: Definitely not sweating, no sir. (Wipe the sweat away.)
Zh: 绝不淌汗，不，先生。（擦掉汗水。）

En: I'm not nervous, I'm a superstar.
Zh: 我是超级明星，我不会紧张。

En: Why is this happening? I thought I had my shit together!
Zh: 为什么会这样？我以为我准备充分了！

En: Why am I getting so worked up? They're just a couple of kids!
Zh: 为什么我这么紧张？他们只是两个小朋友！

En: Excellent work, now there's a dark handprint on the back of your shirt. Everyone will be able to see the evidence of your overactive sweat glands.
Zh: 干得漂亮。现在你的上衣背后留下了一抹黑手印。所有人都能看到你过分发达的汗腺留下的痕迹。

En: Excellent work, now there's a glistening smear across your bare chest. Everyone will be able to see the evidence of your overactive sweat glands.
Zh: 干得漂亮。现在你光裸的胸膛上闪耀着汗液。所有人都能看到你过分发达的汗腺留下的痕迹。

En: Oh, totally. A superstar breaking out in a cold sweat.
Zh: 哦，真不错。超级明星陷入了超级冷汗。

En: You did. You must be extremely nervous for some reason.
Zh: 确实如此。你一定超级紧张。

En: Let's be frank, they're probably way smarter than you. You bet they've read more books than you can even name...
Zh: 我们说实话，他们一定比你聪明多了。你能想到他们读过的书，你可能听都没听说过……

En: No, you're terrible at them. The thought of everyone looking at you, judging you, makes you want to heave, frankly...
Zh: 不，你在他们面前的表现简直糟糕。一想到他们都在盯着你，评判你，你就忍不住想吐……

En: You've been quite studious these past few days. You should be able to handle whatever they want to throw your way.
Zh: 这几天你十分勤奋好学。不管他们要向你丢出什么问题，你都应该能够解答。

En: Of course, if you're nervous it wouldn't hurt to read another book or two.
Zh: 当然，如果你很紧张，再读一两本书也没什么。

En: Of course, it wouldn't kill you to crack open a book every once in a while.
Zh: 当然，如果你偶尔再翻翻书，这总不会有什么坏处。

En: The hardest part will just be working up the nerve to ask without soiling yourself.
Zh: 最难的部分只不过是别怕丢面子，鼓起勇气问他们。

En: Everything alright, gendarme? You look a little green about the gills...
Zh: “你还好吗，警官？你看起来有点面色发绿……”

En: Listen, there's got to be some other way I could prove myself to the group.
Zh: “听着，肯定有别的方式能让我向小组证明我自己的。”

En: 'Some other way'? He squints at you. "If you're not much of a reader, why are you so dead-set on joining this reading group?"
Zh: “其他方式？”他眯眼看你。“如果你不爱读书，为什么你还死命要加入我们的小组？”

En: It's important that I further my critical studies.
Zh: “我亟需拓展批判性学习。”

En: I'm sorry to beg, but I've come all this way, please, don't turn me back now.
Zh: “我不想恳求你们，但是我都走到这一步了，求求你们别把我赶走。”

En: I've come so far, please don't turn me back now.
Zh: “我都和你们聊了这么久了，拜托别把我赶走。”

En: For me it's a matter of honour. I must finish what I started.
Zh: “这和我的荣耀息息相关。我绝不半途而废。”

En: I'm a completionist. I've got to finish what I start.
Zh: “我是个完美主义者。任何事情一旦开始就一定要完成。”

En: Because this plasm stuff sounds totally disco. I have to get to the bottom of it.
Zh: “因为这个什么凝胶听起来实在是太迪斯科了。我非得刨根问底不可。”

En: Like Ignus Nilsen himself, I've come to foretell a Great Reckoning.
Zh: “和伊格纳斯·尼尔森一样，我来向你们预告一场末日的大审判。”

En: I suppose we are all here to advance our studies in some form or another...
Zh: “我想我们都是来深入学习的，不管是以什么形式……”

En: The young man sighs. "God, it's so pitiful watching a grown man beg. I can hardly stand it."
Zh: 年轻人叹了口气。“老天，看着一个成年人苦苦哀求可真让我难过。我受不了了。”

En: 'Honour' is an outmoded concept, a relic of feudal ideology. All the same, I have to admire your commitment...
Zh: “‘荣耀’是一个过时的概念，是封建思想的孑遗。不过不管怎么说，我还是欣赏你的努力……”

En: I do have to admire a certain tenacity, even when it seems to make no sense.
Zh: “我得说，我欣赏你的苦功，即使它并不怎么合理。”

En: Oh, it *is*, and it only gets *more disco* from here...
Zh: “哦，它*确实*很迪斯科，而且之后会变得*更迪斯科*起来……”

En: Hmmm. I thought I perceived some *eschatological* air about you...
Zh: “哼……我确实感觉到你周身笼罩着末世的气息。”

En: But to be perfectly frank, I just don't see it happening.
Zh: “不过说老实话，我并不认为会有什么大审判。”

En: Steban, a moment... The young man's companion beckons him into a private conference.
Zh: “史蒂文，过来一下……”年轻人的同伴把他拉到一边讲起了悄悄话。

En: What... really? You're saying... but there's no way... uh huh... yes, okay, I'm listening...
Zh: “什么……真的？你是说……但是不可能……啊哈……好的，好极了，我听着呢……”

En: Alright, gendarme. He turns back to you. "Comrade Ulixes and I have conferred about it, and we've come up with an alternative way for you to convince us of your commitment to the group."
Zh: 他转回到你面前。“好吧，警官。尤利西斯同志和我探讨了这件事情，现在我们想出了另一种方式，能让你向我们证明你对小组的贡献。”

En: What's that?
Zh: “是什么？”

En: The young man gives his companion an uncertain glance. The companion nods his head vigorously.
Zh: 年轻人不确定地看了一眼他的同伴。同伴激烈地点点头……

En: *Hit* you?
Why would I do that?
Zh: “我为什么要这么做？”

En: It doesn't have to be a *hard* hit. He indicates his cheek. "Just enough to leave a mark."
Zh: “不需要*用劲*打。”他点点自己的脸颊。“只要留下伤痕就行。”

En: The idea, his companion jumps in, "is to show Maurice and those other traitors that we're the only reading group *hard core* enough to tangle with the RCM..."
Zh: “我们的意图就是，”他的同伴插话：“让莫里斯和其他叛徒看看，我们是唯一一个*硬核*到同RCM产生冲突的阅读小组……”

En: Now's your chance to redeem yourself for that dive you took before the gremlin child.
Zh: 是时候弥补你在这个小地精面前跳水的举动了。

En: Now's your chance to avenge yourself for your embarrassment by the harbour gates.
Zh: 是时候为你在港口大门陷入的尴尬处境复仇了。

En: He won't be the first unweaned punk you've shut down. You'll make mincemeat of this fool's jaw.
Zh: 你揍过的乳臭未干的朋克小青年可不止他一个。你会把这个傻瓜的下巴打成肉酱。

En: You've done it before, and against a *much* tougher opponent. You'll make mincemeat of this fool's jaw.
Zh: 你曾经做过这个，并且当时你的对手要比他们强硬*得多*。你会把这个傻瓜的下巴打成肉酱。

En: This is what you've been waiting for! Time to lay his little punk-ass out.
Zh: 你还在等什么！是时候胖揍他的朋克小屁股了。

En: Ah, we're replaying *this* little episode. Please, do try to stay on your feet this time.
Zh: “啊，我们又要重复*这次*发作了。拜托，这次千万要站稳。”

En: This has certainly turned out to be a punchier case than I had anticipated.
Zh: “这起案件涉及的打斗比我想象中多得多了。”

En: Ah, so we're beating *another* youth only tangentially connected to the case. Spectacular.
Zh: “啊，所以我们又要因为和案子的一丁点联系，胖揍*另一个*小年轻了吗。令人惊叹。”

En: Easy, detective. No need to treat this kid like he's some Son of Measurehead.
Zh: “小心点，警探。没必要把这个小孩当成什么测颅先生的儿子。”

En: Ah, so we're beating the youth now? That's an unexpected development.
Zh: “啊，所以我们现在要开始打小孩了？我真没预料到这种发展。”

En: Of course, this isn't some punch-out competition. You just want to leave a harmless bruise.
Zh: 当然，这不是什么拳击比赛。你只想造成一点无害的淤青。

En: Just theorising here, but if you don't have as much control over your muscle groups as you'd like, perhaps you could try something you *know* is bound to fail...
Zh: 只是提个建议，要是你觉得不能很好地控制自己的肌群，或许你可以试试你*知道*一定会失败的举动……

En: Wait, you're saying I could try to really lay this kid out, knowing I'll probably screw it up?
Zh: 等等，你是说我可以真的尝试痛击这个小孩，因为我很有可能会失败？

En: That sounds really stupid.
Zh: 这听起来很愚蠢。

En: Precisely! It's brilliant.
Zh: 一点不错！这好极了。

En: Yes, stupidly *brilliant*.
Zh: 是的，蠢得好极了。

En: Assuming you don't accidentally succeed...
Zh: 只是假设你没有一不小心成功……

En: Kim, how bad an idea is this?
Zh: “金，你说这个主意也太糟糕了吧？”

En: Hey, Kim. what if *you* hit him?
Zh: “嘿，金，要不还是*你*来打他？”

En: Hit him, but not too hard.
Zh: 打他，但不要太用力。

En: Fell before the Cuno.
Zh: 在坤诺面前摔倒。

En: Squeezed by Measurehead.
Zh: 被测颅先生打倒。

En: Shut down Cuno.
Zh: 打倒坤诺。

En: Beat Measurehead.
Zh: 打倒测颅先生。

En: Punchy while drinking.
Zh: 打醉拳。

En: Know RCM technique.
Zh: 知道RCM技巧。

En: This is absurd. I don't want to hit you. (Cancel task.)
Zh: “这太荒唐了。我不想打你。”（取消任务。）

En: The lieutenant looks at you, then at the young man. He seems to be giving your question serious consideration.
Zh: 警督看了看你，又看了看年轻人。他好像在认真考虑你的问题。

En: Speaking as an officer of the RCM, it is an *extremely* bad idea to strike a civilian without cause...
Zh: “作为RCM的警官，无缘无故攻击平民是一个*非常*糟糕的主意……”

En: Speaking frankly, it's a terrible idea. The RCM already has issues with *wanton brutality* and this is unlikely to ameliorate those concerns...
Zh: “老实说，这是个坏主意。RCM本就因*滥用暴力*而恶名远扬，而这个举动想必不会对我们的名声有什么帮助……”

En: However, if one were to strike a civilian, which again I would never recommend, one should at least follow the RCM manual's guidance on non-lethal strikes.
Zh: “但是，如果警官真的要攻击平民——虽然我永远不会支持这种举动——他们至少应当遵守RCM手册对非致命攻击手段的指导意见。”

En: What does the manual say?
Zh: “手册怎么说？”

En: I don't need a book to tell me how to deck a guy.
Zh: “我用不着一本书来教我怎么打人。”

En: It advises officers in physical altercations to rely on the open-palm strike, as opposed to the backhand, in non-lethal situations.
Zh: “手册建议警官在发生冲突时，使用手掌而非手背进行攻击，以避免致命伤害。”

En: You see, that attitude is precisely why the field manual's guidance is so valuable...
Zh: “你瞧，这种态度就是为什么手册的指导意见对现场调查来说这么重要……”

En: I can't recall the exact explanation, but it has something to do with the kinetic energy stored in the elbow. Basically, you're less able to control a *backhanded* slap, and therefore more likely to cause serious injury.
Zh: “我记不清具体的解释了，但是这和肘部运动的动能有关。简单来说，你会更不能控制一个*反手的*耳光，也就因此更容易造成严重伤害。”

En: Or even death, if, say, you happen to be wearing a set of armoured ceramic gauntlets.
Zh: “在某些情况下，比如说你刚好戴着一双陶瓷装甲手套，那甚至可能造成死亡。”

En: At the same time, this is emphatically *your thing*, so do as you will.
Zh: “不过，这总归是*你的事*，所以请自行选择。”

En: Well, gendarme? What are we waiting for?
Zh: “来吧，警官？你在等什么？”

En: There's no *what if*, because I won't. This is your thing, remember?
Zh: “没有什么*要不*，因为我不会这么做。这是你的事，记得吗？”

En: As with all applications of violence, the key is the appropriate amount of force exercised with absolute control on the part of the wielder.
Zh: 和其他任何暴力手段一样，关键在于何时的力度，以及对施力部位的绝对控制……

En: If you were trying this against an actual opponent, it would be a meagre, embarrassing effort.
Zh: 面对一个真正的对手时，这样的小心翼翼会成为一个笑话。

En: Fortunately, you're trying *not* to cause any real damage.
Zh: 但幸运的是，当下你的目标就是*不*造成任何真正的损伤。

En: Ensconced in your ceramic armour, you barely notice the moment of contact. Striking the young man's face is almost indistinguishable from swatting away a fly.
Zh: 在陶瓷盔甲的保护下，你几乎没有任何感觉。打击年轻人的脸颊，给你的触感就和挥走一只苍蝇差不多。

En: At the moment of contact you feel the pads of your bare fingers brush the wiry hairs of the young man's beard, as though caressing a wad of kitchen wool.
Zh: 接触的一瞬间，你感到自己的指肚划过年轻人蜷曲的胡须，触感如同抚摸一块地毯。

En: The actual moment of contact is ever-so-slightly disappointing, as the feeling is dulled by the yellow latex shell that contains your hand.
Zh: 真正接触时，你的指尖触感被包裹双手的黄色橡胶手套所阻隔，这个瞬间短暂得令你失望。

En: At the moment of contact you feel the wool of your fingerless gloves entangling with the wiry hairs of the young man's beard. His eyes are wide as a pair of old silver coins.
Zh: 接触的一瞬间，你感到无指手套的羊绒材质钩住了年轻人蜷曲的胡须。他双眼圆睁，仿佛两枚古旧的银币。

En: At the moment of contact the high-performance gripping that covers the palm of your glove causes your hand to *stick* for an instant against the young man's cheek. As your hand pulls away, his pale skin appears momentarily to follow with it.
Zh: 接触的一瞬间，你的高性能手套的掌心布料，令你的手掌在年轻人的脸颊上*停留*了一刹那。在你收手的时候，他苍白的皮肤似乎还被你的手掌带动。

En: The young man staggers back into the couch, eyes agog and momentarily dazed, but not seriously hurt.
Zh: 年轻人踉跄着后退一步，倒进了沙发，傻乎乎地瞪着眼，但并没有受到重伤。

En: Not the best look for the RCM, but I suppose it could've gone worse.
Zh: “这对RCM的形象可没有什么好处，不过我想已经优于许多更加糟糕的状况了。”

En: Steban? Are you alright?
Zh: “史蒂文？你还好吗？”

En: Who's the baddest communist now?
Zh: “现在，谁才是最厉害的康米主义者？”

En: You need this? (Offer him Kim's handkerchief.)
Zh: “你需要这个吗？”（把金的手帕递给他。）

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: That... was *amazing*. Uli, perhaps we should tangle with the gendarmerie more often. The young man rubs his cheek.
Zh: “这……*棒*极了。尤利，也许我们应该多和这位警官吵吵嘴。”年轻人揉揉脸颊。

En: No, keep it. I feel... *great*, actually... He turns to his companion. "Perhaps we should tangle with the gendarmerie more often."
Zh: “不用，没事。我感觉……*非常好*，真的……”他转向同伴。“也许我们应该多和这位警官吵吵嘴。”

En: I'm fine. Better than fine, actually. The young man rubs his cheek. "Perhaps we should tangle with the gendarmerie more often."
Zh: “我没事。好吧，其实是很好。”年轻人揉揉脸颊。“也许我们应该多和这位警官吵吵嘴。”

En: It doesn't hurt, yet. The rush of endorphins will keep the pain at bay for a while yet.
Zh: 痛感还没起来呢。迅速分泌的内啡肽会为他抑制住疼痛，但那不会持续很久。

En: He isn't bluffing. He appears invigorated, as though he's truly experiencing life for the first time.
Zh: 他没有装模作样。那兴高采烈的样子，仿佛他第一次真正体验人生。

En: You've earned his respect. See how you've *marked* him?
Zh: 你赢得了他的尊重。看看你是怎么*标记*了他的？

En: In the meantime, a small indigo flower begins to bloom just below his cheekbone.
Zh: 与此同时，他的颧骨上开出一朵靛青色的小花。

En: That would be the sweet endorphin rush. You've granted him a natural high, at least until the hurt sets in.
Zh: 想必是甜美的内啡肽在发挥作用。至少在痛觉恢复之前，你把他搞爽了。

En: A little ice on it will keep the swelling down. He'll be fine in a day or two.
Zh: 冰敷可以帮助消肿。一两天之后他就没事了。

En: TOO STRONG, KILLER.
Zh: 太猛了，杀手。

En: OH YEAH, we're gonna spin this kid like a top!
Zh: 哦耶，我们要像一个真正的大哥一样把这个小孩玩弄于掌心！

En: Wait-wait-wait, you were trying *not* to do that!
Zh: 等等等等，你本来的目的可*不是*这个！

En: Ensconced in your ceramic armour, you barely notice the moment of contact. But you do hear the distinct *ting* of your knuckles catching the young man's cheekbone.
Zh: 在陶瓷盔甲的保护下，你几乎没有任何感觉。但是你确实听到了指节撞击颧骨时独特的*脆响*。

En: Even through the yellow latex shell containing your hand you can feel the force of the impact against the young man's cheekbone.
Zh: 即使隔着黄色橡胶手套，你也能感觉到拳击年轻人颧骨的反作用力。

En: En route to its target your FALN glove traces a strangely pleasing black-and-white blur through the air, like some kind of ultra-sonic weasel.
Zh: 你的FALN手套落在他脸上，在空气中留下一道赏心悦目的黑白残影，像一只超音速黄鼠狼。

En: At the moment of contact you're reminded of a fight you once witnessed between two homeless men in an alleyway. Could it be that you've set out to recreate that moment now, right down to the smelly fingerless gloves?
Zh: 接触的一瞬间，你回想起曾在小巷里见过的两个流浪汉之间的斗殴。你是否正在尝试复制那个瞬间，通过这双散发着异味的无指手套？

En: You feel a bright pain as your bare knuckle collides with the young man's oddly prominent cheekbone. At the moment of contact his eyes are wide as old silver coins.
Zh: 你裸露的指节撞在年轻人古怪的高颧骨上，一阵刺骨的疼痛从手上传来。接触的一瞬间，他双眼圆睁，仿佛两枚古旧的银币。

En: Of course, it's not *actually* ultra-sonic, that's just an illusion produced by good branding. Still, the force you've unleashed really is tremendous.
Zh: 当然，你没有*真的*用超音速打他，那只是名牌产品带来的错觉。但是，你挥拳的力道依然超乎想象。

En: Oh god, tell us you haven't done it *again*. Are you twice the killer now?
Zh: 哦老天，你不会*又*干了一次这种事吧。你现在是连环杀手了吗？

En: Oh, no. Tell us you didn't *really* kill him.
Zh: 哦不，你不会*真的*杀了他吧。

En: Good god, detective! What's wrong with you?
Zh: “天啊，警探！你怎么回事？”

En: Steban, can you hear me?
Zh: “史蒂文，你能听见吗？”

En: Errrrck. The young man cradles his jaw and looks up, his expression blank.
Zh: “呃呃呃……”年轻人捧住下巴，眼神空洞地抬头。

En: You're both lucky you didn't shatter his cheekbone. He may want to count his teeth before he goes to bed tonight.
Zh: 你没有一巴掌拍碎他的颧骨，这对你们两人都是幸事。他今晚睡觉之前或许得数一下自己的牙了。

En: Oh, god. I'm sorry about that.
Zh: “哦，老天。我真的很抱歉。”

En: Let that be a lesson to you.
Zh: “给你一个教训。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 一言不发。

En: *Sorry*? You nearly took my fucking jaw off!
Zh: “*抱歉*？你他妈的差点把我的下巴打碎！”

En: What lesson is that, huh? That the RCM are assholes? That they'll take your fucking jaw off?
Zh: “什么教训，哈？你想告诉我，RCM都是混蛋？他们会打碎别人的下巴？”

En: What? You're just gonna stand there after you nearly broke my fucking jaw?
Zh: “什么？你在差点打碎了我的下巴之后，就打算他妈的站在那里什么也不说吗？”

En: You think you can hear a slight popping sound as he moves his mouth.
Zh: 他动嘴的时候你能听到轻微的泡泡声。

En: It certainly won't improve your precinct's image within the community.
Zh: 这对于你的分局在群众面前的形象显然不会有任何帮助。

En: That's right. You don't *owe* him any explanation.
Zh: 没错。你不*欠*他任何解释。

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: Thank god we avoided *that* fiasco, at least...
Zh: “感谢老天我们至少避免了*那种*情况……”

En: For his part, the young man looks rather put off. It's clear he was *also*  looking forward to you punching him.
Zh: 年轻人看起来有些失落。显然他*也*指望着被你殴打。

En: The young man can hardly conceal his disappointment. He was apparently looking forward to being punched by you.
Zh: 年轻人几乎掩饰不住他的失望。显然他指望着被你殴打。

En: And yet you can't help but sense a slight disappointment on his part. It seems a part of him hoped you would hit the pretentious young communist...
Zh: 但你忍不住为他感到一丝失望。看起来他似乎隐隐希望你能够重击这个自命不凡的年轻康米主义者……

En: Well, if you're not going to play along, I don't think we have anything further to discuss. Good evening to you, gendarme.
Zh: “好吧，如果你不想按我们说好的来，那我们也没什么可聊的了。晚安，警官。”

En: I'm ready... let's talk about infra-materialism.
Zh: “我准备好了……我们来探讨亚唯物主义吧。”

En: Yes, let's get right to it.
Zh: 他点点头。“好，我们现在就开始吧。”

En: You'll be leading this conversation, so wherever you take it, they'll be glad to follow.
Zh: 你来起头。你说什么，他们都会乐意跟着你讨论。

En: I have some questions about the origins of this theory.
Zh: “我对这个理论的起源有一些问题。”

En: I really want to hear more about these hyper-revolutionary figures.
Zh: “我只想进一步了解那些超-革命人物。”

En: It's the theoretical parts I'm interested in. The third-level stuff.
Zh: “我对理论部分感兴趣。”

En: Oh, it's a great story. You should check out Nilsen's journals if you ever have the chance.
Zh: “哦，那是个很有趣的故事。如果有机会，你可以读一度尼尔森的日记。”

En: Ah, yes. That's certainly one of the *least* developed parts of the theory, but also one of the most intriguing.
Zh: “啊，好。它是这个理论*最不*完善的部分之一，但也是最有趣的部分之一。”

En: Oh, yes. Or the *fun parts*, as we like to call them.
Zh: “哦，好。我们也喜欢管它叫*乐趣部分*。”

En: His companion nods in agreement.
Zh: 他的同伴点头表示同意。

En: His companion leans forward, ready to jump in...
Zh: 他同伴身体前倾，随时准备插入对话……

En: His companion nods his head with expectation.
Zh: 他的同伴期盼地点点头。

En: These two are full of interesting stories and rumours about Nilsen, Mazov, and their revolutionary exploits. Half an hour passes and the conversation gradually wends its way to some unexpected places...
Zh: 这两人对尼尔森、马佐夫轶闻趣事以及革命成果简直门清。你们聊了半个小时，对话逐渐拐向了前所未料的方向……

En: They're impressed that you dove right in to the most advanced parts of the theory. Half an hour evaporates and the conversation is still wending its way toward new and unexpected places...
Zh: 他们很惊讶你一头栽进了这个理论最前沿的部分。半个小时凭空蒸发，对话逐渐拐向了前所未料的全新方向……

En: A cool breeze coming off the bay wraps itself around the Capeside Apartments. In a nearly forgotten part of that glorified construction site, surrounded by rusted pieces of scaffolding and walls of faded tarpaulin, a detective of the RCM debates the intricacies of an abstruse theory with a pair of university students...
Zh: 清凉的夜风从海湾边吹来，绕着岬岸公寓打转。在这曾经辉煌的施工地点一个被遗忘的角落，被生锈的脚手架零件与褪色油布构成的墙壁所包围着，一名RCM的警探与两名大学生正辩论着一个深奥理论的精妙之处……

En: ... but if it's true that this individual possesses *so much capital* that light literally *bends* around him, then that must mean...
Zh: “……但如果这个人拥有*这么多资本*，以至于光线都在他周围*扭曲*，这是否意味着……”

En: That *capital* produces its *own* form of plasm!
Zh: “意味着*资本*能够产生出它*自己的*凝胶类型！”

En: That *light itself* is a bourgeois illusion!
Zh: “那个*光线*本身就是资产阶级的幻觉！”

En: In 'Innocence of Capital', Puncher and Wattmann argue that communists must possess as little capital as possible, in order to keep their thinking *undiluted* by its influence...
Zh: “在‘资本无罪论’里，庞奇和瓦特曼提出，康米主义者应当持有尽可能少的资本，以避免自己的思想被它*稀释*……”

En: *Or* it suggests that capital, in sufficient quantities, must exhibit its own plasm-like qualities...
Zh: “*或者*它意味着，足够大量的资本能够体现出类似于凝胶的独有性质……”

En: Watch out. You call yourself a communist and yet you've been accumulating a *suspiciously* large share of capital in just the last few days. It's almost certainly distorting your thinking.
Zh: 注意。你自命为康米主义者，却在过去几天里积攒了数额大到*可疑*的资本。它一定在扭曲你的思想。

En: You've accrued a modest account balance over the last few days, but probably not enough to warp your thinking *too* much.
Zh: 过去几天里，你在自己账户里积累了数额适中的金钱，但它们或许还不能*严重*扭曲你的思想。

En: Nothing to fear. Judging from your account balance, you should be thinking *extremely* clearly.
Zh: 你无所畏惧。从你的账户余额来看，你的思想*一片清澈*。

En: Again, he was just a regular high-net-worth individual. There was nothing *extra-physical* about him...
Zh: “他只是个有-钱-人。他没有任何超物理效用……”

En: Now the breeze has subsided. All is still except for the last bits of steam rising from the coffee pot.
Zh: 风停了。除了咖啡壶上袅袅升起的最后一缕蒸汽，一切都静止下来。

En: ... but my question is where does the RCM *recruit* all these hyper-revolutionaries to join this 'Remote Viewers Division'?
Zh: “……但我的问题是，RCM是从哪里*招募*了那么多超-革命分子加入遥视者部门的？”

En: It's less that you're 'recruited' into the RVD and more like you're *called*.
Zh: “遥视者部门并不是‘招募’，而是更像*召唤*需要的人。”

En: And my *point* is that maybe these *hyper-revolutionary* individuals are more common than infra-materialism generally assumes...
Zh: “我的*意思*是，这个世界上的*超-革命分子*的存在，可能比亚唯物主义所估计的要普遍得多……”

En: Fascinating...
Zh: “有意思……”

En: Yes, only the detective here would draw a connection between a made-up division of para-psychics and the addled ramblings of a defunct communist mage...
Zh: “也只有这位警探才会觉得，一个捏造出来的类通灵师部门和一个死掉的康米主义思想家的信口开河，这两者能有什么联系。”

En: A fortunate turn that the lieutenant isn't here. Imagine what he would have to say about this.
Zh: 幸好警督不在。想想他会说什么吧。

En: The young man ignores the lieutenant. "But I suppose it makes sense that some common strain of infra-materialist thought would persist in the RCM, even to this day, given its origins."
Zh: 年轻人无视了警督。“但我想我可以理解，为什么到了今天，一些大众化的亚唯物主义思想依然存在于RCM。考虑到这个理论的起源。”

En: The young man nods thoughtfully. "But I suppose it makes sense that some common strain of infra-materialist thought would persist in the RCM, even to this day, given its origins."
Zh: 年轻人若有所思地点点头。“但我想我可以理解，为什么到了今天，一些大众化的亚唯物主义思想依然存在于RCM。考虑到这个理论的起源。”

En: ... all I'm saying is if you want to get *really extra-physical*, pyrholidon is the way to go...
Zh: “……我只想说，如果你想变得*非常超物理*，吡嚯浣酮是唯一的手段……”

En: ... anyway, I think I've made my point.
Zh: “……不管怎样，我已经说出了我的看法。”

En: ... or maybe I'm wrong. It could really go either way.
Zh: “……当然我也可能是错的。两种可能都有。”

En: Undoubtedly.
Zh: “显而易见。”

En: ... but I'm still trying to figure out how the *pale* fits into all this...
Zh: “……但我还在思索*灰域*和这些到底有什么关系……”

En: ... so wait, you're saying that President Knezhinisky has *never opened a book*?
Zh: “……等等，你是说涅仁斯基总统*从未翻开过书*？”

En: The two phenomena are actually deeply intertwined. He nods. "Many infra-materialists believe that pale is actually a manifestation of nostalgia and historical inertia..."
Zh: 他点点头。“这两种现象之间有着错综复杂的联系。许多亚唯物主义者们认为，灰域其实是怀旧与历史的惯性在现实中的显现……”

En: Those same theorists have hypothesised that revolution may in fact create a *counter-force* that prevents the pale from expanding.
Zh: “这些理论家也提出了一种假说，他们认为革命实际上制造了一种*反作用力*来抵御灰域的扩张。”

En: No-no-no. We're saying he doesn't *need* to open them, because he absorbs all the relevant content just by staring intently at the cover. It's a widely understood second-level effect...
Zh: “不-不-不。我们的意思是，他不*需要*翻开书，因为他能通过瞪视封面来吸收所有他需要的内容。这是第二级的效用之一……”

En: We've tried it ourselves, but thus far we haven't been able to absorb more than the preface and table of contents.
Zh: “我们自己也试过，但目前为止我们还只能吸收前言与目录的内容。”

En: The evening is winding down...
Zh: 夜色渐深……

En: Well, on that note, I think we can call it an evening.
Zh: “好极了，我想今晚的活动差不多该结束了。”

En: Yes. One of our better discussions lately, on the whole.
Zh: “是的。这是我们最近一段时间参与的最佳讨论之一。”

En: Hang on, is that *it*?
Zh: “等等，*这*就没了？”

En: What do you mean, 'is that it'? You've done the reading, we talked about it, what more do you expect from a reading group?
Zh: “什么叫‘这就没了’？你读完了材料，我们也讨论过了，你还想在阅读小组里做什么？”

En: What if I have more questions, you know, about communism?
Zh: “那我要是有更多，你懂的，关于康米主义的问题呢？”

En: Well, you could always *ask*, I guess. The two young men look at each other a moment.
Zh: 两个年轻人面面相觑了一刻。“我想，那你当然能随时*问*我们。”

En: But it's getting late, so maybe pick the *most important* question?
Zh: “但现在已经很晚了，所以你能选出*最重要的*那个问题吗？”

En: He probably won't get a better chance, honestly.
Zh: “老实说，这可能是他最好的提问机会了。”

En: Kim, what should I ask?
Zh: “金，我该问什么呢？”

En: I need a minute to think about it.
Zh: “我需要考虑一下。”

En: Ask the most important question about communism.
Zh: 问关于康米主义最重要的问题。

En: Showed him the rifle.
Zh: 给他展示步枪。

En: Showed him the figurine.
Zh: 给他展示小雕像。

En: Did all the reading.
Zh: 读完所有内容。

En: Did most of the reading.
Zh: 读完大部分内容。

En: Gave good edits.
Zh: 给出优秀的修订意见。

En: Haunted by lost love.
Zh: 被失去的爱情困扰。

En: Residual fascist tendencies.
Zh: 残余的法西斯倾向。

En: Called it a yank-fest.
Zh: 叫它扯鸡鸡比赛。

En: Called it a book club.
Zh: 叫它读书俱乐部。

En: I changed my mind. I don't have any questions.
Zh: “我改变主意了。我其实没有问题要问。”

En: Yeah, I'm good. No questions for me. (Cancel task.)
Zh: “是的，我很好。我不想问问题。”（取消任务。）

En: Detective, how should *I* know what questions *you* have about communism?
Zh: “警探，*我*怎么能知道*你*对康米主义有什么问题呢？”

En: Come on. We have things to do in the morning.
Zh: “来吧。我们早上还有事情要做呢。”

En: He says he doesn't have any questions, Steban.
Zh: “他说他没有问题要问，史蒂文。”

En: Are you absolutely sure? A communist can't be afraid to ask questions, just because they don't know what the answer might be.
Zh: “你真的确定吗？康米主义者应当勇于提问，不要顾惜面子。”

En: In other words, you'll forfeit their respect if you back out now.
Zh: 换句话说，如果你现在退缩，他们刚才对你产生的尊敬就要大打折扣了。

En: See, Steban? I told you there's no such thing as a gendarme communist.
Zh: “你瞧，史蒂文？我就说根本没有警官康米主义者这种东西。”

En: It seems you're right, Uli. I should've listened to you. He sighs, then turns to you.
Zh: “看来你是对的，尤利。我应该听你的。”他叹了口气，转向你。

En: Now, if you'll excuse us, we have some *group business* to attend to.
Zh: “如果您不介意的话，现在我们还有些*小组事务*要处理。”

En: The question you mean to ask is both very complicated and incredibly simple...
Zh: 你的问题无比复杂，同时也无比简单……

En: Alright, here we go. We're devoting all your available brain cells to coming up with a question about communism. Scratch that, to coming up with *the* question about communism, the alpha and omega of communism questions, and that question is:
Zh: 好了，来吧。我们已经开动了你所有能用的脑细胞来想出一个关于康米主义的问题。为了想出*这个*问题，所有康米主义问题的开始与终结，而这个问题就是……

En: Take a deep breath. Best to go one piece at a time.
Zh: 深呼吸。别着急。

En: If the whole theory we're meant to believe is completely unfalsifiable...
Zh: “如果我们应当相信的理论是完全无法被证伪的……”

En: If communism is all based on *theories* that are impossible to prove...
Zh: “如果康米主义的基础完全是无法被证实的*理论*……”

En: If we can't even agree whether communism is about plasm or psychology or beans...
Zh: “如果我们连康米主义到底是有关凝胶，心理学，还是豆子，都无法达成共识……”

En: If no one can agree what real communism even *means*...
Zh: “如果我们连真正的康米主义到底*象征着*什么都无法达成共识……”

En: If we can't escape the doom that's coming...
Zh: “如果我们无法逃脱即将来临的厄运……”

En: If communism keeps failing every time we try it...
Zh: “如果我们对于康米主义的每一次尝试都归于失败……”

En: The young man waits patiently for you to finish.
Zh: 年轻人耐心地等待你说完自己问题。

En: ... and the rest of world conspires to invade and massacre us when we dare to stand up for our beliefs...
Zh: “……而世界上的其他势力都勾结在一起，阴谋入侵、屠杀我们，因为我们胆敢站起来坚持理想……”

En: ... and the rest of the world keeps killing us for our beliefs...
Zh: “……而世界上的其他势力都因为我们的理想而追杀我们……”

En: ... and the SRV has degenerated into a corrupt farce of a workers' republic...
Zh: “……而SRV已经堕落成了一个腐朽的，笑话般的所谓工人国家……”

En: ... and whenever we *do* try it just devolves into a corrupt farce...
Zh: “……而我们不管*尝试*什么，最后都堕落成一个腐朽的笑话……”

En: ... and we waste all our time arguing over who's secretly a liberal or not...
Zh: “……而我们将所有时间浪费在争论我们是否是个自由主义者上面……”

En: ... and we keep fracturing over theoretical disagreements...
Zh: “……而我们始终因为理论上的分歧陷于党争……”

En: Yes?
Zh: “然后？”

En: Say it.
Zh: 说出来。

En: ... what's the point?
Zh: “……这有什么意义呢？”

En: The young man considers your words for a minute.
Zh: 年轻人听了你的话，沉思了一分钟。

En: You're witnessing his ironic armour melt before you. This is his *true self* you're seeing now.
Zh: 你眼见着他用讽刺搭建的盔甲在你面前熔化。现在你看见了他*真实的自我*。

En: He's thinking about someone...
Zh: 他在想着某个人……

En: Wait, who is he thinking about?
Zh: 等等，他在想谁？

En: Forget it.
Zh: 别问了。

En: The theorists Puncher and Wattmann -- not infra-materialists, but theorists nonetheless -- say that communism is a secular version of Perikarnassian theology, that it replaces faith in the divine with faith in humanity's future...
Zh: “理论学家庞奇和瓦特曼——不是亚唯物主义理论家，但也是理论家——认为康米主义只是佩里卡纳西斯神学的一种世俗形式，它取代了对于神明与人类未来的信仰……”

En: Hard to say. Someone dear to him.
Zh: 很难说。他的亲爱之人。

En: Track his gaze. He's looking out past the broken wall, toward the opposite side of the Bay...
Zh: 他的视线越过破碎的墙壁，望向海湾的对面……

En: Let the thought go.
Zh: 别想了。

En: What's there?
Zh: 那里是什么？

En: Toward the skyscrapers of La Delta.
Zh: 德尔塔的摩天大楼。

En: They rise like electric obelisks in the night.
Zh: 在夜色中，它们如同方尖碑一般伫立着。

En: Many tall buildings, belonging to a district whose name escapes you.
Zh: 那里有许多高楼大厦，但你无法想起这片区域的名字。

En: Who's in La Delta?
Zh: “德尔塔有谁？”

En: Who's in those skyscrapers over there?
Zh: “那里的摩天大楼里有谁？”

En: You're thinking about someone, aren't you?
Zh: “你在想着某个人，对吗？”

En: The matchboxes... you were trying to make the tower from Nilsen's journals.
Zh: “这些火柴盒……你们想用它们重构尼尔森日记里的那座塔。”

En: Broken.
Zh: “支离破碎。”

En: Unfinished.
Zh: “半途而废。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 一言不发。

En: So you really *did* read all the way to the end. A sheepish grin. "Yeah, Uli and I were trying to see whether there was enough plasm between the two of us to hold up a few matchboxes..."
Zh: “所以你*真的*读到了结尾。”一个羞怯的微笑。“是的，尤利和我想试试看，我们两个能不能产生出足够支撑起一些火柴盒的凝胶……”

En: Saying it out loud makes him realise how foolish it sounds.
Zh: 大声把这个念头说出来，让他听听这有多蠢……

En: It was just a little *informal* experiment. No reason to take it too seriously.
Zh: “这只是一个小小的*非正式*实验。别把它太当真。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: The young man looks at you a moment, then at his companion.
Zh: 年轻人看了你一眼，然后看向他的同伴……

En: Right. Basically an infra-materialist parlour game. Let's forget about it.
Zh: “没错。这差不多就是一个亚唯物主义者的客厅游戏。忘了它吧。”

En: He's not accustomed to exposing his doubt like this.
Zh: 他并不习惯于像这样袒露自己的疑虑。

En: What could it hurt? Alright, let's give it another go...
Zh: “失败了又能怎么样呢？来吧，我们再试一次。”

En: Before long, a modest tower begins to rise from the pile of matchboxes...
Zh: 不久，一座小塔就从成堆的火柴盒中立起。

En: You place every box with the utmost delicacy and precision.
Zh: 你带着无与伦比的仔细与精准放下每一个火柴盒……

En: Easy, Uli...
Zh: “小心，尤利……”

En: It's holding, it's holding...
Zh: “它稳住了，它稳住了……”

En: Damn it to hell!
Alright. You go next, Steban.
Zh: “好了。下一个你来，史蒂文……”

En: It's my fault, Steban. My hands were shaking... Nerves got the better of me.
Zh: “是我的错，史蒂文。我的手抖了……我太紧张……”

En: The young man pushes back his shirt sleeves, revealing the pale flesh of his forearms.
Zh: 年轻人挽起衬衫袖子，露出苍白的小臂。

En: Easy now...
Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Is it... it's holding... it's holding!
Zh: “它是不是……它稳住了……它稳住了！”

En: Aaagh! Shit!
Zh: “啊啊啊！干！”

En: Alright, gendarme, your turn.
Zh: “好了，警官，轮到你了……”

En: It's alright, Steban. That was still *very* close. One of our best performances yet.
Zh: “别难过，史蒂文。那已经*非常*接近了。是我们表现最好的一次了。”

En: You've got this. Theories aside, they're only matchboxes.
Zh: 你能做到的。别管理论怎么说，这些不过是一堆火柴盒而已。

En: Ghost of Mazov, give me strength... (Proceed.)
Zh: “马佐夫的英魂，给予我力量吧……”

En: Take a deep breath. (Proceed.)
Zh: 深呼吸。

En: It's... it's...
My god, is it really holding?
Zh: “老天啊，它是稳住了吗？”

En: It is, it is...
There, in the middle of the floor, the winding spire quivers with an improbable energy...
Zh: 就在地板中央，盘绕的螺旋带着不可思议的能量而震颤着。

En: Don't apologise. As I was saying, if it were *easy*, it wouldn't require so much plasm to work.
Zh: “不用道歉。如我之前所说，如果它很*简单*，就不需要那么多凝胶才能做到了。”

En: There's no way it should stay up like that. How is the weight being distributed?
Zh: 它绝不可能那样立起来。这是怎么分配重量的？

En: But it is! It is!
Zh: 但它就是做到了！它做到了！

En: For a long moment, no one says a word. Even the lieutenant is transfixed.
Zh: 房间里一片寂静，久久无人开口。连警督也陷入了震惊……

En: For a long moment, no one says a word. You're all three transfixed.
Zh: 房间里一片寂静，久久无人开口。你们三个呆若木鸡……

En: ... what do we do now?
Zh: “……接下来我们做什么？”

En: Shhh. This is the most amazing thing I've ever seen...
Zh: “嘘。这是我见过最神奇的造物了……”

En: A low pressure system is gathering itself over the bay. It's begun to tug violently at the tarpaulins...
Zh: 一个气旋正在海湾的上空形成。它开始激烈地撕扯防水油布……

En: ... and there it goes...
That was weird.
Zh: “这真古怪。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 一言不发。

En: My mums. The young man looks surprised. "She works in one of those skyscrapers, but it's not like you think... she's a cleaning lady..."
Zh: 年轻人看起来有些惊讶。“我的妈妈们。她在那种摩天大楼里工作，但不是你想的那种……她是个清洁工……”

En: Yes, in fact... my mums. The young man smiles sheepishly. "She works in La Delta... in one of those skyscrapers, but it's not like you think... she's a cleaning lady."
Zh: 年轻人羞怯地微笑起来。“是的，其实是……我的妈妈们。她在德尔塔工作，在一栋那样的摩天大楼里，但不是你想的那种……她是个清洁工。”

En: But how did you... oh, never mind. I guess I'm easier to read than I thought...
Zh: “但你是怎么……哦，没事。我还以为我的心思没那么容易看透呢。”

En: Why do you call her 'mums'?
Zh: “为什么你叫她‘妈妈们’？”

En: She's given up a lot for you.
Zh: “她为了你牺牲了很多。”

En: You want her to live in a better world.
Zh: “你希望她能生活在一个更好的世界里。”

En: Because I always have. Though I try not to do it too often now... it's kind of embarrassing, isn't it?
Zh: “因为我一直这么叫。但我现在尽量不这样了……这有点尴尬，不是吗？”

En: We all have to make sacrifices for the future. Maybe that's what I was trying to get at earlier...
Zh: “我们都得为未来作出牺牲。或许这就是我先前想要得出的结论……”

En: I want us *all* to live in a better world. But yes... she's a part of it... I guess we're all a part of it...
Zh: “我希望*所有人*都能生活在一个更好的世界里。但是……她自然也是其中一员……我想我们都是其中一员……”

En: You made him self-conscious now. He's practically paralysed.
Zh: 你指出了他忽视的念头。他宕机了。

En: Yes, that's a good way to think of it, broken, but not irreparable.
Zh: “是的，这么想不错，破碎，但并非无可救药。”

En: Yes, that's a good way to think of it... a work-in-progress.
Zh: “是的，这么想不错……正在建设中的事业。”

En: It's a nice thought.
Zh: “不错的想法。”

En: I don't know if I believe it, though.
Zh: “但我不知道是否能够相信。”

En: A thought can be a very powerful thing. That's the whole idea of infra-materialism...
Zh: “思想能够变成强有力的武器。这就是亚唯物主义的核心论点……”

En: You've got to believe in *something*. Otherwise, what are you doing?
Zh: “你总得相信*一些念头*。否则，你都在做什么呢？”

En: His words aren't really directed at you. He's wrestling with himself now...
Zh: 他并不是在针对你。他正在经历激烈的内心斗争……

En: Steban, it's getting pretty late.
Zh: “史蒂文，已经很晚了。”

En: You're right... We should clean this up and get going. He gestures to the matchboxes.
Zh: “你说得对……我们应该打扫房间然后回去了。”他指向火柴盒。

En: The young man appears lost inside his own thoughts...
Zh: 年轻人似乎沉浸在思绪之中……

En: A cold wind whips at the canvas. From far below, the sounds of waves smashing themselves against the rocks. You are exposed.
Zh: 寒风呼啸着吹过帆布。在遥远的下方，海浪凶猛地拍碎在岩石上。你感到自己暴露于自然中。

En: Pull your jacket tighter.
Zh: 拉紧夹克。

En: Rub your arms with your hands.
Zh: 用手掌揉搓上臂。

En: (Whisper.) "Are women bourgeois?"
Zh: （低语。）“女人都是资产阶级吗？”

En: Oh god, that's bad. Surely I can think of something better.
Zh: 哦老天，这真糟糕。我肯定能想出更好的。

En: The young man gives you a slightly confused look, as though he's sure you asked something else and he simply misheard.
Zh: 年轻人有些疑惑地看了你一眼，好像他以为自己一定是听错了。

En: No, he heard you crystal clear.
Zh: 不，他清清楚楚地听到了你的问题。

En: Somehow it sounded even *worse* out loud.
Zh: 不知怎么，大声说出来显得它更*糟糕*了。

En: You only said what everyone else was thinking. Look around -- do you see any women here?
Zh: 你只是说了所有人都在想的事情。看看你周围——这里有女人吗？

En: He wants to know if women are bourgeois, Steban...
Zh: “他想知道女人是否都是资产阶级，史蒂文……”

En: Yes, I heard him. Give me a moment. He takes a deep breath, then turns to you...
Zh: “我听到了。让我想想。”他深呼吸一口，然后转向你。

En: What makes you ask *that* question, above any other?
Zh: “为什么你在那么多问题当中，选择问*这个*问题？”

En: Because they can't handle me, I'm a goddamn superstar.\n
Zh: “因为她们无法掌控我，我可是个超级明星。”

En: Because they can't handle *me*.
Zh: “因为她们无法掌控*我*。”

En: Because I can't handle them. I'm a sorry excuse for a man.
Zh: “因为我无法掌控她们。我是男人的耻辱。”

En: Because I can't handle *them*.
Zh: “因为我无法掌控*她们*。”

En: Because they won't let me forget the life we used to have together.
Zh: “因为她们不让我忘却我们曾经的生活。”

En: I don't know! It's just what my brain told me to say.
Zh: “我不知道！只是我的脑子逼我这么说。”

En: Maybe your problem is that you think it's their job to *handle* you...
Zh: “或许你的问题其实是，你认为*掌控*你是她们的任务……”

En: Maybe your problem is that you think they're *yours* to handle...
Zh: “或许你的问题其实是，你认为*掌控*她们是你的任务……”

En: Ah, gendarme. Your problem isn't ideological, it's personal...
Zh: “啊，警官。你的问题不是意识形态问题，它完全是私人问题……”

En: That's because your brain is floating in ideology, gendarme. It's practically drowning in it...
Zh: “那是因为你的脑子漂浮在意识形态之中，警官。它简直要在里面淹死了……”

En: Is "Ideology" some kind of high-proof liquor? Because that's the only thing your brain is floating in.
Zh: “意识形态”是什么高档酒吗？你的脑子只会漂浮在那种东西里面。

En: You asked that question because you're still under the influence of ideology. That's natural. You're like a fish that's only now discovering that her whole life has been dictated by the movements of sea currents.
Zh: “你问出这个问题，是因为你仍在意识形态的影响之下。这很正常。你就像一条鱼，刚刚发现它的整个人生都被海潮的涌动所支配。”

En: That's what ideology is. It's like there are these invisible forces everywhere, pushing and tugging you this way and that, and you don't even know they're *there*.
Zh: “这就是意识形态。好像处处都是这种无形的力量，你被它们摆布，甚至意识不到它们的*存在*。”

En: What does this have to do with communism?
Zh: “这和康米主义又有什么关系？”

En: What does this have to do with women?
Zh: “这和女人又有什么关系？”

En: Is it even *possible* to imagine a world without ideology?
Zh: “有任何*可能*构想一个意识形态不存在的世界吗？”

En: Everything, man. That is part of the communist project, to destroy the ideological structures that reduce men and women to these hollow shells of identity.
Zh: “一切，老兄。这是康米主义计划的一部分，摧毁那将人们降格为空洞身份的意识形态结构。”

En: Of course it's possible. Just extremely difficult. You practically have to be a world-historical individual to do it.
Zh: “这当然是可行的。但是会极为困难。你得达到世界历史人物那样的地位才能做到它。”

En: Do you know the story of Dobreva and Abadanaiz?
Zh: “你知道多布列娃与阿巴达奈斯的故事吗？”

En: You mean the Revolutionary Lovers?
Zh: “你指那对革命伴侣？”

En: I thought that was just some gibberish you made up for the passphrase.
Zh: “我还以为那是你编出来当暗号的胡话。”

En: I don't know what you're talking about. Who were they?
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么。他们是谁？”

En: No. They were the Revolutionary Lovers, the Dual Commissars of Revolution in Insulinde.
Zh: “不。他们是革命伴侣，也是伊苏林迪大革命时代的政委二人组。”

En: The so-called 'Revolutionary Lovers'. They were the Dual Commissars of Revolution in Insulinde.
Zh: “人们常说的‘革命伴侣’。他们是伊苏林迪大革命时代的政委二人组。”

En: Not just lovers, they were the Dual Commissars of Revolution in Insulinde.
Zh: “不仅是伴侣，他们还是伊苏林迪大革命时代的政委二人组。”

En: It's too bad they wound up equally dead on some nameless island in Ozonne...
Zh: 不幸的是，他们最终双双殒命于欧佐纳尔的某个无名小岛……

En: What became of them, though? There seems to be a blank paragraph in the history books...
Zh: 不过，他们在那之前是什么人？历史书上似乎有一段空白……

En: It's from them we get the notion of dual-leadership, the *décomptage*, Revachol's truly great contribution to revolutionary thought. I believe it even persists in some form in the RCM, even to this day.
Zh: “是他们率先提出了双重领导体系，也即*倒装*这一概念。这是瑞瓦肖对于革命思想的杰出贡献。我相信直到今天，它依然以某种形式留存在RCM。”

En: It's too bad they died like all the rest of the communards.
Zh: “不幸的是，他们和其他的公社社员一样都死去了。”

En: What happened to them, Dobreva and Abadanaiz?
Zh: “多布列娃与阿巴达奈斯身上发生了什么？”

En: Is that true, Kim, about the décomptage?
Zh: “金，这个倒装的事情是真的吗？”

En: So... you're saying women are only bourgeois because of ideology?
Zh: “所以……你是说，女人只是因为意识形态而变成资产阶级？”

En: I need to take some time to think about all this... (Conclude.)
Zh: “我得花点时间思考一下……”（总结。）

En: They died on their own terms, like real revolutionaries. There's a lot to say for that.
Zh: “他们死得其所。每个真正的革命者都会如此。这件事说来话长。”

En: He sighs. "There's more to it than that. People still make their own choices. It's a matter of being aware what's influencing them."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“比那更复杂。人们依然能够自主作出决定。重点在于他们是否意识到自己受到的影响。”

En: The young man nods but says nothing. He appears to be distracted by something...
Zh: 年轻人点点头，但一言不发。他似乎有些分神……

En: I had no idea its origins went all the way back to the Revolution itself. But it makes sense, in a way...
Zh: “我不知道它能一路追溯到大革命本身。但这样说来倒是有道理……”

En: He's wrestling with some question of his own.
Zh: 他似乎正在思索什么问题。

En: He's lost in his own thoughts. You wonder what silt you've stirred in the bottom of his soul.
Zh: 他沉浸于思绪中。你搅动了他灵魂深处的泥沙，但不知那是什么样的。

En: Maybe in another life, but not this one. Face it, this is *the* question that's been simmering inside you all along.
Zh: 或许来世可以，但今生不行。面对它。这就是*那个*长久以来在你内心翻滚不休的问题。

En: No-no-no! This was supposed to take your mind *off* the other thing!
Zh: 不-不-不！这本是为了让你转移一下注意力的！

En: Hang on, I thought I was really getting into the theory back there!
Zh: 坚持住，我想我已经接近理论的核心了！

En: Maybe I should keep this one to myself.
Zh: 也许我应该缄口不言。

En: Sure, you spent some time getting lost in your theoretical labyrinths, but *this* has been the beast at the centre this whole time...
Zh: 当然，你曾在理论的迷宫中徘徊了一段时间，但*这*始终都是迷宫中心盘踞的那头凶兽……

En: It's too late. You've already opened your mouth to speak...
Zh: 来不及了。你已经开口说道……

En: That's enough for tonight. Will you still be here if I have more questions? [Leave.]
Zh: “今晚聊得够多了。如果我还有别的问题，你们还会在这里吗？”[离开。]

En: Well, I guess we're off. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，那我们该走了。”[离开。]

En: Well, I guess I'm off. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，那我该走了。”[离开。]

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Sure, we're here most every night. He shrugs. "Maybe we'll catch you again."
Zh: 年轻人耸耸肩。“没事，我们几乎每个晚上都会在这里的。你想来可以再来。”

En: Sleep well, gendarme.
Zh: “好梦，警官。”

En: Wait a minute, if you don't mind...
Zh: “等等，如果你不介意的话……”

En: We wanted to get your opinion on something: a few little changes we we've been thinking about.
Zh: “我们想听听你的意见：我们最近在考虑的一个小改动。”

En: What kind of changes?
Zh: “什么改动？”

En: Nothing *too major*, I think. We were talking, potentially, about relaxing some parts of our admissions process...
Zh: “不是什么*重大*改动，我想。我们在考虑，或许，可以放宽一点招新标准……”

En: Hmm. It probably *would* help if you didn't make people go through a whole interview just to join.
Zh: “嗯……如果你们不弄出这么一整套面试来考核新生，或许*是*会有点帮助……”

En: Maybe you should ditch the passphrases and meet in a coffeehouse.
Zh: “也许你们应该在咖啡厅里集会，别用那个暗号了。”

En: Have you considered changing up the reading list?
Zh: “你想过更改阅读材料吗？”

En: No, you should keep it the way things are.
Zh: “不，你们应该继续这样下去。”

En: That's interesting. I thought people *loved* group interviews.
Zh: “有趣。我还以为大家*热爱*群面呢……”

En: Hmm. I thought the *cloak-and-dagger routine* made us seem more appealing.
Zh: “嗯……我还以为*秘密行动*让我们更有吸引力了呢……”

En: I suppose we have read through most of the infra-materialist canon by this point.
Zh: “我想目前为止，我们已经读完了亚唯物主义的大部分经典著作……”

En: Exactly what I've been saying. On fundamental matters there can be no compromise!
Zh: “正是我想说的。根本问题不容退让！”

En: I thought so, too, but perhaps we overdid it, just a little...
Zh: “我也这么想，但可能我们做得有点过头了，就一点点……”

En: He sighs. "You're right. It probably wouldn't *kill* us to read more conventional historical materialism."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“你说得对。也许传统的历史唯物主义论著并不会*干掉*我们。”

En: I'm not saying we abandon our beliefs. I just think it would be better if our group had more than *two* regular attendees.
Zh: “我不是说我们要抛弃信念，只是觉得如果我们的小组能有*两个*以上的常驻成员，或许会更好一些。”

En: As long as we maintain the appropriate critical distance.
Zh: “只要我们保持合适的批判距离。”

En: Good point. Two's not really a 'group,' is it?
Zh: “好主意。两个人其实并不能算一个‘小组’，对吧？”

En: Nah, two's the best number. Three's when they start cramping your style.
Zh: “我看不是，二是最棒的数字。等你数到三，多出来的那个就会开始束缚你的风格啦。”

En: Plus, all these empty chairs are a little sad, aren't they?
Zh: “再说，这些空椅子看着有点悲伤，不是吗？”

En: Precisely my point. It's about quality, not quantity.
Zh: “一点不错。贵精不贵多。”

En: There was another thing: We were also debating putting up some posters around town.
Zh: “还有一件事：我们也在讨论，要不要在镇上张贴一些海报。”

En: Though *some of us* maintain that advertising is an unacceptably bourgeois tactic.
Zh: “不过*我们中的某些人*坚称打广告是一种恶臭的资产阶级策略。”

En: That's what makes it so beautiful. The irony is unbeatable.
Zh: 这才漂亮呢，讽刺意味是最好的。

En: Ahem. As a noted art cop you definitely have an opinion on this.
Zh: 啊哼。作为闻名遐迩的艺术警探，你必得对此发表一番见解。

En: As someone who's dabbled in murals himself, I support this plan.
Zh: “我对壁画略有涉猎，因此我支持这个计划。”

En: As something of an art critic myself, I support this poster plan.
Zh: “作为一名艺术评论家，我支持这个海报计划。”

En: Think of it this way: you're appropriating bourgeois methods for revolutionary ends.
Zh: “你要这么想：你在为了革命的目的利用资产阶级的手段。”

En: Sounds like something Cindy could help with...
Zh: “听起来辛迪能帮上忙……”

En: Posters are great. Who doesn't like posters?
Zh: “海报很棒，谁不喜欢海报？”

En: Yeah, posters *are* pretty bourgeois.
Zh: “是的，海报*确实*非常资产阶级。”

En: Glad to hear it. I was thinking we might be able to get Cindy to design the posters...
Zh: “很高兴听到你这么说。我正在想我们能不能请辛迪来设计海报呢……”

En: Ah, it makes sense. There's already a sub-theme of radical street art running through our reading group...
Zh: “啊，有道理。我们的阅读小组本来就有一层激进街头艺术的底色……”

En: Oh, I *like* that. We're dismantling the structures of capital with their own tools...
Zh: “哦，我*喜欢*。我们在用资产阶级的工具摧毁他们……”

En: That's *exactly* what I was thinking...
Zh: “我*就是*这么想的……”

En: Exactly! I was thinking we could print them in the old communard style, with huge slogans...
Zh: “一点不错！我正在想我们可以把海报做成过以前的公社风格，放上巨大的标语……”

En: See? Even the gendarme agrees.
Zh: “你瞧？连警官都同意我的意见。”

En: I suppose that would open us up to charges of hypocrisy... not to mention it might make us *look pretty desperate* for new members...
Zh: “我想这可能会让人指责我们虚伪……更别提它会显得我们*急不可耐*地渴求新成员……”

En: When nothing could be further from the truth.
Zh: “现实本来也差不多。”

En: Right. We'll have to table this question until we've considered the potential downstream effects.
Zh: “是的。我们得继续讨论这个问题，直到我们考虑完所有潜在的下游效应。”

En: ... and just like that, their ironic defences are back up.
Zh: ……如此，他们又构筑起了讽刺的防线。

En: Plus, I've got the *perfect* place in mind...
Zh: “再说，我脑海里已经有了*完美的*地点……”

En: Put some more coffee on, Uli. We've got a long night ahead of us. We should probably get Cindy in here too...
Zh: “再喝点咖啡，尤利。我们还有一整晚呢。或许应该把辛迪也请来……”

En: In any case, we've kept you long enough. Goodnight to you both.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我们已经占用你们很多时间了。晚安。”

En: In any case, we've kept you long enough. Goodnight to you.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我们已经占用你很多时间了。晚安。”

En: Oh! And, gendarme. One last thing...
Zh: “哦，还有，警官。最后一件事……”

En: I've been thinking about what you asked before, about women, you know...
Zh: “我刚刚一直在想你先前的问题，和女人有关的那个，你知道的……”

En: About that question you asked earlier. It reminded me of a certain poem that you might appreciate...
Zh: “你先前问过的那个问题。它让我想起一首你或许会欣赏的诗……”

En: Not that it's any of my business, but it sounds like this question is *really* tearing you up inside.
Zh: “虽然这不是我的事，但我能看出来这个问题*真的*令你备受折磨。”

En: So, good luck with that, I guess.
Zh: “那么，祝你好运，我想。”

En: 'In dark times, should the stars also go out?'
Zh: “在黑暗的时代，群星/是否也应熄灭？”

En: Anyway, goodnight to you.
Zh: “不管怎么说，晚安。”

En: Ah, so he *has* read something besides his books of abstruse theory!
Zh: 啊，所以说除了那些深奥理论，他确实还*读过*别的书！

En: Even when they laugh at us?
Zh: “即使当他们将我们视为笑谈？”

En: Even then.
Zh: “即使在那个时候。”

En: Mazov tells us to laugh at the monster that hides behind false hopes and pieties.
Zh: “马佐夫告诉我们，应当将那些藏在虚假的希望与伪神之后的怪物视为笑谈。”

En: Precisely. It's only beneath the cold light of scorn that deep truths are revealed...
Zh: “说得很对。只有在讥嘲的冷光之下，深层的真相才会被揭露出来……”

En: A gang of cheap psychologists and intellectual midgets. His companion sneers. "Typical Gottwalders, in other words."
Zh: “一群拙劣的心理学家和蠢蛋。”他的同伴嗤笑一声。“换句话说，典型的戈特伍德派。”

En: ... and next thing you know there are *budget cuts*, so now we've got to cut the bean rations in half *again*...
Zh: “……然后就来了*预算下调*，所以我们*又*得把豆子的配给减半……”

En: Comrade Ulixes is right. The amount of plasm required to strike down an ideological enemy outright is enormous. We're talking second-level effects, at a minimum.
Zh: “尤利西斯同志说得对。一击打倒意识形态敌人，需要数量庞大的凝胶。至少得是第二级的效用。”

En: Or even *third*.
Zh: “甚至*第三级*。”

En: Nothing, I just thought there would be more...
Zh: “没什么，我只是以为还有更多的……”

En: More what?
Zh: “更多的什么？”

En: I don't know. More... something?
Zh: “我不知道。更多的……某种东西？”

En: It seems like there's something on your mind. Maybe a question you mean to ask?  The young man turns to his companion, then back to you.
Zh: 年轻人转向同伴，然后转回到你。“看来你心中有事。或许你有问题想问？”

En: Hit him as hard as you can.
Zh: 全力打他。

En: Fell before the Cuno.
Zh: 在坤诺面前摔倒。

En: Squeezed by Measurehead.
Zh: 被测颅先生打倒。

En: Shut down Cuno.
Zh: 打倒坤诺。

En: Beat Measurehead.
Zh: 打倒测颅先生。

En: Punchy while drinking.
Zh: 打醉拳。

En: Know RCM technique.
Zh: 知道RCM技巧。

En: Wait, who are these *infra-materialists*?
Zh: “等等，这些*亚唯物主义者*是谁？”

En: I know who Kras Mazov is, but who is this 'Ignus Nilsen' guy?
Zh: “我知道卡拉斯·马佐夫，但这个叫‘伊格纳斯·尼尔森’的家伙又是谁？”

En: You're not familiar with them? It's... pretty advanced stuff. You may not be ready for it yet, gendarme. The two young men exchange skeptical side-glances.
Zh: “你不熟悉他们？那是……很高深的学问。或许你还没准备好接受它们。”两个年轻人交换了一个怀疑的眼神。

En: Okay, but then who's this 'Ignus Nilsen' guy?
Zh: “好吧，那这个叫‘伊格纳斯·尼尔森’的家伙是谁？”

En: Only Kras Mazov's most trusted lieutenant, the Evangelist of the Revolution, and the founding father of the People's Republic of Samara.
Zh: “他只不过是卡拉斯·马佐夫最信任的副手，革命福音的传教士，以及萨马拉共和国的奠基人。

En: It's hard to overstate how unimpressed he is that you've never heard of this world-historical individual.
Zh: 你竟从未听说过这位世界历史人物，他对此简直不能更失望了。

En: No, wait! Can this really be the end? You feel like you've just gotten to the real stuff.
Zh: 不，等等！真的就这么结束了吗？你感觉你才刚刚接近真正的知识。

En: It's embarrassing, but at that point my political consciousness was still pretty rudimentary.
Zh: “我知道这很尴尬，现在我的政治觉悟还很粗浅。”

En: Perhaps some vestigial sexual objectification from your pre-revolutionary consciousness. He nods.
Zh: 他点点头。“或许你的前-革命觉悟造成了一些退化的性别物化思想。”

En: No, it was an artifact of your pre-ideological consciousness.
Zh: 不，这是你的前-意识形态觉悟的造物。

En: It happens to the best of us.
Zh: “即使是最伟大的革命家也有可能经历这些。”

En: ... even the lieutenant seems engrossed, despite your revealing details of an RCM investigation...
Zh: ……即使是警督也听得入了神，没注意到你泄露了RCM调查的信息……

En: ... even the lieutenant seems engrossed, despite your revealing details of an RCM investigation...
Zh: ……即使是警督也听得入了神，没注意到你泄露了RCM调查的信息……

En: There's something going on in there, but his innermost sanctum is still beyond your reach...
Zh: 他似乎在想着什么，但他内心深处的圣所依然对你紧闭着……

En: Wait, you're saying communism is some kind of religion?
Zh: “等等，你是说康米主义是某种宗教？”

En: But what if you don't believe there *is* a future?
Zh: “但要是你不相信*会有*什么未来呢？”

En: But what if humanity keeps letting us down?
Zh: “但要是人性反反复复地令你失望呢？”

En: Has it gotten cold in here? Your arms seem to be covered in gooseflesh.
Zh: 这里变凉了吗？你的胳膊上似乎起了鸡皮疙瘩。

En: Only in this very specific sense. Communism doesn't dangle any promises of eternal bliss or reward. The only promise it offers is that the future can be better than the past, if we're willing to work and fight and die for it.
Zh: “仅在这个狭义的范畴内。康米主义并不鼓吹什么永恒的极乐与恩典。它只能保证，如果我们愿意为之奋斗和牺牲，那么未来会比过去更好。”

En: Then you don't believe in the historic role of the proletariat, which also means you reject the basis of historical materialism.
Zh: “那你并不相信无产阶级的历史使命了。也就是说，你反对历史唯物主义的理论基础。”

En: Nobody said fulfilling the proletariat's historic role would be easy. A tight smile. "It demands great faith with no promise of tangible reward. But that doesn't mean we can simply give up."
Zh: 一个小小的微笑。“没人说让无产阶级实现历史使命是一件容易的事。它需要你不追求物质利益，为崇高的信念而奋斗。但这不意味着我们就应该放弃。”

En: It means you're not a communist, in other words.
Zh: “换句话说，这意味着你不是康米主义者。”

En: Even when they ignore us?
Zh: “即使当他们无视我们？”

En: Even when they shoot at us?
Zh: “即使当他们冲我们开枪？”

En: Even then.
Zh: “即使在那个时候。”

En: Especially then.
Zh: “尤其在那个时候。”

En: And of course, we'll be shooting right back.
Zh: “而且，我们当然会开枪打回去。”

En: Mazov says it's the arrogance of capital that will be its ultimate undoing. It does not believe it can fail, which is why it must fail.
Zh: “马佐夫说了，资本的傲慢将会为它自己敲响丧钟。它不相信自己会失败，这就是为什么它必将失败。”

En: Yes. Whereas we understand that we'll almost certainly fail, which is what allows us the chance to succeed...
Zh: “是的。我们知道我们几乎一定会失败，但也因此我们能够看到一线胜利的曙光……”

En: Why do you see the two of them with their backs against a bullet-pocked wall, all of a sudden?
Zh: 为什么你好像突然看到他们两个站在一堵布满弹孔的墙的前面？

En: So young. So *unbearably* young...
Zh: 如此年轻。年轻得让人*无法忍受*……

En: I have to say, I've never *entirely* understood what they mean, but I think maybe the answer is in there, somewhere.
Zh: “我得说，我*从未*真正理解过那是什么意思，但我想或许答案就在某个地方。”

En: What are you then?
Zh: “那么，你又是什么呢？”

En: Okay, but what if you *know* there isn't going to be a future?
Zh: “好吧，但要是你*知道*不会有什么未来呢？”

En: Something else... The young man shakes his head.
Zh: “别的什么东西……”年轻人摇摇头。

En: I'm not sure I get what you're driving at. Of course there will be a future, even if the form it takes might appear unrecognisable. That's the spiral of progress.
Zh: “我不确定你想问什么。未来当然存在，即使是以一种无法辨认的方式。这就是历史的螺旋发展……”

En: Julia Dobreva came from Graad. Jean Abadanaiz was born in Revachol. She was brilliant, charismatic, a revolutionary comet. He was her partner and stabiliser, the planet who kept her in orbit.
Zh: “茱莉亚·多布列娃来自格拉德，而让·阿巴达奈斯则是瑞瓦肖人。她光彩照人、惊才绝艳，是一颗革命的彗星。他是她的伴侣与支点，是防止她迷失于茫茫宇宙的那颗星球。”

En: I guess you could say we believe it *because it's impossible*. He looks at the scattered matchboxes on the ground. "It's our way of saying we refuse to accept that the world has to remain... like this..."
Zh: 他看向地板上散落的火柴盒。“我想你可以说，我们相信它*是因为它不可能*。我们只是借此表达，我们拒绝接受这个世界只能……像这样下去…… ”

En: Somehow you only now realise that the temperature has been dropping steadily for the last half hour.
Zh: 不知怎么，你现在才意识到，房间里的温度在过去的半个小时内稳定地下降了。

En: Their faces, blurred yet frozen as though in ambrotype. You were never *that young*, were you?
Zh: 他们的面孔模糊而沉静，如同玻璃干板相片冻结的影像。你从未*那样年轻过*，不是吗？

En: We should try again, all of us.
Zh: “我们应该再试一次，我们一起。”

En: The charge in the air has changed. You allow the moment to pass.
Zh: 空气中积蓄起了电荷。你让这一刻过去。

En: I'll sit this one out. I don't think you want my *skeptical materialism* interfering...
Zh: “我看你们玩吧。我不觉得你们会需要我的*怀疑唯物主义*的干扰……”

En: It's fine, Uli. That's enough parlour games for one night. We should clean up.
Zh: “没关系，尤利。今晚也玩了够多的客厅游戏。我们该打扫房间了……”

En: Undoubtedly.
Zh: “确实如此。”

En: The lieutenant says nothing, but a bemused grin crosses his face.
Zh: 警督什么也没说，但一抹愉悦的笑容划过他的面颊。

En: The lieutenant says nothing, but his expression suggests otherwise.
Zh: 警督什么也没说，但他的表情似乎蕴含着什么想法。

En: Even the lieutenant is watching intently now.
Zh: 即使警督也全神贯注了。

En: This is tall enough, better not to risk it.
Zh: “已经很高了，最好还是别冒风险……”

En: Come on, gendarme. Real progress requires great risks.
Zh: “别这样，警官。只有甘冒风险的人才能真正前进。”

En: Yes. This is the closest we've ever gotten.
Zh: “是的。这已经是我们最接近目标的一次了……”

En: What are you talking about, detective? You can't give up *now*.
Zh: “你在说什么，警探？你不能*就这样*放弃。”

En: Either way, that's enough parlour games for one night. Thanks for humouring us.
Zh: “不管怎么说，今晚也玩了够多的客厅游戏。谢谢你给我们带来了欢乐。”

En: Shit.
Zh: “该死。”

En: Fuck.
I'm sorry.
Zh: “我很抱歉。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 一言不发。

En: Well, it may be that we've found the outermost limit of our capabilities under the prevailing regime.
Zh: “好吧，或许我们只是找到了在当前政权下我们的能力极限……”

En: Alternatively, it could be that our own ideological fervour is still *insufficient*.
Zh: “或者，也可能是因为我们的意识形态激情依然*不够*。”

En: Harder to believe, but still possible...
Zh: “这很难相信，但有可能是真的……”

En: For a second there I thought we had it. Just goes to show how constrained we are under the limits of the prevailing regime.
Zh: “我刚才差点以为我们要成功了。这体现了我们在当前政权下受到了多少束缚。”

En: Yes, the problem is almost certainly your *lack of commitment*, and not, say, gravity.
Zh: “是的是的，你们失败的原因显然是*缺乏奉献精神*，而不是，比如说，重力。”

En: This is impossible, right? There's no way it's really staying up like that...
Zh: “这是不可能的，对吧？它无法真正地像那样伫立……”

En: That was miraculous.
Zh: “这是个奇迹。”

En: No. He looks up. "It was exactly what's *supposed* to happen. We should treat it that way."
Zh: “不。”他抬起头。“这正是*应该发生*的事态。我们应该这样想……”

En: That was remarkable.
Zh: “这是个令人赞叹的成就。”

En: Yes, that's probably the best way to describe it.
Zh: “没错，或许正应该这样形容它。”

En: The more it hurts, the more you have to have it.
Zh: 越是疼痛，越是不能放弃。

En: In another life, you might've made a fine seafaring man. Who knows, you may become one yet.
Zh: 或许在另一个世界，你会成为一个优秀的水手。谁知道呢，或许在这里你也可以试试。

En: The beast is *you*, a mass of teeth and claws and violent urges.
Zh: 野兽就是*你*，一团凌乱的尖牙利爪与暴力冲动。

En: Matchboxes, it appears.
Zh: 看起来是火柴盒。

En: Yeah, I'm sure you did. That's real Saramirizian twill. Only old Saramirizian communists and drug smugglers wear those anymore...
Zh: “你觉得自己有可信度吗。那可是真正的萨拉米尔泽斜纹布。现在只有老萨拉米尔泽康米主义者和毒贩还穿这种衣服了……”

En: We think of ourselves as more of an intellectual vanguard.
Zh: “我们更愿意将自己视作学术先锋。”

En: What about Cindy, is she part of the group?
Zh: “那辛迪呢，她也是你们小组的一员吗？”

En: Cindy is... how to describe her *role*...
Zh: “辛迪是……该怎么说她的*身份*呢……”

En: Yes, that's exactly how I would put it. And naturally we support her radical counter-liberal aesthetics.
Zh: “是的，我正打算这么说。以及我们天然地支持她的激进反自由主义美学。”

En: But she refuses to submit an essay, so we can't call her a member of the group *per se*.
Zh: “但她拒绝提交论文，所以我们还不能把她算作*真正的*小组成员。”

En: That doesn't stop her from using the room for studio space, of course.
Zh: “不过，她当然还是可以用这个房间来学习的。”

En: In other words, they have lost faith in their own relevance.
Zh: 换句话说，他们已经对自己的同伴都缺乏信心。

En: One-thousand percent, yes.
Zh: 百分之一千，是的。

En: No, of course not. That's reductive in the extreme.
Zh: 不，当然不是。这顶多起到一点反作用。

En: Of course, the matchboxes! You'd very nearly forgotten to ask about them. Now may be your last chance...
Zh: 当然，火柴盒！你差点忘了问他们。现在可能是你最后的机会了……

En: ... but I've done some reflecting and these days I consider myself a feminist.
Zh: “……但这几天我反思了一番，现在我认为自己是一个女权主义者。”

En: That's a fascinating trajectory, for sure. It's good to hear that you can still grow ideologically even at an... um... advanced age.
Zh: “这确实是一条有趣的道路。很高兴听到你在这样……呃……成熟的年纪，还能继续意识形态的成长。”

En: Yes, a fascinating case study...
Zh: “是的，一个有趣的案例……”

En: They really have no idea how old you are, much less what it means to age.
Zh: 他们是真的对你的年纪没有概念，也不懂年岁增长究竟意味着什么。

En: Interesting. So you're saying that in a certain sense the *game plays the players* as much as they play the game?
Zh: “有意思。所以你认为，在某种程度上可以说是*游戏玩玩家*，而不仅仅是玩家玩游戏？”

En: Hmm. Then what does that make the *game designers*?
Zh: “嗯……如果是这样的话，那么*游戏设计师*又是什么人呢？”

En: Superstars.
Zh: “超级明星。”

En: Malevolent deities.
Zh: “邪恶的神明。”

En: Just people working a job, like any other.
Zh: “只是普通人，和其他人一样做着一份工作。”

En: The irradiated waste tokens have this great metallic ochre sheen.
Zh: “受到辐照的废奖券，带着这样惑人的赭色金属光泽。”

En: This is your chance to show off your own critical faculties. Show them what an *introspective* cop looks like!
Zh: 是时候向他们露一手学术批判能力了。让他们看看什么叫*反思*条子！

En: ... something of an ideological auxiliary, perhaps.
Zh: “……或许是某种意识形态的辅助体系。”

En: Maybe you just haven't found the right group yet?
Zh: “可能你只是还没找到对的小组？”

En: Novels, unbelievable. (Shake your head.)
Zh: “长篇小说，不可置信。”（摇摇头。）

En: See? Even a gendarme gets it.
Zh: “你瞧？连警官都明白。”

En: I like a good novel every now and then.
Zh: “我有时会读一两本长篇小说。”

En: Oh, sure. But you can always join a *book club* if that's what you're into.
Zh: “哦，当然。但如果你想加入一家*读书俱乐部*，那你随时都能加入……”

En: A note of real uncertainty in his voice.
Zh: 他的声音中充满了疑虑……

En: I want you... to hit me.
Zh: “我想让你……打我。”

En: Yes, this worked out quite nicely. He seems immensely pleased with himself.
Zh: “是的，它运作得很好。”他看来十分自得。

En: It seems the face of violent reaction has revealed itself...
Zh: “反动势力的暴力面似乎自己暴露出来了……”

En: The young man sighs. His companion looks about furtively.
Zh: 年轻人叹了口气。他的同伴无聊地四下张望。

En: We should probably clean up...
Zh: “我们或许应该打扫房间了……”

En: Hmmm, it *does* sound pretty cool when you describe it like that.
Zh: “嗯……你这么一说，好像听起来*确实*很酷了。”

En: I suppose that makes it a historical inevitability, beyond contradiction.
Zh: “我想这确实让它成为了一种超越了矛盾的历史必然性。”

En: Hmmm, I guess no one could accuse Cindy of having a *bourgeois aesthetic*.
Zh: “嗯……我觉得没人能指责辛迪有什么*资产阶级审美*。”

En: Effects like... having more people in their reading group?
Zh: 什么效用……让他们的阅读小组招到更多人？

En: It was written by a young communard who was killed on the barricades during the Coalition landings. The story goes that he wrote it on the last night of his life, keeping watch from the barricades in the middle of the night. I don't have the whole thing committed to memory, but there's a line in it I think about sometimes.
Zh: “作者是一名年轻的公社社员，他牺牲于联盟军登陆的那场战斗。他在生命的最后一夜凝望着防线写下它。我不记得整首诗了，但其中有一行我时常想起。”

En: What's the line?
Zh: “是哪一行？”

En: You're holding it together, *somehow*, despite everything...
Zh: *不知怎么*，你在对其他事情一无所知的情况下，感到自己开始推导出故事全貌了……

En: It *is* tearing you up. It's like there's a tiny hateful beast shredding your very soul...
Zh: 它*确实*令你备受折磨。好像有一只充满恨意的小野兽，正在撕咬你的整个灵魂……

En: Take a deep breath. You've done some reading these past few days. But you also know there's more to life than what you find in books...
Zh: 深呼吸。这几天你读了一些书。但你知道生命的意义不仅在于书本……

En: Give 'em sex and drugs! They're clearly not getting any on their own.
Zh: 给他们性和毒品！他们显然自己搞不到……

En: How about a tale of adventure, intrigue, and derring-do?
Zh: 一个冒险、阴谋、闯关故事如何？

En: A real-life procedural, complete with gory details...
Zh: 一个写实的刑侦故事，字里行间充斥着血腥的细节描写……

En: (Point to the art supplies.) "I'm guessing all this art stuff belongs to Cindy?"
Zh: （指向绘画工具。）“我猜这些艺术的东西都是辛迪的？”

En: (Point to the art supplies.) "I'm guessing these pots of gouache belong to Cindy?"
Zh: （指向绘画工具。）“我猜这几罐水粉颜料都是辛迪的？”

En: (Point to the art supplies.) "Gouache, huh? Is that the medium of the proletariat?"
Zh: （指向绘画工具。）“水粉颜料哈？那就是无产阶级的绘画材质吗？”

En: (Point to the art supplies.) "What's with the paint stuff?"
Zh: （指向绘画工具。）“这些油漆都是什么东西？”

En: He sighs. "Yeah, it's hers. She just sort of... moved it all in a few months ago."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“是她的。她只是……几个月前把这些陆续搬进来了。”

En: He sighs. "That's Cindy's stuff. She just sort of... moved it all in a few months ago."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“是辛迪的东西。她只是……几个月前把这些陆续搬进来了。”

En: Do you like her art?
Zh: “你喜欢她的艺术吗？”

En: You want me to kick her out for you? I've served an eviction or two recently.
Zh: “你们想让我帮忙把她踢出去吗？我最近刚驱逐了几个人。”

En: Now I'm wondering, what's the deal with this place?
Zh: “我现在就好奇，这个地方有什么特殊的性质吗？”

En: Oh, sure. It's definitely *interesting*, I would say...
Zh: “哦，当然。我得说，那很*有趣*……”

En: She said if she's going to make truly radical art, she needs a suitably radical workspace.
Zh: “她说她要创造出真正激进的艺术，因此她需要一个激进的工作场所。”

En: And I don't think she could afford rent at an actual studio.
Zh: “但我觉得她租不起一个真正的工作室。”

En: Hmmm, I guess you could call her latest stuff a sort of *counter-bourgeois calligraphy*. She's got a real taste for radical slogans.
Zh: “嗯……我想你可以把她最近的作品归类于某种*反-资产阶级书法*。她对激进标语颇有心得。”

En: It's too bad she hasn't developed the theoretical foundation to do truly radical work.
Zh: “真遗憾她还未建立起真正激进作品的理论基础。”

En: I'm not sure it would do very much good. She's sort of... impervious to authority.
Zh: “我不知道这样有什么用处。她对权威有点……油盐不进。”

En: It's true. And let's not bring up that shameful oinking episode.
Zh: 确实如此。更别说那次可耻的哼哼哧哧的发作了。

En: That's only because you haven't *really* laid down the law with her yet.
Zh: 那只是因为你还没*真正*地在她心里建立起法律的权威。

En: Seems unlikely you'll be able to 'lay down the law' with a child who made you oink like a pig...
Zh: 你不太可能在一个让你猪叫起来的小鬼心里’建立起法律的权威‘……

En: Let's never bring up that shameful episode again.
Zh: 别再提起那次可耻的发作了。

En: Besides, it would look pretty bad if people thought we were collaborating with the gendarmerie.
Zh: “再说，要是人们觉得我们在和警察合作，那对我们的形象不好。”

En: Wait, I thought 'interesting' was what people said when they don't have anything real to add?
Zh: “等等，我以为‘有趣’是在人们无话可说的时候用的套话？”

En: His companion's eyes widen with interest. He has a cold smile.
Zh: 他的同伴感兴趣地睁大了双眼。他露出一抹冰冷的微笑。

En: Okay, let's move on.
Zh: “好吧，我们换个话题。”

En: It's like you sucker-punched him *with your words*.
Zh: 好像你*用言语*击倒了他。

En: If this is what the Union man meant by theory combat, you've just scored a decisive blow.
Zh: 如果这就是工会的人所说的理论搏斗，你刚刚打出了致胜一击。

En: Come on. It's not that I don't *have* anything to say about Cindy's art. It's more that I'm... still working out the details. It's a complex question, you've got to take your time with it...
Zh: “别这样。我不是对辛迪的艺术*无话可说*。我只是……还在研究细节。你得多花些时间……”

En: 'The deal'? At a fundamental level I guess you could call it the shattered bones of a dream crushed by capital.
Zh: “‘特殊’？用最简单的话说，这个地方是一个被资本所摧毁的旧梦的残骸。”

En: No doubt.
Zh: “毫无疑问。”

En: 'Interesting' -- isn't that just binoclard for 'I don't have a real opinion'?
Zh: “‘有趣’——这不就是‘我不知道自己想说什么’的意思吗？”

En: I think she'll get there, though. She's still looking for a subject equal to her ambitions.
Zh: “不过，我觉得她会实现梦想的。她还在寻找能配得上她的野心的题材。”

En: That's really good, Steban. You should save that for an essay.
Zh: “它很好，史蒂文。你应该用它写一篇论文。”

En: A moment of silence. They're waiting for you to speak.
Zh: 房间里安静了一刻。他们都在等你开口。

En: Good point, Uli.
Zh: “你说得对，尤利。”

En: Thanks, Uli. When the idea is sound the words just sort of flow.
Zh: “谢谢，尤利。点子对了，遣词造句只是顺其自然的事情。”

En: If this place is the shattered bones, that must make us the bone weevils.
Zh: “如果这个地方只是一片残骸，那我们就都是一群骨头象鼻虫了。”

En: Okay, but what *is* this place?
Zh: “好吧但这个地方*到底*是什么？”

En: Yes, now keep developing the idea.
Zh: 没错，现在继续深入思考这个点子。

En: Mmm, yeah that's not bad.
Zh: “嗯……是的，那还不赖。”

En: Not as good as Steban's original idea, though.
Zh: “不过没有史蒂文一开始的想法那么好。”

En: A feeble and hopelessly mixed metaphor.
Zh: 一个虚弱无望的复杂隐喻。

En: Actually, I think that's a mixed metaphor.
Zh: “其实，我觉得那是个复杂的隐喻。”

En: Hmph. Leave it to the gendarme to enforce bourgeois stylistic values.
Zh: “哼。还是让警官来为资产阶级的风格添砖加瓦吧。”

En: Besides, it's just a first draft.
Zh: “再说，这只是初稿。”

En: It's an old construction site. He looks around. "They've been trying to restore these apartments for years, but as you can see they haven't made much progress."
Zh: 年轻人环顾四周。“这里以前是个施工地点。他们早就想修复这些公寓，但如你所见，并没有完成多少进度。”

En: I heard these used to be luxury apartments. Million reál views, that sort of thing.
Zh: “我听说这里曾经是豪华公寓。几百万雷亚尔的那种。”

En: That could be. He nods. "It would explain some of the more ornate detailing. But we're speaking in world-historical terms here. What this place *represents*, not what it merely *was*."
Zh: 他点头。“有可能。这就能解释某些精致的装饰纹样了。但我们在用世界历史的角度看待问题。重要的这个地方*代表*了什么，不是它曾经*是*什么。”

En: His friend yawns, evidently bored by literal reality.
Zh: 他的朋友打起了哈欠，显然对现实感到无聊。

En: (Let it go.)
Zh: (随它去吧。)

En: The way they billow in the night breeze reminds you of something...
Zh: 它们在晚风中翻滚的样子让你想起了什么……

En: It reminds you of something, but you can't think of what. Oh well, let's see if the students are still going...
Zh: 这让你想起了什么，但你记不清楚。好吧，还是看看这些学生接下来要做什么……

En: You've flustered the poor boy. Now he's *got* to say something.
Zh: 你让这个可怜的男孩慌张起来。现在他*得*说点什么了。

En: For the moment.
Zh: 只是现在……

En: The higher the tower goes, the quieter the room seems to become. Aside from the occasional comment, the three of you are completely absorbed by the task.
Zh: 塔建得越高，房间里变得越安静。除了偶尔的几句评论，你们三个全神贯注地投入了任务……

En: It's almost exactly as Nilsen's sketch imagined, a physical manifestation of the dialectical spiral of history.
Zh: 这几乎和尼尔森的速写一模一样，真实地呈现了历史的辩证性螺旋发展。

En: Well, I think we're probably finished here, detective.
Zh: “好了，我觉得我们差不多完成了，警探。”

En: Yes, it's probably time for you to take your leave as well.
Zh: 是的，你也该离开了……

En: You know, the young man looks up at you as you enter, "if you wanted to borrow my jacket, you could've just *asked*..."
Zh: “你知道吗，”年轻人抬头看你。“如果你想借走我的夹克，只要*问我一声*就行了……”

En: But no matter. What's on your mind?
Zh: “不过没关系。你在想什么呢？”

En: It looks better on you, anyway. He never had the shoulders for it.
Zh: 但显然你穿着它更帅气。他的肩膀不够宽。

En: What does this question say about me?
Zh: “这个问题是怎么说我的？”

En: Yes, that's the question you should be asking yourself.
Zh: “是的，你应该问自己这个问题。”

En: His companion nods, but offers no further solace.
Zh: 他的同伴点点头，但没有再给出多余的安慰。

En: He smiles. "You could say I'm undoing some of the *material* damage the international community caused when we arrived here."
Zh: 他笑了笑。“当初国际社会到来的时候对这里造成了很大的伤害，你可以说我在*从物质上*对其中一部分伤害进行弥补。”

En: I think we can drop the drummer-angle, the lieutenant says impatiently. "That was my bad."
Zh: “我想我们可以放弃鼓手的推断了，”警督不耐烦地说到。“这是我的错。”

En: Two Areopagites and a Mesque -- could be Eugene, Theo, and Alain.
Zh: 两个艾瑞奥帕吉特人，还有一个梅斯克人——可能是尤金，特奥和阿兰。

En: Easy, detective. No need to jump to conclusions. He eyes the spectacled man near the window, who smiles and spreads his hands.
Zh: “放松点，警探。没必要这么快就下结论。”他看着窗边戴眼镜的男人，对方微笑着张开双手。

En: That's when I understood -- I should not be seeing this.
Zh: “那个时候我就明白了——我不应该看到这些的。”

En: That's not the real reason he's so apprehensive -- men in his position shouldn't be seen loitering around in underprivileged young men's apartments in the middle of the night.
Zh: 这并不是他如此焦虑的真正原因——像他这种地位的人，根本不应该半夜在一个贫困潦倒的年轻男子的公寓里逗留。

En: I'm trying to form a committee to assign *la responsabilité*.
Zh: “我想建立一个委员会来分配*责任*。”

En: My friend, if it's *la responsabilité* you're after, I have good news for you: There's no need to form such a committee, because it already exists!
Zh: “朋友，如果你在考虑的是*责任*，我有一个好消息要告诉你：你不用忙活了，因为已经有一个这样的委员会了！”

En: The Comité de Responsabilité de Revachol. It acts as a sort-of clearinghouse for Coalition activities. To put it simply, they are the ultimate arbiters of la responsabilité in this part of the world.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖责任委员会。它的功能类似于联盟事务的清算所。简单来说，他们对这片区域的责任归属作出最终的裁决……”

En: I would offer to connect you with the committee myself, but alas, I am not actually *in* Martinaise.
Zh: “我很想亲自向委员会引荐你，可惜，我现在并不*在*马丁内斯。”

En: Yes, this is just the sort of reasonable authority you're looking for!
Zh: 没错，这就是你想要的官方渠道！

En: What? Where is he, then?
Zh: 什么？那他现在在哪？

En: You *knew* there was something strange about this one! He's an astral projection, a phantasm!
Zh: 你就*知道*这个人有点不对劲！他是群星的投射，一道幻影！

En: He's speaking figuratively. He means he's not in Martinaise in his *official* capacity.
Zh: 他只是打个比方，意思是他不是因为*公务*来到马丁内斯的。

En: What are you talking about? I'm looking at you right now!
Zh: “你在说什么？你不就在我面前吗？”

En: Isn't it? A triumph of both technical ingenuity and international cooperation. I, for one, feel much safer knowing it's up there, watching all of us.
Zh: “确实如此。这是科技进步与国际协作的胜利。很多人，包括我，就因为我们都在它的监管之中而感到安心。”

En: Of course, we are speaking informally, but *officially* I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives...
Zh: “当然，我们只是随便聊聊。按照*官方说法*，我此时正在德尔塔，准备参加即将到来的燃油衍生品会议……”

En: (Attempt to stick your hand through his astral form.)
Zh: （试图伸手穿过他的星光投影。）

En: I got it. You're only here on *personal* business.
Zh: “我明白了。你只是出于*私人*原因来到这里。”

En: So... where *are* you, then?
Zh: “那么……你到底*在*哪里？”

En: So what am I supposed to do?
Zh: “那我该做什么？”

En: Unauthorised circuit-bending is a very serious matter with grave security implications, but as of the moment there is no specific law *forbidding* it, in Revachol at least...
Zh: “未经许可的电路改装是个大问题，因为它会带来严重的安全隐患，但与此同时，在并没有一条明确的法律*禁止*它，至少在瑞瓦肖没有……”

En: Précisément. *Officially* I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives...
Zh: “说话要精准。按照*官方说法*，我此时正在德尔塔，准备参加即将到来的燃油衍生品会议……”

En: Why, *officially* I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives. I'm only in Martinaise in an unofficial capacity...
Zh: “当然不。按照*官方说法*，我此时正在德尔塔，准备参加即将到来的燃油衍生品会议。我来马丁内斯只是为了一点私事……”

En: Mais que faites-vous, officer? Your hand bumps up against the man's solid if somewhat fleshy chest.
Zh: “你怎么回事，警官？”你的巴掌撞上这个男人坚实，甚至可以说是肌肉发达的胸膛。

En: That's why it would be extremely irregular, or potentially even *inappropriate* for me to intercede with the Committee concerning a district I'm not officially in.
Zh: “这就是为什么我绝不能和这里的委员会产生交集，因为按照官方说法我并不处在这个区域。这件事将会是严重违规，甚至*极不恰当*的行为。”

En: Let me just say that I have *complete confidence* in the RCM. I'm sure you'll figure something out.
Zh: “我得说，我对RCM*满怀信心*。我确信你会搞明白的。”

En: Unfortunately not, no, I don't have the pleasure. I am merely a representative of the Coalition Government, not very well-versed in technology...
Zh: “不幸的是，不，我并不拥有这样的荣幸。我只不过是联合政府的一名小小代表，对技术问题不甚了解……”

En: Well, of course it *is* a heavily armed and armoured warship with a full complement. But I assure you its purpose in Revachol is to *keep* the peace, not disturb it.
Zh: “这个嘛，当然它*确实*是一艘重火力、重装甲、物资充沛的战舰。但我可以向你保证，它开到瑞瓦肖来的目的是*维护*和平，而不是破坏它……”

En: Yes, I was wondering the same thing.
Zh: “是的，我也在想这件事。”

En: You *will* figure it out. Something tells you the person you're looking for is already on this coast...
Zh: 你*会*找出原因的。不知为何，你觉得你要寻找的那个人已经在这片海岸边了……

En: Don't let the paintjob fool you, it's a goddamn killing machine, and there's nothing you can do to stop it!
Zh: 别被上面的装饰骗了，这是台杀人机器，你做什么都没法让它停下来的！

En: Perhaps your new friends in the tent might be able to help you?
Zh: 也许你那几位帐篷里的新朋友能帮帮你？

En: I don't think I've ever noticed it before.
Zh: “我觉得之前我并没有注意到它。”

En: This sounds like something that programmer you met in the church might help with...
Zh: 听起来，你在教堂里遇到的那个程序员或许能帮上忙……

En: Oh, right. The *Archer*. I thought you said... something else.
Zh: “哦，对。*弓箭手*。我以为你在说……别的东西。”

En: Don't let the shiny lights fool you, it's a goddamn killing machine, and there's nothing you can do to stop it!
Zh: 被那些闪亮的灯泡骗了，这是台杀人机器，你做什么都没法让它停下来的！

En: Oh, good: 'Techno-tinkerers.' Just what this *side task* was missing...
Zh: “哦，不错：‘发明家’。*支线任务*正需要这个……”

En: Hmmm, perhaps your young friends in the *tent* might be of assistance?
Zh: “嗯，也许你那几位*帐篷*里的年轻朋友能帮上忙？”

En: He said he wasn't really here. I had to make sure he wasn't an astral projection.
Zh: “他刚刚说他不在这里。我得确认一下他是不是一道星光投影。”

En: You said you weren't really here. I had to make sure you weren't an astral projection.
Zh: “你刚刚说你不在这里。我得确认一下你是不是一道星光投影。”

En: Nothing, just lost my balance a moment.
Zh: “没什么，只是手滑了。”

En: Hmmm, sounds like something that programmer we met in the church might be able to help with...
Zh: “嗯，听起来我们在教堂里遇到的那个程序员像是能帮上忙……”

En: But this is an urgent matter. I *need* to get in touch with the Committee.
Zh: “但我有急事。我*必须*马上联系到委员会。”

En: How am I supposed to contact the Committee then?
Zh: “那我要怎么联系委员会呢？”

En: Are you saying I should just give up?
Zh: “你的意思是我应该就这么放弃？”

En: No, it's more like a creature from the abyssopelagic zone, a great dark bioluminescent fish...
Zh: 不，它更像是从深渊之地爬出的怪物，一条雄伟、黑暗的荧光巨鱼……

En: It's about four-and-a-half kilometres from your position, you estimate.
Zh: 根据估算，它离你约四千五百米。

En: Ah, well, I'm glad to hear that at least. It would really be *quite unusual* for you to have never noticed it before...
Zh: “啊，好吧，至少你说出来了。如果你在之前从来没注意到过，那一定是*十分不同寻常*的……”

En: No, no, don't be silly. We're simply speaking in an informal capacity. *Officially* I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives...
Zh: “不，不，别犯傻。我们只是随便聊聊。按照*官方说法*，我此时正在德尔塔，准备参加即将到来的燃油衍生品会议……”

En: Perhaps your blood sugar is a bit low? As I was saying, *officially* speaking I am still in La Delta, preparing for an upcoming conference on fuel oil derivatives...
Zh: “或许你有点低血糖？我刚才说了，按照*官方说法*，我此时正在德尔塔，准备参加即将到来的燃油衍生品会议……”

En: Not just any fish, it moves like some ancient bottom-feeder gliding over the ocean floor in search of prey...
Zh: 它可不是普通的鱼，它就像远古的清道夫那样滑过海床，搜寻猎物……

En: Yes, you've made your *persistence* quite clear. Under normal circumstances I would have to insist you go through the regular channels.
Zh: “是的，你已经清楚地传达了你的*执着*。在一般的情况下，我一定会通过常规渠道协助你……”

En: The man frowns. "Well, under ordinary circumstances, the Committee contacts *you*."
Zh: 他皱起眉。“嗯，在正常情况下，是委员会来联系*你*……”

En: I don't mean to discourage you, of course, just to say that it won't be *easy* to reach the Committee.
Zh: “当然，我无意阻止你，我只是告诉你，想要联系委员会并没有那么*容易*……”

En: Interesting. In a sense, that's the very idea. The Coalition doesn't want the people of Revachol to feel that they are under a *military* occupation. That is why the Archer is always careful to maintain a constant, discreet distance.
Zh: “有趣。某种程度上来说，基本是这样没错。联盟并不希望瑞瓦肖的人民感到自己生活在*军事*占领之下。因此弓箭手总是注意保持一个隐秘的距离……”

En: No, no. You can't let them give you the runaround.
Zh: 不，不。你不能让他们踢皮球。

En: In other words, the committee only knows what it *wants* to know.
Zh: 换句话说，委员会只知道它*想要*知道的事情。

En: Not exactly a model of accessible and accountable governance...
Zh: 这可称不上什么平易近人、负责可靠的模范统治……

En: But if you have information of a vital interest, they might be willing to entertain an exception. In which case, I would advise you to contact them via Coalition Warship Archer.
Zh: “但如果你确实有生死攸关的大事，他们也乐于为你破例。在这种情况下，我建议你通过联盟战舰弓箭手联系他们。”

En: It looks, to your eye, like some kind of long, metallic fish, suspended beneath a number of ceiling fans...
Zh: 在你的眼中，它如同一条巨大的钢铁巨鱼，悬挂在许多吊顶式电风扇的下方……

En: That is part of the idea, of course. The Coalition doesn't want the people of Revachol to feel that they are under a *military* occupation. That is why the Archer is always careful to maintain a constant, discreet distance.
Zh: “差不多是这样没错。联盟并不希望瑞瓦肖的人民感到自己生活在*军事*占领之下。因此弓箭手总是注意保持一个隐秘的距离……”

En: I suppose there might be some way to *circuit-bend* your way onto those frequencies, but you'd have to be one of those *techno-tinkerers* to do it.
Zh: “我想应该有办法通过改装电路，把你连接到那些频率，但你得是‘发明家’才行。”

En: Fascinating. I always thought it was more like a dragonfly. But isn't it interesting how humanity always finds inspiration in nature?
Zh: “有意思。我一直觉得它更像一只蜻蜓。但人们从大自然中汲取永恒的灵感，这岂不是非常有趣的事情吗？”

En: That, my friend, is your responsabilité. I cannot take it from you, nor can you delegate it to someone else...
Zh: “这个嘛，朋友，就是你的责任了。我无法替你完成它，你也不能把它委托给别人……”

En: Is that... legal?
Zh: “那……合法吗？”

En: Wait, what's Coalition Warship Archer?
Zh: “等等，联盟军舰弓箭手是什么？”

En: So how does one go about contacting a Coalition warship? (Proceed.)
Zh: “那么我要怎么联系一艘联盟军舰？”（继续。）

En: Officer? You mean to say you haven't noticed the giant Coalition aerostatic peacefully patrolling the skies over Revachol?
Zh: “警官？你是说，你从未注意到那艘在瑞瓦肖天际和平地巡逻的、属于联盟的矩形静风舰吗？”

En: It looks like it's about four-and-a-half kilometres away.
Zh: “它看起来像有四千五百米那么远。”

En: Huh. Can't believe I never noticed it before.
Zh: “ 哈。难以置信，我居然从未注意到过它。”

En: This is quite the problem, very tricky. Of course, the Archer has orders to fire on any unidentified aerostatics that might approach it, so it might be safer to get in touch *from the ground*...
Zh: “这确实是个问题，很棘手。当然，弓箭手有权限向任何接近它的静风舰开火，所以*从地面*接触它或许会更安全……”

En: It looks like a killing machine.
Zh: “它看起来像一台杀人机器。”

En: It looks like a fish.
Zh: “它看起来像一条鱼。”

En: It's amazing. An inspiring symbol.
Zh: “太迷人了，它是一个令人振奋的标志物。”

En: Do you know any of these tinkerers?
Zh: “你认识这些发明家吗？”

En: But in that case, you would require a radio transmitter capable of broadcasting on Coalition frequencies. And that kind of technology naturally isn't typically available for non-Coalition use. Yes, it *is* a bit of a conundrum.
Zh: “但在这种情况下，你就需要一台无线电发射机，要能在联盟的通讯频率上进行广播。但这样的技术通常不会对非联盟成员开放。是的，这确实*是*个难题……”

En: You look out the window toward the north. There, hanging some distance above the horison, you can just make out a strange silhouette...
Zh: 你透过窗户向北方看去。在那个方向，地平线之上，你隐约看到一个奇怪的轮廓……

En: You look out the window toward the north. There, amidst the low-hanging stars, you can just make out signs of motion...
Zh: 你透过窗户向北方看去。在那个方向，低垂的群星之中，你隐约看到一些物体移动的痕迹……
