En: You look out the window, but you can't see anything. But then, it's dark, and your eyes aren't the sharpest...
Zh: 你向窗外看去，但什么都没看到。毕竟窗外一片昏黑，你的眼神又不怎么好……

En: It's a set of position lights moving in unison, like a restless constellation...
Zh: 那是一组同步移动的灯信号，如同永不停歇的星座……

En: You look around, but you don't see anything unusual. Then again, your eyes aren't the sharpest...
Zh: 你环顾四周，但没有看到任何不同寻常的事物。毕竟，你的眼神不怎么好……

En: What's the meaning of this? He looks first at you, then at the lieutenant. "Is your colleague here trying to *arrest me*?"
Zh: “这是什么意思？”他先是看着你，然后看向警督的方向。“你这位同事是想*逮捕我*吗？”

En: Back off -- *now*! This man has diplomatic immunity. It serves no good to accuse him of a crime he didn't commit.
Zh: 退下——就是*现在*！这个男人拥有外交豁免权。指控他犯下莫须有的罪名一点好处都没有。

En: He sounds almost mocking, saying those words -- *arrest me*.
Zh: 当他说出那句话——*逮捕我*的时候，口气里几乎有些嘲弄的意味。

En: Back off -- *now*! This man has diplomatic immunity. It serves no good to accuse him of a crime he didn't commit.
Zh: 退下——就是*现在*！这个男人拥有外交豁免权。指控他犯下莫须有的罪名一点好处都没有。

En: But we were talking about my friend here, not politics... He chuckles gently.
Zh: “但是我们在讨论我的朋友，不是政治……”他轻声笑了出来。

En: My friend, my friend, what about him?
Zh: “我的朋友，我的朋友，他怎么了？”

En: Already? He glances at his watch. "Well, I'm glad I could help."
Zh: “这么快吗？”他瞥了一眼手表。“好吧，很高兴我能帮上忙。”

En: The lieutenant goes pale. 'No badge?' he mouths to himself, silently.
Zh: 警督脸色苍白。“没有警徽？”他默默地自言自语到。

En: He didn't see the hanging, he saw the little *show* staged by the Hardies. Let him talk. He may know more than even he knows.
Zh: 他没有看见绞刑，他看见的是哈迪兄弟做的一场小*秀*。让他说。他知道的可能比自己了解的还要多。

En: The lieutenant nods to you. "That lines up with *previous testimony*, doesn't it? Well done, detective."
Zh: 警督朝你点点头。“这跟*之前的证词*是一致的，不是吗？做得好，警探。”

En: Thank you, sir. I think we've got the picture. Anything else stand out?
Zh: “谢谢你，先生。我想我们明白了。还有什么特别的事吗？”

En: But I suspect you knew that already. I can't say I'm surprised. The fine reputation of the men and women serving in the RCM is well deserved.
Zh: “但是，我怀疑你已经知道了。不能说我很惊讶。在RCM服务的男男女女享有良好的声誉是当之无愧的。”

En: Only that there were about eight or ten -- I couldn't make out anything, it was so dark -- and that it was *quiet*, he says, smoothing his hair. "Quietest lynching I ever *heard of*, let alone heard..."
Zh: “只有一点，大概有8到10个人吧——太黑了，我什么也辨认不出来——而且还很*安静*，”他一边说，一边捋了捋头发。“这是我*听说*过的最安静的私刑，更不用说是亲耳听过的……”

En: His name was Ellis Kortenaer.
Zh: “他的名字叫埃利斯·科顿艾尔。”

En: Ellis Kortenaer? That's an interesting name.
Zh: “埃利斯·科顿艾尔？这个是很有意思的名字。”

En: He was a mercenary.
Zh: “他是个雇佣兵。”

En: Why do I need to go through Archer to speak to the Committee?
Zh: “为什么我得通过弓箭手才能接触到委员会？”

En: I find it a great comfort to know there are benevolent powers watching over all, in strict accordance with the Wayfarer Act and the Bristelmüne Conventions.
Zh: “善良的掌权者在严格依照旅人法案与布里斯特尔穆协定照管我们，这令我感到安慰。”

En: You see, in addition to being an airborne artillery platform, Coalition Warship Archer is also the linchpin of the Coalition's surveillance and communications infrastructure in Revachol.
Zh: “你看，除作为浮空火力平台以外，弓箭手还是联盟在瑞瓦肖的监视与通讯系统的关键……”

En: And listening! It has the most objective vantage point in the entire city. Not to mention a vast array of radio, photographic, and meteorological monitoring instruments.
Zh: “听着！它有着全城最开阔的视野，更别提那数不胜数的无线电、摄像、气象设备了……”

En: The Bristelmüne Conventions are a series of international agreements governing the treatment of military personnel and civilians in theatres of war as well as occupied territories. In practice, they are more talked about than observed.
Zh: 布里斯特尔穆协定是一系列国际条约的统称，它规定了军事人员与普通公民在交战区与占领区的待遇。但在实际情况中，这项协定更多是被口头提起。

En: Hold on, it's *watching* us, too?
Zh: “等等，它也在*监管*我们？”

En: Officers of the Revachol Citizens Militia. The man in business-casual removes his cuff links.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖公民武装的警官。”穿着商务休闲装的男人解开袖扣。

En: Was there anything else?
Zh: “还有别的事吗？”

En: You shouldn't be seeing him in an intimate setting. For some reason you feel this man is your... *superior*.
Zh: 你不应该在私密场合看见他。出于某种原因，你感觉这个男人是你的……*上级*。

En: His hands are clean and well-manicured. This is a man who knows the importance of appearances.
Zh: 他的手很干净，指甲修剪的很整齐。这是很懂得外表重要性的男人。

En: Can you tell me about your friend again?
Zh: “能再跟我说说你的朋友吗？”

En: Can you tell me about your friend?
Zh: “能跟我说说你的朋友吗？”

En: What's Kedra?
Zh: “肯德拉是什么？”

En: Someone's baby is bawling its eyes out.
Zh: “谁家的小孩哭的都不能自已了。”

En: Kedra is a candidate member of EPIS. But, between you and me, their potential membership is a more... *contentious* issue.
Zh: “肯德拉是EPIS的候补成员。但是，这句话仅限于你我之间——他们可能取得成员资格是一件更加……*有争议*的问题。”

En: What's this EPIS thing you keep talking about?
Zh: “你一直说的这个EPIS到底是什么？”

En: How did you two even become friends?
Zh: “你们俩怎么会成为朋友的？”

En: How did any of us become friends? Bad things happening on the Insulindian isola. Oil platforms ablaze in the night. Civil wars lasting for years. Finally, the international community is forced to step in.
Zh: “大家都是怎么成为朋友的？伊苏林迪洲正在发生一些很糟糕的事情。钻井平台在夜间燃烧。内战持续了好多年。最终，国际社会被迫干预。”

En: You're describing how the Coalition occupied Revachol.
Zh: “你在描述联盟是如何占领瑞瓦肖的。”

En: Except that *candidate* members never become *full* members, do they?
Zh: 只是*候选*成员永远也不会成为*正式*会员，是吧？

En: A busy bee? What an odd choice of words.
Zh: 忙碌的蜜蜂？这个选词很奇怪。

En: EPIS is a very special program developed by the Moralintern to support certain Occidental nations. It began as a unified system of weights and measures, which proved to be a *wild* success. Nothing but kilograms and centimetres as far as the eye can see!
Zh: “EPIS是国际道德伦理委员会为了支持某些奥西登国家专门开发的一个特殊项目。起初，它是一种统一的度量衡制度，结果收获了*巨大的*成功。目之所及，大家全都改用了公斤和厘米！”

En: It was *such* a wild success that we expanded it into an economic union for the processing of steel -- another success. And between you and me... the Moralintern feels *emboldened* by this success. Emboldened to take EPIS to the next level.
Zh: “由于获得了*非常*巨大的成功，人们将它扩展到了钢铁加工的经济联盟里——结果又成功了。下面的话仅限于你我之间……国际道德伦理委员因为它的成功而*倍受鼓舞*，于是大胆地将EPIS提升到了一个新的水平。”

En: My friend comes and goes. I'm sure you'll see him around, he's a busy bee.
Zh: “我的朋友来来去去。我肯定你会看见他的，他就像只忙碌的蜜蜂。”

En: What's on the next level?
Zh: “提升到新水平之后呢？”

En: I was asking about your friend.
Zh: “我在问你的朋友。”

En: A supranational political alliance, the United States of Occident.
Zh: “一个超国家政治联盟，奥西登合众国。”

En: Is it going to be like this place here?
Zh: “会像这个地方一样吗？”

En: You mean Revachol? No, it's going to have transparent democracy.
Zh: “你说瑞瓦肖？不会的，它会采用透明的民主制度。”

En: Waves crashing in the distance, the sound of sea below the window.
Zh: 远处的海浪不断撞击，窗户下面传来大海的声音。

En: Is Revachol going to be part of EPIS?
Zh: “瑞瓦肖会变成EPIS的一部分吗？”

En: Listen, he says, raising his hand...
Zh: “听，”他抬起手，说到……

En: What view? It's dark outside.
Zh: “什么风景？外面很黑。”

En: A baby is crying in the neighbouring apartment.
Zh: 隔壁公寓里传来婴儿的哭声。

En: What are you talking about? No one becomes friends that way.
Zh: “你在说什么呢？没人会通过那种方式变成朋友的。”

En: We're old friends, nothing's taboo between us. He comes and goes.
Zh: “我们是老朋友，彼此之间百无禁忌。他总是来来去去。”

En: Officer, you have to understand... I can't give you his personal information. I'm sure you have your own methods and databases, right? Please don't put me in this situation.
Zh: “警官，你得理解……我不能给你他的个人信息。我肯定你有自己的方法和数据库，对吗？请不要把我置于这种境地。”

En: Au contraire. It's how millions of people end up where they are, meeting the people they meet. It's how I came here, and my friend, too.
Zh: “相反。数百万人就是这样走到今天这一步的，遇到他们会遇到的人。我就是这么来的，我的朋友也是。”

En: Fine, but what's his real name?
Zh: “好吧，那他的真名叫什么？”

En: One of the wonders of democracy is that everyone is allowed to have his own opinion. The man pats his pockets, looking for something. "And not just allowed, *encouraged* even. Have you ever tried debate?"
Zh: “民主的奇观之一就是允许每个人拥有自己的意见。”男人拍打着自己的口袋，寻找着什么。“不仅仅是允许，甚至是*鼓励*。你有试过辩论吗？”

En: He's a bright young man here to pursue his education. Education is the foundation of our future, especially the arts. It is a cornerstone of our civilization.
Zh: “他是个聪明的年轻人，来这里追求学业。教育是未来的根基，特别是艺术。它是人类文明的奠基石。”

En: Ah, my friend. My friend is a good young man. His family immigrated here from Kedra and life has not been easy for him. But he understands the importance of education. He has taken his future into his own hands and that's all that matters.
Zh: “啊，我的朋友。我的朋友是一个好青年。他的家族从肯德拉移民过来，生活一直都不太容易。但是他明白教育的重要性。他把未来掌握在了自己手中，这才是最重要的事情。”

En: Isn't it rude for your friend to leave you alone like this?
Zh: “你的朋友把你一个人留在这里，难道不觉得很不礼貌吗？”

En: Martin Martinaise? That's not a name. Are you referring to my friend? But I told you, he's from Kedra -- definitely not just another Martin Martinaise or Raoul Revachol. Perhaps that's why he and I became friends...
Zh: “马丁·马丁内斯？那根本就不算是个名字。你是想说我的朋友吗？我不是跟你说过吗，他是从肯德拉来的——肯定不是什么马丁·马丁内斯或者拉乌尔·瑞瓦肖。也许这就是我和他变成朋友的原因……”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你什么意思？”

En: Your friend, Martin Martinaise.
Zh: “你的朋友，马丁·马丁内斯。”

En: Debating. You should consider joining a debating society for adults. I hear they're *oodles* of fun. I used to have a flyer for one, but...
Zh: “辩论。你应该考虑加入成年人的辩论社。我听说那里真的*特别*有趣。我以前有一份传单，但是……”

En: I'm just enjoying the view, the man smiles, nodding to the window.
Zh: “我只是在欣赏风景，”男人笑着朝窗户点了点头。

En: But now that I start to think of it, it was for an improv class anyway. It's this funny theatre thing, you know, he moves his fingers, "very *creative*, helps relieve stress."
Zh: “但是现在这么一想，它只是一门即兴表演课程。你知道的，就是那种有趣的戏剧类的东西，”他动了动自己的手指，“非常*有创造性*，能帮忙减轻压力。”

En: But the million reál view stays. You can't take that away. He knocks on the balcony door, his face mirrored in the darkened glass.
Zh: “但是价值百万雷亚尔的风景还保留着。这是谁也拿不走的。”他敲了敲阳台大门，面容映照在黑暗的玻璃上。

En: No, *listen*, he says again, looking outside...
Zh: “不，*你听*，”他看着外面，又说了一次……

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你什么意思？”

En: That it's never going to happen. They entered negotiations in '21 and it's been pending ever since.
Zh: “这种事永远也不会发生的。他们从21年就开始谈判，在那之后就一直悬而未决。”

En: What? I'm not hearing anything.
Zh: “什么？我什么也没听到。”

En: The Insulindian Bay.
Zh: “伊苏林迪海湾。”

En: But that doesn't tell me anything about Kedra itself. Is it warm there? Cold? Something in-between?
Zh: “但是这也没告诉我任何有关肯德拉的信息。那里是暖还是冷？还是适中？”

En: I'm all ears, officer.
Zh: “我洗耳恭听，警官。”

En: You still haven't told me who he is.
Zh: “你还没告诉我他是谁。”

En: It's one day going to be a *candidate member* of EPIS, sure.
Zh: “某一天，它会成为EPIS的*候选成员*，这是肯定的。”

En: I had something else in mind...
Zh: “我在想别的事情……”

En: Didn't you say that candidate members never become real members?
Zh: “你不是说候选成员永远也当不了正式成员吗？”

En: Someone's baby is crying.
Zh: “谁家小孩在哭。”

En: No, no, candidate members *do* become members, why do we even have the whole system in place if they don't? It just takes time -- time and evaluation.
Zh: “不，不是，候选成员*会*变成正式的，如果整个系统不起作用的话，我们为什么还要创建它呢？只不过需要一点时间——时间和评估。”

En: Which arts?
Zh: “什么艺术？”

En: I'm sure you'll see him around, he's very active.
Zh: “我肯定你会看见他的，他很活跃。”

En: So all you can tell me about him is that he's here to study the arts?
Zh: “所以你唯一能告诉我的，就是他是来这里学习艺术的？”

En: He's a truly free spirit. He likes all the arts. Perhaps graphic design? Printmaking? Who knows? The world is open wide for a talented youth like him.
Zh: “他拥有真正自由的灵魂。他喜欢各种各样的艺术。也许是平面设计？版画复制？谁知道呢。对于他这种才华横溢的年轻人，世界是大张怀抱的。”

En: But I told you, officer. He's a bright young man here to pursue his education. Education is the foundation of our future, especially the arts. It is a cornerstone of our civilization.
Zh: “但是我告诉过你了，警官。他是一个来这里追求学业的聪明年轻人。教育是未来的根基，特别是艺术。它是人类文明的奠基石。”

En: Sorry, who? The man throws a quick glance at his watch.
Zh: “对不起，你说谁？”男人飞快地瞥了一眼手表。

En: Your friend, the smoker on the balcony. We were just talking about him.
Zh: “你的朋友，阳台上那个抽烟的人。我们刚刚还在聊他。”

En: This place used to be a luxury accommodation, before the Revolution. Apartments, of course, were much bigger then -- a few walls have been added here and there, leaving some of the tenants without a private bathroom or a kitchen...
Zh: “在大革命之前，这里曾经是豪华住宅。当然了，那时的公寓要更大一些——后来到处加了些墙，结果有些租客就没有私人浴室或者是厨房了……”

En: What about it?
Zh: “它怎么了？”

En: I can hear the waves.
Zh: “我可以听见海浪的声音。”

En: He's deeply enmeshed in the study of the fine arts, yes.
Zh: “是的，他深深地沉浸在艺术学习的世界里。”

En: It's warm, he nods. "It's also known for its mandarin trees and dust storms from Supramundi."
Zh: “那里很温暖，”他点点头。“以桔子树和苏帕穆迪的沙尘暴而闻名。”

En: Kedra's an emerging market, but it still has a long way to go -- maybe that's why my friend's ménage decided to emigrate.
Zh: “肯德拉是一个新兴市场，但还有很长的路要走——也许这就是我朋友的家人决定移民的原因。”

En: Do you think *peace* is boring? What about *prosperity*?
Zh: “你觉得*和平*无聊吗？那*繁荣*呢？”

En: Yes. As I said before, I'm a commissioner from Sur-la-Clef working for the Institute of Price Stabilité. He glances at his watch. "This is one of the main projects of the Moralintern."
Zh: “是的。就像我之前说的，我是一名来自苏拉菲的委员，在物价稳定研究所工作。”他瞥了一眼自己的手表。“它是国际道德伦理委员会的主要项目之一。”

En: What's an official like you doing in Martinaise?
Zh: “像你这样的官员在马丁内斯做什么？”

En: Okay, but what are you doing *here* in this apartment?
Zh: “好吧，但是你在*这间*公寓里做什么？”

En: So you're some kind of bureaucrat?
Zh: “所以你算是某种官僚了？”

En: What is this *international community*?
Zh: “*国际社会*是什么意思？”

En: I've heard about this Moralintern before, but I want to know more.
Zh: “我之前听说过这个国际道德伦理委员会，但是想再多了解一些。”

En: What's the Moralintern?
Zh: “国际道德伦理委员会是什么？”

En: Are you a moralist?
Zh: “你是一个道德家吗？”

En: Ah, well, I'm renovating it. It is an interesting project. The building used to be a twelve-story skyscraper before the cannons took the top four stories off. This of course happened when the Coalition forces landed here...
Zh: “呃，我正在翻新它。这是个很有趣的工程。这栋建筑曾经是一栋12层楼高的摩天大厦，直到大炮把顶上四层削掉了。当然了，这件事发生在联盟部队登陆的时候……”

En: Oh, yes, the big bad Coalition crushed the Revolution. Tell me, if the revolution was succeeding, would it have been crushed so easily?
Zh: “啊，是的，邪恶的联盟粉碎了大革命。告诉我，如果大革命是成功的，它还会这么轻易就被粉碎吗？”

En: La communauté internationale is what Revacholians colloquially call *the Coalition*. In other words, the nations that stopped the disaster of the Revolution.
Zh: “国际社会，通俗的讲，就是瑞瓦肖人口中的*联盟*。换句话说，是阻止那场灾难性大革命的国家。”

En: Whatever you wish, officer.
Zh: “随你的便，警官。”

En: What is *ze price stabilité*?
Zh: “那*物价稳定研究所*是什么？”

En: It is ze most important thing.
Zh: “是最重要的东西。”

En: Enough business, let's talk about something else.
Zh: “这个说够了，我们聊聊别的吧。”

En: The Coalition is only looking out for *ze price stabilité*. He raises an index finger. "Inflation is a killer, like a heart disease blocking the normal circulation of the economy -- It must be controlled..."
Zh: “联盟只是在留意这里的*物价稳定*情况。”他竖起食指。“通货膨胀是一个杀手，就像阻碍经济正常循环的心脏疾病——必须把它控制住……”

En: And what *do* I call the Coalition?
Zh: “那我*怎么*称呼联盟呢？”

En: Your employer, technically speaking. The governing authority of Revachol. The RCM is but one part of this provisional administration.
Zh: “从技术的角度讲，它是你的雇主。瑞瓦肖的管理当局。RCM是临时行政机构的一部分。”

En: Ah, yes, the unattainable ideal. Never settle for less! Good luck with that, my friend.
Zh: “啊，是的，不可能实现的理想。永远也不要退而求其次！祝你好运了，我的朋友。”

En: If he knows so much about Oranje, maybe he also knows about Oranjese mercenaries?
Zh: 如果他这么了解奥兰治，也许也会知道奥兰治雇佣兵的事？

En: It's usually the most boring one.
Zh: “一般是最无聊的那个。”

En: That doesn't tell me anything.
Zh: “还是相当于什么都没说。”

En: I agree. And we as an international community should strive to bring this prosperity to every kid on every isola.
Zh: “我同意。而作为国际社会的我们，应该努力为每一个洲上的孩子带去繁荣的生活。”

En: It's the central goal of any sound monetary policy. Maintaining ze price stabilité is essential to maintaining high levels of economic activity, which is essential for maintaining high levels of employment...
Zh: “这是任何稳健的货币政策的核心目标。维持物价稳定对于保持高水平的经济活动来说是必不可少的，同时也是保持高就业水平的必需……”

En: Basically it makes sure the price of bread doesn't change.
Zh: “基本上就是保证面包的价格不会改变。”

En: Exactly. *I* don't find it boring, I find it *fulfilling*. Peace is what allows me to have my morning coffee, my afternoon golf, my sundown friends.
Zh: “没错。*我*觉得它一点也不无聊，而且很有*成就感*。和平能让我安心享用早晨的咖啡，下午的高尔夫，还有黄昏的友情。”

En: *Précisément*! Too much inflation, bread becomes too expensive; too much deflation, it becomes too cheap for bakers to produce...
Zh: “精辟！通货膨胀太严重，面包就会变贵；通货紧缩太严重，面包就会变得太便宜，面包师就不想做了……”

En: Below 2% of what?
Zh: “什么低于2%？”

En: But not *too* far below, no. Too below is also bad -- below, *but close to* 2%.
Zh: “但是不会低*太多*，不可以。太低也不好——低于，但是*接近*2%。”

En: You're not answering my questions at all.
Zh: “你根本没有回答我的问题。”

En: It's not like you're going to gather any forces soon with all that drinking -- and he knows it.
Zh: 并不是说喝了那么多酒之后，你马上就能聚集力量——而且他也明白这一点。

En: The Coalition believes in the importance of informing the public about the benefits of ze price stabilité. Transparency is one of our principles. Would you like an informational pamphlet?
Zh: “联盟相信——把稳定物价带来的益处传达给普罗大众是很重要的。透明是我们的原则之一。你想要一本信息手册吗？”

En: No, I'm good.
Zh: “不，我挺好的。”

En: Are we really so bad for wanting compromise, peace, and prosperity -- on reasonable, achievable terms? Ask yourself that.
Zh: “我们真的那么渴望妥协、和平和繁荣吗——在合理而又可实现的条件下？问问你自己。”

En: It's the international organization for moralists. Hence 'Moralist International.' The Institute of Price Stability is just one of its many *mind babies* -- as is the Coalition.
Zh: “它是一个道德家组成的国际组织。因此是‘国际道德组织’。物价稳定研究所只是其众多*思想传承*的其中之一——联盟也是。”

En: That's why the Institute of Price Stabilité works to keep inflation just below 2%...
Zh: “所以物价稳定研究所的工作就是将通货膨胀维持在正好2%以下的水平……”

En: How does this benefit us, exactly?
Zh: “那它到底是怎么为我们带来利益的呢？”

En: Okay, sure, give me a leaflet.
Zh: “好吧，当然了，给我一份。”

En: But why?
Zh: “但是为什么呢？”

En: Because moralists believe in a normal, stable world governed by democratic values.
Zh: “因为道德家相信要在民主价值观的统治下，建立一个正常而稳定的世界。”

En: Oranje's economy is one of the most advanced in the world. It has successfully transitioned from heavy industry to advanced services, and generally acts as an engine for sustainable change in the international community.
Zh: “奥兰治拥有世界上最发达的经济水平。它成功地从重工业过渡到了现代服务业，成为了国际社会可持续变革的引擎。”

En: Okay, but outside of EPIS, what is Oranje?
Zh: “好吧，但是除了EPIS之外，奥兰治又是什么呢？”

En: As founding members they are *both* very EPIS. Oranje carries a lot of political weight, while Sur-la-Clef takes care of the business side of things -- Sur-la-Clef hosts the headquarters of the major EPIS institutions.
Zh: “作为创始成员之一，它们*都*非常EPIS。奥兰治拥有很大的政治影响力，而苏拉菲则是关注经济方面问题——EPIS的主要研究机构的总部都设置在苏拉菲。”

En: Which one of them is more EPIS?
Zh: “哪个更EPIS一些？”

En: But they are working towards it! You're all doing very well here, relatively speaking. He gives you an approving nod.
Zh: “但是它们都在朝那个方向努力！相对而言，你们这里做的还是比较好的。”他赞许地向你点点头。

En: But of course!
Zh: “当然了啊！”

En: Tell me about Oranje.
Zh: “跟我讲讲奥兰治。”

En: Am *I* a moralist?
Zh: “*我*是道德家吗？”

En: The smile he gives you is straight off a campaign poster, the kind of smile focus group-tested to appeal to the most gullible swathe of the electorate.
Zh: 他的笑脸仿佛直接从竞选海报上撕下来的一样，这种经过焦点小组测试的微笑，非常能吸引那些最容易上当受骗的选民。

En: Moralism is all about compromise and achieving the achievable. It's pragmatic, realistic, and level-headed, an ideology for *doers*. Are you a doer, my friend? It looks to me like you are.
Zh: “道德主义是关于妥协和实现可实现目标的。它实用、现实、清醒，是为*实干家*准备的意识形态。你是个实干家吗，我的朋友？在我看来你就是。”

En: Well, do you value freedom? Do you believe in a normal, stable world governed by democratic values?
Zh: “呃，你重视自由吗？你相信由民主价值观统治的正常而又稳定的世界吗？”

En: It 'didn't work' because the Coalition crushed it violently.
Zh: “它没有‘起作用’，是因为联盟用暴力摧毁了它。”

En: We just haven't tried *real* communism yet.
Zh: “我们只是还没有尝试过*真正的*康米主义。”

En: But what is a normal, stable world?
Zh: “一个正常又稳定的世界是什么样的？”

En: The Occident is part of the normal world. Oranje, Sur-la-Clef...
Zh: “奥西登是正常世界的一部分。奥兰治，苏拉菲……”

En: No, prosperity sounds like a lot of fun.
Zh: “不，繁荣听起来要更有趣一些。”

En: Martinaise isn't very stable, even with the Moralintern giving us money.
Zh: “就算国际道德伦理委员给我们钱，马丁内斯也不稳定。”

En: Martinaise doesn't seem very normal, or stable, to me.
Zh: “在我看来，马丁内斯好像不太平常，也不稳定。”

En: Martinaise? No, Martinaise is... something else.
Zh: “马丁内斯？不，马丁内斯是……另一回事。”

En: No, peace sounds pretty good.
Zh: “不，和平听起来挺好的。”

En: What about the rest of Revachol? Is it part of the normal world?
Zh: “那瑞瓦肖的其他地方呢？也是平常世界的一部分吗？”

En: It's moving towards the right direction and that's all that matters. Progress is measured in centimetres, my friend.
Zh: “它正在朝正确的方向前进，这才是最重要的。进步是以厘米来衡量的，我的朋友。”

En: Revachol is generally... difficult. It's led by an interim government, which means it hasn't yet achieved *full* democracy...
Zh: “通常来说，瑞瓦肖……挺困难的。它由临时政府领导，这就意味着它还没有达到*完全的*民主……”

En: And you're telling me *this* world here is working out well?
Zh: “你是想告诉我，*这个*世界运转的不太好？”

En: Sounds like you're a moralist indeed, my friend. Welcome.
Zh: “听起来你确实是个道德家，我的朋友。欢迎欢迎。”

En: Yes, everything else is super extreme. It's like I'm living with a bunch of lunatics...
Zh: “是的，其他一切都太极端了。比如说我，就跟一群疯子生活在一起……”

En: Tell me about Sur-la-Clef.
Zh: “跟我说说苏拉菲。”

En: Is this 'Option D' usually the most *reasonable* answer?
Zh: “这个‘选项D’一般不都是最*合理*的答案吗？”

En: Your full autonomy is important, I agree. But it's an ongoing process. Nothing happens overnight.
Zh: “我同意，完全自治权对于你们来说是很重要的。但这是一个不断前进的过程。没有什么是一夜之间就能发生的。”

En: It's like every time I'm talking to people I'm choosing Option D, 'none of the above'... Is that moralism?
Zh: “简直就像，每次和别人说话的时候我都会选D，'以上都不合适’……这就是道德主义？”

En: Now, enough of this delightful political interlude. Was there anything else you wanted to ask?
Zh: “好了，愉快的政治小插曲已经说得差不多了。你还有什么想问的吗？”

En: I don't think I'm a moralist. Moralism sounds incredibly boring. I want more action.
Zh: “我不觉得自己是个道德家。道德主义听起来太无聊。我想要更多行动。”

En: Moralism is the ideology of foreign occupiers. Revachol must be governed by Revacholians!
Zh: “道德主义是外国侵略者的意识形态。瑞瓦肖必须由瑞瓦肖人自己统治！”

En: Democracy is a meaningless sham as long as the working class is under the boot-heel of Capital.
Zh: “只要工人阶级还被踩在资本家的脚底，民主就是一场毫无意义的骗局。”

En: Ah, my friend, but the lesson of the Revolution is that communism does not work.
Zh: “啊，我的朋友，但大革命的教训就是，康米主义是行不通的。”

En: A moment, officer.
Zh: “稍等一下，警官。”

En: Thanks, I've got all I need. [Leave.]
Zh: “谢谢，我已经得到所有需要的信息了。”[离开。]

En: The man who was lynched outside your window was from Oranje.
Zh: “在你窗外被处以私刑的男人是奥兰治来的。”

En: You have acquired the hat. He assesses the situation. "Keep it, officer, it looks good on you."
Zh: “你拿到那顶帽子了。”他评估了一下眼前的形势。“留着吧，警官，你戴上很好看。”

En: You have acquired the robe. He assesses the situation. "Keep it, officer, it looks good on you."
Zh: “你拿到制服了。”他评估了一下眼前的形势。“留着吧，警官，你穿着很好看。”

En: You have acquired the hat. The man assesses the situation. "Keep it, officer, it looks good on you."
Zh: “你拿到那顶帽子了。”他评估了一下眼前的形势。“留着吧，警官，你戴上很好看。”

En: You have acquired the robe. The man assesses the situation. "Keep it, officer, it looks good on you."
Zh: “你拿到制服了。”他评估了一下眼前的形势。“留着吧，警官，你穿着很好看。”

En: What's there to say? Sur-la-Clef is a modern, urbanized country that measures very high on the Human Development and Freedom Index. Mostly, though, it's known as the executive heart of *EPIS*...
Zh: “有什么好说的呢？苏拉菲是一个现代化、城市化的国家，人类发展和自由的指标得分很高。不过，它主要还是以*EPIS*的行政中心而闻名……”

En: Oranje is an *exemplary* nation who, as a core member of EPIS, contributes 28% of our annual budget. Next to Sur-la-Clef, Oranje is probably the most prominent member of the international community.
Zh: “奥兰治是一个*模范*国家，作为EPIS的核心成员，它为我们贡献了28%的年度预算。奥兰治紧邻苏拉菲，可能是国际社会最杰出的成员之一。”

En: Do you have everything you need from me? I'm afraid we won't have the chance to speak again once you leave.
Zh: “还有什么需要问我的吗？一旦你离开之后，恐怕就不会再有机会跟我说话了。”

En: I'm not going anywhere, I just want to take a look around in this apartment. [Leave.]
Zh: “我哪里也不去，我只是想看看这个公寓。”[离开。]

En: Thanks for the heads up, but my work here is done. [Leave.]
Zh: “感谢你的提醒，但是我在这里的工作已经完成了。”[离开。]

En: Of course, I'm glad I could help.
Zh: “当然，很高兴能帮到你。”

En: Hold on, why can't we talk later?
Zh: “等等，为什么我们以后不能说话了？”

En: Sure, go ahead, it's a beautiful space. He glances at his watch. "Let me know if you have any further questions."
Zh: “当然，请吧，这是个漂亮的地方。”他瞥了一眼手表。“如果你还有其他问题，直接说就好。”

En: It's against diplomatic best practices for an official in my position to be discussing murders with local militiamen. He pauses. "And I'm pressed for time. After you leave, I should be leaving as well."
Zh: “像我这种地位的官员，跟一位本地民兵讨论谋杀案是违反外交最佳实践的。”他停顿了一下。“而且我的时间很紧张。你离开之后，我也要走了。“

En: No, that's not his... the lieutenant sighs. "Martin Martinaise is a *joke*, a generic nickname for someone from Martinaise."
Zh: “不，不是他的……”警督叹了口气。“马丁·马丁内斯是个*玩笑话*，是马丁内斯人的通用昵称。”

En: And my friend's not even your typical local -- definitely not just another Martin Martinaise or Raoul Revachol. Maybe that's why we're friends.
Zh: “而且我的朋友甚至都不是典型的本地人——肯定不是又一个马丁·马丁内斯，或者是拉乌尔·瑞瓦肖。也许这就是我们能成为朋友的原因。”

En: But that didn't tell you anything about Oranje.
Zh: 但是这样还是不知道任何奥兰治的信息。

En: Can't you just... talk like a normal person?
Zh: “你能不能……像个普通人一样说话？”

En: I once heard from *here* that they are a reasonably sized superpower. (Tap your head.)
Zh: “我曾经在*这里*听说，它们是一股规模巨大的超级力量。”（拍拍你的脑袋。）

En: That's one way to put it, yes. They certainly have the power to exert influence over others, but they only do it in the name of regional stability. After all, Oranje is a Moralintern nation, not some crazed Mesque petro-fascist state.
Zh: “这是一种说法，没错。它们的确拥有对其他国家产生影响的能力，但这也是为了维护地区稳定性而做的。毕竟奥兰治是一个道德主义国家，不是什么疯狂的梅斯克*石油法西斯主义*国家。”

En: About what?
Zh: “关于什么的？”

En: About Oranje -- just tell me what it's like there.
Zh: “关于奥兰治——跟我说说那里什么样。”

En: Oh, it's very urban and very well-organized. Their streets are clean, their horsecars run on time, the people are polite and efficient. Like I said, they are an example for less-emerged nations to follow.
Zh: “哦，它很城市化，而且井井有条。那里的街道很干净，轨道马车运行准时，人们礼貌又有效率。就像我说的，它是欠发达国家效仿的范例。”

En: What makes Revachol Sur-la-Clef's 'darling'?
Zh: “为什么瑞瓦肖会成为苏拉菲的‘宠儿’？”

En: Because a great percentage of Revachol's culture hails from Sur-la-Clef -- its language, its people, its cuisine even, or at least in the downtown La Delta area.
Zh: “因为瑞瓦肖文化有很大一部分都来自于苏拉菲——它的语言、人民，甚至是烹饪方法，至少在德尔塔市区是这样的。”

En: Jamrock and other parts of the International Zone have been mercifully spared of Sur-la-Clef's love for meatballs and mashed potatoes.
Zh: 加姆洛克和国际区的其他部分被仁慈地赦免了，不用分享苏拉菲对肉丸和土豆泥的热爱。

En: The tone somehow suggests to you that you're starting to become a little sponsored by him as well.
Zh: 不知怎么的，这种语气暗示着他也有点开始支持你了。

En: Superior? But he's not in the command chain...
Zh: 上级？但他不在指挥链里……

En: (Point at the bed.) "Before we go on, I absolutely *have* to inquire about this wonderful canopy."
Zh: （指向床。）“在我们继续之前，我*必须*询问一下这个美妙的华盖。”

En: (Show him the silk robe.) "Before we get to that, tell me where you got this *beautiful* silk robe from?"
Zh: （把丝绸长袍拿给他看。）“在说那个之前，能告诉我这件*漂亮的*丝绸长袍是哪来的吗？”

En: (Show him the Samaran hat.) "We'll get to that. Right after you tell me the story behind the black Samaran hat."
Zh: （把萨马拉帽子拿给他看。）“之后会说到的。你先跟我说说这顶黑色萨马拉帽背后的故事吧。”

En: No, first ask an innocuous personal question to get the interview off on the right foot.
Zh: 不，先问个无伤大雅的私人问题，让这从询问有个良好的开端。

En: Yes. Make it clear you're the one setting the terms here...
Zh: 是的。明确一点，你才是这里定规矩的人……

En: (Get down to business.) "Hello, Mr. Villedrouin. Let's get started."
Zh: （言归正传。）“你好，维尔德鲁安先生。我们开始吧。”

En: Yeah, *yeah*! Let's *fuck* with him! Who does he think he is, assisting *you*?
Zh: 是啊，*是的*！去他妈的吧！他以为自己是谁，还说什么协助*你*？

En: Hanging? What a drag. He seems like a *cultured* gentleman -- you should ask him about the finer things...
Zh: 绞刑？真无聊。他像是那种*有文化*的绅士——你应该问他些更精细的问题……

En: Of course. Let me just say it has been an emotional week for me. He forms a little rooftop with his fingers. Cold air comes in from the balcony.
Zh: “当然。对我来说，这周真的非常激动人心。”他把手指搭成一个小屋顶的形状。冷风从阳台上吹了进来。

En: The lieutenant takes out his notebook and nods to you to proceed.
Zh: 警督拿出他的笔记本，点头示意你继续。

En: (Point to the silk robe.) "Before we get to that, tell me where you got this *beautiful* silk robe from?"
Zh: （把丝绸长袍拿给他看。）“在说那个之前，能告诉我这件*漂亮的*丝绸长袍是哪来的吗？”

En: (Point to the Samaran hat.) "We'll get to that. Right after you tell me the story behind the black Samaran hat."
Zh: （把萨马拉帽子拿给他看。）“之后会说到的。你先跟我说说这顶黑色萨马拉帽背后的故事吧。”

En: That's really all I can tell you about it. He forms a little rooftop with his fingers. Cold air sweeps in from the balcony.
Zh: “我真的只能说这么多了。”他把手指搭成一个小屋顶的形状。冷风从阳台上吹了进来。

En: I got it from the head of the Samaran delegation on my trip to Lo-Manthang. It's made from a *special* charcoal-coloured bamboo. It's an emblem of the formal normalization of our diplomatic relations.
Zh: “这是去罗马丹的途中，我从萨马拉代表团团长那里得来的。它是用一种*特殊的*炭色竹子制成的，是我们外交关系常规化的象征。”

En: Oh, we got it from an atelier in the East Delta Commerce Centre. Personally I think it's a little culturally insensitive, but the material is great. Sadly the shop is now out of business...
Zh: “哦，我是从东德尔塔商业中心的一间工作室得到的。就我个人而言，我觉得它对文化有些不太敏感，不过材质非常好。可惜那家商店现在已经破产了……”

En: He's alluding to the decade-long war of independence while deftly brushing aside the *complex* causes behind the conflict.
Zh: 他一方面在暗指那场持续十年之久的独立战争，同时也巧妙地忽略了那场冲突背后*复杂*的原因。

En: Wait, there isn't actually an *Institute of Price Stabilité*, is there? Or maybe there is... God, it's impossible to understand whether someone from the Moralintern is joking or not.
Zh: 等等，其实根本没有什么*物价稳定研究所*，对吧？或者可能有……天啦，要搞清楚国际道德伦理委员会的人是不是在开玩笑是不可能的。

En: Moreover, it is a great sponsor of less-emerged countries. Revachol is only one of its many darlings whose progress it supports and cherishes.
Zh: “而且，它还是很多欠发展国家的大赞助方。瑞瓦肖只是它支持和珍视的众多宠儿之一。”

En: A *fugitive*?
Zh: “一个*逃亡者*?”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 一言不发。

En: Sounds like the victim was unconscious. Or at least incapacitated. Interesting.
Zh: “听起来被害人失去意识了。或者至少是丧失了行动能力。有点意思。”

En: That didn't work at all.
Zh: 这根本一点用也没有。

En: What was *that*?! You were supposed to *fuck* with him, not compliment the décor!
Zh: *那是*在干什么？！你应该给他*好看*，而不是恭维装饰品！

En: The economy impacts the entire *international community*, which is why it requires international oversight.
Zh: “经济影响了整个*国际社会*，这就是需要国际监管的原因。”

En: You actually *witnessed* the lynching?
Zh: “你实际上是*目击*了那次私刑吗？”

En: I'm sorry to say I did, officer. The man gives a solemn nod.
Zh: “很抱歉的说，我确实看到了，警官。”男人郑重地点了点头。

En: (Turn to the lieutenant.) "This is just the break we've been looking for!"
Zh: （转向警督。）“这就是我们寻找的突破口！”

En: Start from the beginning, if you don't mind.
Zh: “如果你不介意的话，请从最开头说起吧。”

En: Is it because you did it, Mr. Villedrouin?
Zh: “因为是你做的吗，维尔德鲁安先生？”

En: Officer, it's very difficult to describe what I saw that night. It was so surreal to me, like in a play.
Zh: “警官，要描述那晚我看见的东西，真的很困难。对我来说太不真实了，就像一出戏剧。”

En: He holds out his hands and blossoms his fingers, like a drama teacher setting the scene.
Zh: 他伸出双手，张开手指，像一个布置场景的戏剧老师。

En: Because I *did it*? the man scoffs.
Zh: “因为是我*做的*？”男人的口气有些嘲弄的意味。

En: He's clearly not a man accustomed to being spoken to in that manner, let alone to being accused of *murder*.
Zh: 他显然不习惯别人用这种方式跟他说话，更不用说被指控*谋杀*了。

En: I was on the balcony when it happened, getting some fresh air. I remember that first they came in, carrying what looked like a body, and then I saw all the surrounding windows go dead one-by-one...
Zh: “事情发生的时候，我站在阳台上透气。我记得一开始他们走了进来，抬着一个类似人形的东西，然后我看见周围的窗户一个个地关上了……”

En: My apologies, I misspoke. I mean, what did you see?
Zh: “抱歉，我说错话了。我是说，你看见什么了？”

En: Don't jerk my chain, paper-jockey. You did it, didn't you?
Zh: “别戏弄我，坐办公室的家伙。就是你做的，对吗？”

En: The lieutenant is already scribbling down notes. "What do you mean, 'like in a play'?"
Zh: 警督已经开始潦草地做笔记了。“你说‘像一出戏剧’是什么意思？”

En: Meaning it was terrifying?
Zh: “意思是很可怕？”

En: Meaning it was entertaining?
Zh: “意思是让人很愉快？”

En: It was just so strange. I could barely comprehend what was happening...
Zh: “真的太奇怪了。我几乎不能理解到底发生了什么……”

En: No, no. The man shakes his head. "I mean it was so strange I could barely believe what was happening..."
Zh: “不，不。”男人摇摇头。“我的意思是实在太奇怪了，我都不敢相信这一切真的发生了……”

En: There is no meaning, sir. Please excuse my colleague. This case has... been a great strain on his nerves.
Zh: “没什么特别的意思，先生。请原谅我的同事。这个案子……给他的神经带来了不小的压力。”

En: What's the meaning of this?
Zh: “这是什么意思？”

En: What gets on my nerves is *murderers*. Why'd you kill him, man?
Zh: “让我心烦意乱的是*杀人犯*。你为什么要杀他，老兄？”

En: Kim's right. I'm sorry. I haven't been myself lately.
Zh: “金说得对。我很抱歉。我最近真的有些不太对劲。”

En: I'm sorry. Let's start over. My nerves got the best of me there.
Zh: “很抱歉。让我们重新开始吧。我的头脑有些不太清醒。”

En: It means the jig's up, creep. I am the law!
Zh: “意思是抓到你了吧，死变态。我就是法律！”

En: Well, I can certainly understand *that*. The man loosens his tie knot and appears to relax slightly. "That hanging was a surreal play. Everything since then has been too..."
Zh: “呃，我确实能理解*那个*。”男人松开领结，好像稍微放松了些。“那场绞刑是一出超现实主义戏剧。那之后发生的一切也是如此……”

En: I won't stand for this. His left eye starts twitching -- as the last warning. "What's your name? I'll have your badge!"
Zh: “这我可不能忍受。”他的左眼开始抽搐——就像在发出最后的警告。“你叫什么名字？把你的警徽拿出来！”

En: *I* don't even have my badge -- so spin on this! (Give him the finger.)
Zh: “*我*连警徽都没有——所以给你这个吧！”（朝他竖起中指。）

En: I'm in-between names right now.
Zh: “我现在还游离在各种名字中间呢。”

En: My name's Harry.
Zh: “我叫哈里。”

En: Lieutenant-yefreitor Harrier Du Bois.
Zh: “荣誉警督哈里尔·杜博阿。”

En: Harrier Du Bois, and don't forget it.
Zh: “哈里尔·杜博阿，你可别忘了。”

En: You can just call me *The Law*.
Zh: “你可以直接叫我*法律*。”

En: I am Detective Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau, at your service.
Zh: “我是拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·科斯泰，乐意为你效劳。”

En: I *prefer* to be called Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “我*更喜欢*别人叫我龙舌兰日落。”

En: Around these parts I go by Tequila... Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “在这一带我叫龙舌兰……龙舌兰日落。”

En: Very well, *Harry*. You're a disgrace to the RCM. Get out of my sight, this instant! He points at the door.
Zh: “很好，*哈里*。你是RCM的耻辱。滚出我的视线，马上！”他指向大门。

En: Who were 'they'? Can you describe them?
Zh: “‘他们’是谁？能描述一下吗？”

En: How many of them were there?
Zh: “他们有多少人？”

En: I couldn't see their faces well, and there were quite a few of them. But they were very loud and very... Martinaise... He pauses, looking for the right wording.
Zh: “我看不清他们的脸，有好几个人。但是他们很吵，非常……马丁内斯。”他停顿了一下，寻找正确的词汇。

En: Let's just say that the labouring classes can get rather *expressive* with their profanities.
Zh: “这么说吧，劳动阶级的脏话可以变得特别*有表现力*。”

En: What happened next? (Proceed.)
Zh: “然后呢？”（继续。）

En: I couldn't tell you exactly. Less than ten. Maybe eight?
Zh: “我也说不明白。少于10个。也许是8个？”

En: The lieutenant sends you a sharp look at the mention of that number.
Zh: 一提到那个数字，警督就向你抛来一个犀利的眼神。

En: Sound familiar? That matches the prints you observed at the scene.
Zh: 听起来很熟悉？跟你在现场观察到的脚印数量是相符的。

En: Were any of them the Giant of Koko Nur?
Zh: “他们中间有没有像科科努尔巨人那种体型的？”

En: Were any of them huge? Like 200 kilograms huge?
Zh: “他们中间有没有个头特别大的？比如200多公斤那种？”

En: Did any of them look like drummers?
Zh: “他们中间有没有看起来像鼓手的？”

En: What happened next?
Zh: “接下来发生了什么？”

En: Sounds like the victim *was* unconscious. Or at least incapacitated. Well done, detective. He nods to you.
Zh: “听起来被害人*当时*是不省人事的。或者至少丧失了行为能力。干得好，警探。”他朝你点点头。

En: Drummers? Why... no... but then, I don't know what a drummer is *supposed* to look like.
Zh: “鼓手？为什么……没有……不过话又说回来，我不知道一个鼓手*应该*长什么样。”

En: No, there were no *mythological giants*, I'm afraid. They were all *quite* human, so far as I could tell.\n
Zh: “不，恐怕没有那种*神话巨人*。据我所知，他们都是特别*正常*的人类。”

En: That's a giant you're describing. No, they were all *quite* human, so far as I could tell.
Zh: “你描述的可是个巨人啊。不，据我所知，他们都是特别*正常*的人类。”

En: I went back inside.
Zh: “我进屋了。”

En: Were you able to see anything from inside?
Zh: “你从里面能看到什么吗？”

En: Wait, so you didn't actually *see* the lynching happen?
Zh: “等等，所以你确实*看见*私刑发生了？”

En: Officer, the yard was pitch black. There was nothing to see. But I could still hear their voices. They were threatening to kill that poor man.
Zh: “警官，院子里一片漆黑。几乎什么都看不见。但我还是能听到他们的声音。他们威胁说要杀掉那个可怜的男人。”

En: Were they men, women?
Zh: “他们是男人，还是女人？”

En: Were they Kojkos? Kipts? Those funny squinting men?
Zh: “他们是克吉克？吉皮特？那些搞笑的斜眼人？”

En: What ethnicity were they?
Zh: “他们是什么种族的？”

En: What happened next? (Proceed.)
Zh: “然后呢？”（继续。）

En: All men, I presume. But again, I couldn't see very clearly.
Zh: “我推测，全都是男人。不过再说一次，我看的不是很清楚。”

En: I believe they were mostly white, though I believe I saw two Areopagites among them. And I am quite certain that one spoke with a Mesque accent.
Zh: “应该大部分都是白人，虽然我相信在其中看见两个艾瑞奥帕吉特人。而且我很肯定其中一个是梅斯克口音。”

En: Those funny... Oh. He blinks. "Officer, that kind of language is unbecoming of an officer of the RCM..."
Zh: “那些好笑的……哦。”他眨眨眼。“警官，这种话可不适合从一个RCM警官的口里说出来……”

En: Yes, back inside. Keep yourself safe from the killing.
Zh: 是的，进屋去了。这样自己就安全了，不会被杀掉。

En: Are you sure at least *one* of them wasn't a woman?
Zh: “你确定里面*一个*女人都没有吗？”

En: It's possible, officer, but I cannot say with certainty. It was very dark, you must remember.
Zh: “有这个可能，警官，但我不太确定。你应该记得，那时真的很黑。”

En: Hmmm. But we're fairly certain the lady driver was present... The lieutenant flips through his notes.
Zh: “嗯。不过我们非常确认那个女司机在场……”警督浏览着自己的笔记。

En: That doesn't quite comport with what you know. Don't forget the lady driver!
Zh: 这跟你掌握的信息不太一致。别忘了那个女司机！

En: Well, that's the strangest part, officer. *Nothing* happened. It was oddly quiet for a public lynching.
Zh: “呃，那才是最奇怪的地方，警官。什么也*没发生*。对于一次公开处刑来说，实在是安静地奇怪。”

En: What do you mean 'nothing happened'? They lynched a guy!
Zh: “你说‘什么也没发生’是什么意思？他们可是私下处死了一个人啊！”

En: You're right, that does seem strange.
Zh: “你说得对，确实挺奇怪的。”

En: Why didn't you call the RCM?
Zh: “你为什么没打给RCM？”

En: The man wipes his glasses. "You're right, of course. That is what one is supposed to do in such circumstances. I was simply in shock..."
Zh: 男人擦拭着自己的眼镜。“当然了，你说得对。在这种情况下，确实应该这么做。我只是太震惊了……”

En: Eventually their shouts died down, and that was all. There were no gunshots, no celebratory shouts, no anything.
Zh: “最终他们的叫喊声逐渐消失，就这些了。没有枪声，没有庆祝的欢呼，什么也没有。”

En: About what time was all this happening, approximately?
Zh: “这一切大概是什么时间发生的？”

En: Can you at least tell me when it happened?
Zh: “至少能告诉我发生时间吗？”

En: All I can say is that it was late.
Zh: “我只能说很晚。”

En: No, I didn't see the corpse until the following day.
Zh: “没有，直到第二天我才看见尸体。”

En: My watch... He looks at his watch, as though noticing it for the first time. "Yes, now I remember -- it was thirty minutes past midnight, give or take."
Zh: “我的表……”他看着自己的手表，像是第一次注意到它一样。“是的，现在我记起来了——是12:30分，可能有点小误差。”

En: You didn't check your watch?
Zh: “你没看自己的手表吗？”

En: So let me get this straight. You didn't actually witness the hanging itself, did you?
Zh: “那我就直接这么说吧。你没有真的亲眼目睹绞刑，对吗？”

En: I'm afraid I don't have anything else to add.
Zh: “恐怕我没什么可以补充的了。”

En: It seems this wasn't the 'break' you were hoping for...
Zh: “看来这不是你期望的‘突破’……”

En: I think we have everything we need, the lieutenant says, closing his notebook. "Thank you for talking to us, Mr. Villedrouin."
Zh: “我想我们已经拿到所有必要的信息了，”警督合上笔记本说到。“感谢你的配合，维尔德鲁安先生。”

En: Of course, anything I can do to assist the RCM.
Zh: “别客气，只要能帮上RCM就好。”

En: Well, that was a waste of time.
Zh: “呃，这是在浪费时间。”

En: Those are all my questions. Thanks for your time.
Zh: “我的问题问完了。感谢你能抽出时间。”

En: I'm sorry I couldn't be of more assistance.
Zh: “很抱歉，我没办法提供更多帮助了。”

En: Ah, that clears everything up.
Zh: “啊，这样一切都清楚了。”

En: Which is essential for maintaining *ze social stabilité*...
Zh: “对于维持*社会稳定*来说，这是必不可少的……”

En: Take your daily bread, *par example*. Too much inflation, bread becomes too expensive; too much deflation, it becomes too cheap for bakers to produce...
Zh: “就拿你每天吃的面包来*举例*吧。通货膨胀太严重，面包就会变贵；通货紧缩太严重，面包就会变得太便宜，面包师就不想做了……”

En: Okay, I think that's all I needed to know.
Zh: “好吧，我要知道的就是这些。”

En: This is *fascinating*, but I wanted to ask you something else.
Zh: “这真是*太吸引人*了，不过我还想问你点别的问题。”

En: He pats his pockets. "But, oh! I don't have my leaflets on me today. That's too bad. You can always call our information line. Making information available is part of the Moralintern's commitment to transparency."
Zh: 他拍了拍自己的口袋。“不过，哦！今天我没把传单带在身上。太可惜了。你可以随时拨打我们的咨询电话。公开信息是国际道德伦理委员会恪守透明度承诺的一部分。”

En: A sound monetary policy is *essential* for addressing *uncertainty*. Stability is the *raison d'être* of the Moralintern. It's the reason why I identify as a moralist.
Zh: “稳健的货币政策对于处理*不确定问题*来说，是*必不可少*的。稳定性是国际道德伦理委员会*存在的理由*。这就是我认为自己是一名道德家的原因。”

En: Not boring, my friend. *Responsible*.
Zh: “不无聊，我的朋友。这叫*负责*。”

En: My friend, that is only because you have never known the *alternative*. And I pray you never do.
Zh: “我的朋友，这是因为你永远也不知道*取舍*。而且我也祈祷你永远不会知道。”

En: Yeah, that shit sounds like a snooze-fest.
Zh: “是啊，那些鬼话听起来真的无聊死了。”

En: That's nice of them.
Zh: “他们挺好的啊。”

En: Oh, yes. Sur-la-Clef is *mega-benevolent* to its darlings.
Zh: “是啊。苏拉菲对于自己的宠儿来说，是*非常仁慈*的。”

En: What is it about this 'benevolence' that makes you break out in sweat and want to run for the hills?
Zh: 那这份*仁慈*又为什么会让你汗流浃背，想要逃之夭夭呢？

En: *Darlings*? That can't be an official designation.
Zh: *宠儿*？这可绝对不是官方说辞。

En: (Turn to Kim.) "Lieutenant, are *you* a moralist?"
Zh: （转向金。）“警督，*你*是道德家吗？”

En: Hmm, me? I... uh...
You've managed to catch the lieutenant off guard, but only for a moment. He quickly recomposes himself.
Zh: 你成功地给了警督一个措手不及，不过只有那么一小会儿。他很快恢复了镇静。

En: I'm a lieutenant of the RCM, dedicated to maintaining law and order in Revachol.
Zh: “作为RCM的一名警督，我致力于维护瑞瓦肖的社会治安。”

En: A very moralist answer, the man nods.
Zh: “一个很道德家的答案，”男人点点头。

En: The lieutenant is practised in the art of putting on a show for one's superiors.
Zh: 在上级面前作秀这一套，警督非常娴熟。

En: The lieutenant clears his throat. "Of course, the detective's personal views do not represent the views of the RCM."
Zh: 警督清了清喉咙。“当然，一名警探的个人观点不能代表RCM。”

En: The lieutenant clears his throat. "Of course, the detective's personal views do not represent the views of the RCM."
Zh: 警督清了清喉咙。“当然，一名警探的个人观点不能代表RCM。”

En: God, yes. Sweet standardisation. The backbone of rationality -- and commerce.
Zh: 天啦，没错。甜美的标准化。它是合理性——还有商业的支柱。

En: Okay, but like, what does it stand for?
Zh: “好吧，但是它代表什么呢？”

En: Why, it stands for progress and stability, like the Moralintern as a whole.
Zh: “哈，它代表了进步和稳定，就像整个国际道德伦理委员会一样。”

En: Sure, fine. What's this next level you were talking about?
Zh: “当然，好吧。那你说的更上一层楼又是什么意思？”

En: No, what do the *letters* stand for?
Zh: “不，我是问这些*字母*代表什么？”

En: It's been such a wild, *extraordinary* success thus far. We are very excited to take it to the next level...
Zh: “到目前为止，这是一次疯狂而又*非凡*的成功。我们都很兴奋，想要让它更上一层楼……”

En: You don't even hear the words I'm saying, do you?
Zh: “其实你根本就没听见我在说什么，是吧？”

En: What are you doing in his apartment by yourself?
Zh: “你一个人在他的公寓做什么？”

En: Enjoying the view? There's a dead body hanging in the tree!
Zh: “欣赏风景？那棵树上可是挂了个死人啊！”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "A dead body we still need to get down, by the way."
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“顺便说一句，那具尸体还等着我们弄下来呢。”

En: A chill runs down your spine as you envision a half-dozen people in professional attire standing around a chair awkwardly pretending to be waiting for a motor-bus. It's neither funny nor creative.
Zh: 6个身穿职业装的人尴尬地站在椅子周围，假装在等一辆公共汽車……一想到这里，你感觉一阵寒意顺着你的脊椎往下窜。这一点也不好笑，也没有什么创造性。

En: Yeah, I'm what they call a *master 'bater*. (Brush some invisible dirt off your shoulder.)
Zh: “没错，我就是大家口中的*辩论大师*。”（拍掉肩膀上看不见的灰尘。）

En: Oh! How wonderful. I've heard it's *oodles* of fun. Not that I have time to take up new hobbies myself...
Zh: “哦！真是太棒了。我听说它真的*非常*有趣。并不是说我有时间培养新的爱好……”

En: There's a joke here, and you know what it is.
Zh: 这里有个玩笑，而且你也知道是什么。

En: C'mon. It's so juvenile. Besides, there's no way this man is going to get it.
Zh: 拜托，这也太幼稚了吧。再说，这个男人是不可能做到的。

En: That's why it's good.
Zh: 这就是它为什么这么好的原因。

En: See? He didn't get it. Good job, jokemeister.
Zh: 看见了吗？他没明白。干得漂亮，笑话大师。

En: As though you weren't envious enough of the boy as-is.
Zh: 就好像你对这个男孩的现状还不够妒忌似得。

En: You're a disgrace to the RCM. Out with you, this instant! He points at the door.
Zh: “你是RCM的耻辱。给我滚出去，马上！”他指着大门。

En: You're doing me no service! Get out, this instant! He points at the door.
Zh: “你根本就没有为我服务！出去，马上！”他指着大门。

En: What kind of name is that? You're a disgrace. Get out, this instant! He points at the door.
Zh: “那是什么名字？你真是太丢人现眼了。出去，马上！”他指着大门。

En: You'll be in-between jobs soon! Get out, this instant! He points at the door.
Zh: “你很快就要重新找工作了！出去，马上！”他指着大门。

En: You're a disgrace to the law! Get out, this instant! He points at the door.
Zh: “你是法律的耻辱！出去，马上！”他指着大门。

En: In all my years I've never seen a lieutenant-yefreitor behave this way. You're a disgrace! Get out, this instant! He points at the door.
Zh: “我活了这么多年，从没见过一个荣誉警督这么做。你实在是太丢人现眼了！给我出去，马上！”他指着大门。

En: Very well, *Mr. Du Bois*. You're a disgrace to the RCM. Get out, this instant! He points at the door.
Zh: “很好，*杜博阿先生*。你是RCM的耻辱。给我出去，马上！”他指着大门。

En: Good god, detective. One more stunt like that and they'll have you institutionalized.
Zh: “天啦，警探。要是再来一次那种表现，他们就要把你送到精神病院了。”

En: Now let's go, before you get us into any *more* trouble.
Zh: “我们还是走吧，免得你搞出*更多*麻烦来。”

En: Remember that guy, Ace Ulsterberg? the redheaded officer asks. He leans against the doorframe while his bald companion is hunched over an open filing drawer...
Zh: “还记得那个家伙吗，埃斯·乌尔斯腾伯？”红发警官问到。他正依靠在门框上，他的秃头搭档正蜷缩在一个打开的文件柜前……

En: Hmm, the novelty dicemaker also mentioned that she didn't hear any signs of a struggle.
Zh: “嗯，那个骰子匠人也提过她没有听到任何挣扎的迹象。”

En: That comports with the dicemaker's testimony as well.
Zh: 这跟骰子匠人的证言是相符的。

En: Of course, I'm sorry I couldn't be the 'break' you were looking for. Good luck with the investigation.
Zh: “当然，很抱歉，我不是你正在寻找的‘突破口’。祝你调查顺利。”

En: I have nothing to say to you. Get out of my sight.
Zh: “我跟你没什么好说的。滚出我的视线。”

En: The lieutenant lets out a long, nasal sigh.
Zh: 警督发出一声悠长的、带着鼻音的叹息声。

En: Oh? That's unusual. The Oranjese are not the kind of people you associate with heinous crimes. It's such an upstanding and responsible country...
Zh: “哦？那还真是不太寻常。奥兰治人一般不会跟那种跟穷凶极恶的犯罪联系在一起。那是个特别可敬而又负责的国家……”

En: Well certainly one cannot rely simply on generalizations -- that would go against our commitment to individual freedoms...
Zh: “的确，我们不能单纯地依赖一般情况——那样违背了我们对个体自由的承诺……”

En: That's not how I would describe this guy.
Zh: “我不会这样描述那家伙。”

En: Yeah, that's all I wanted to say.
Zh: “是啊，我也想说这个。”

En: And, of course, any loss of life is a tragedy. The man seems to observe a moment of silence. "So tell me, what have you learned about the victim?"
Zh: “当然，任何生命的逝去都是悲剧。”男人好像默哀了片刻。“所以告诉我，你对受害者有多少了解？”

En: After he was discharged from the military he joined a mercenary outfit named Krenel.\n
Zh: “他自愿退伍之后，加入了一个名叫科奈尔的雇佣兵组织。”

En: After he was discharged from the military he joined a group of mercenaries.
Zh: “他自愿退伍之后，加入了一群雇佣兵。”

En: As a merc, he killed a lot of people on the Semenine Islands for the sake of the Oranjese pharmaceutical industry.
Zh: “作为一个雇佣兵，他为了奥兰治制药工业的利益在塞美尼群岛杀了很多人。”

En: As a merc, he killed a lot of people on the Semenine Islands to advance Oranjese business interests.
Zh: “作为一个雇佣兵，他在塞美尼群岛和幽灵岛杀了很多人，以此来增加在奥兰治的商业价值。”

En: He liked to get high and brag about war crimes he'd committed.
Zh: “他喜欢嗑药，吹嘘自己犯下的战争罪行。”

En: That's all. That's who he was.
Zh: “就这些。他就是一个这样的人。”

En: His name was Lely and he was 42 years old.
Zh: “他的名字叫莱利，今年42岁。”

En: His name was Lely.
Zh: “他的名字叫莱利。”

En: Ah, what a shame. A man cut down in his prime earning years.
Zh: “啊，真可惜。一个男人在赚钱的黄金年月里倒下了。”

En: Lely? That's an interesting name.
Zh: “莱利？这可真是个有趣的名字。”

En: Lely wasn't his real name. It was a *nom de guerre*.
Zh: “莱利不是他的真名，是他的*假名*。”

En: His earning potential isn't really important. What matters is that he was a mercenary.
Zh: “他的收入潜力并不重要。重要的是，他是个雇佣兵。”

En: His name's not really important. What matters is that he was a mercenary.
Zh: “他的名字其实真的不重要。重要的是，他是个雇佣兵。”

En: Ah, he was a soldier?
Zh: “啊，他以前当过兵？”

En: Aha, a *condottiere*, the man says, as though describing a character from a fantasy novel. "Yes, it's hard to believe they still exist."
Zh: “啊哈，一个*雇佣兵*，”男人念叨着，就像在描述幻想小说中的一个人物。“是的，很难相信他们仍然存在。”

En: A mercenary.
Zh: “一个雇佣兵。”

En: The man shakes his head solemnly. "A great shame, yes. Colonialism is a dark spectre in Oranje's past..."
Zh: 男人郑重地摇摇头。“确实非常可惜。殖民主义是奥兰治过去的一个黑色幽灵……”

En: Who's talking about the past? It's still going on!
Zh: “谁说已经过去了？它仍然继续！”

En: (Shake your head too.) "History is very complicated and rarely simple."
Zh: （自己也跟着摇头。）“历史非常复杂，从来都不简单。”

En: (Shrug.) "What's a bunch of dead kipts where there's fortune and glory?"
Zh: （耸耸肩。）“如果有财富和荣耀的话，死几个吉皮特又怎么样？”

En: ...which is *why* the work of the Moralintern is so critical. It is our great bulwark against another century of bloodshed.
Zh: “……这就是国际道德伦理委员会的工作如此重要的*原因*。它是我们抵御另一场世纪大杀戮的坚实壁垒。”

En: Officer, please! The Moralintern is dedicated to fostering a more tolerant and pluralistic society. There's no room for racial slurs there.
Zh: “警官，拜托！国际道德伦理委员会致力于铸造一个更加宽容和多元化的社会。那里可没留下种族歧视的空间。”

En: That is extremely unfortunate. Yes, it's regrettable that this practice has a history in certain Occidental nations, even highly advanced members of EPIS...
Zh: “那样真是极大的不幸。是的，令人遗憾的是某些奥西登国家的历史中确实有过这种做法，甚至还有EPIS里某些高度发展的成员国……”

En: But rest assured: The Oranjese government has committed to producing a timetable that would lay out a path to transition to a professional army. Soon there'll be no need for mercenaries like this poor man.
Zh: “但是请放心：奥兰治政府已经决心制定出一个时间表，规划出一条将其过渡为职业军队的道路。很快就不需要像这个可怜男人一样的雇佣兵了。”

En: What kind of a timetable are we talking about here?
Zh: “我们这里说的时间表是什么样的？”

En: Oh, well that solves that, then.
Zh: “哦，那样确实能解决问题。”

En: The man nods. "See? That's the kind of progress that the Moralintern makes possible."
Zh: 男人点点头。“明白了吧？当国际道德伦理委员会能给我们带来的进步。”

En: Oh, it's hard to put an exact date on it. I believe the proposal for the timetable is due to be completed around '60 or '61.
Zh: “哦，很难说出个具体日期。我相信时间表的提案应该会在60或者61年完成。”

En: What? That's like a decade from now!
Zh: “什么？那差不多都要10年了吧！”

En: I guess that's better than nothing.
Zh: “我猜这样总比什么都不做的好。”

En: Extremely distasteful behaviour. I cannot condone either drug use or needless boasting...
Zh: “极度令人不快的行为。不管是滥用毒品还是毫不必要的吹嘘，都是我绝对不能宽恕的……”

En: Suffice it to say, that is *not* how we do things in Sur-la-Clef.
Zh: “一言蔽之，这*不是*我们在苏拉菲的做事方式。”

En: As I said, the loss of any life is terrible, no matter who the person may have been...
Zh: “我也说过了，任何生命的逝去都是可怕的，不管这个人曾经是谁……”

En: I do hope you're able to bring his killer or killers to justice. We must show that the rule of law still applies... even in Revachol.
Zh: “我希望你能将杀害他的凶手绳之以法。我们必须证明法治仍然适用……甚至是在瑞瓦肖这种地方。”

En: What if I told you there's a fugitive from the Moralintern in Martinaise?
Zh: “如果我告诉你，马丁内斯有一个国际道德伦理委员会的逃犯呢？”

En: Hypothetically sssspeaking, you might add.
Zh: 您可以加上一句，说不定。

En: ...hypothetically speaking.
Well, I would say that is very bad, indeed. Not super.
Zh: “呃，那样确实挺糟糕的。不过也没特别糟。”

En: What sort of a fugitive are we talking about here?
Zh: “我们说的是哪种类型的逃犯？”

En: Pretty serious, like corporate espionage-type stuff.
Zh: “挺严重的，比如企业间谍之类的。”

En: I think they may have broken into some places.
Zh: “我认为他们可能闯入了某些地方。”

En: I'm pretty sure I saw them littering.
Zh: “我很肯定，我看见他们在乱丢垃圾。”

En: That is *extremely serious*!
Zh: “那就真的是*极其严重*了！”

En: This fugitive must be turned over to the ICP immediately. Have you apprehended them already?
Zh: “这名逃犯肯定会立即被移交给ICP。你已经逮捕他们了吗？”

En: Oh! Hahaha. A good joke. Very funny. Littering is of course a local offence that need not concern the Moralintern.
Zh: “哦！哈哈哈。这笑话不错，很有趣。乱丢垃圾是一种地方性的犯罪行为，犯不着让国际道德伦理委员会担心。”

En: Actually, I think it was way worse than that. Like, corporate espionage.
Zh: “其实，我觉得比那个要糟糕的多。比如，企业间谍。”

En: Cool. I guess we'll handle it from here.
Zh: “赞。那从这里开始，就交给我们来处理了。”

En: Hmmm, that could be *quite* bad, depending on what sort of place you're talking about.
Zh: “嗯，可能*挺*糟糕的，取决于你说的是哪种地方。”

En: What if I told you it was the international headquarters of a major corporation?
Zh: “如果我告诉你，是一家大企业的国际总部呢？”

En: Probably like a Frittte or something.
Zh: “可能类似弗利多或者其他之类的。”

En: He won't tell you how to do your job, but a good police officer knows when to listen to his partner.
Zh: 他不会告诉你该怎么做事，但是一个好警官知道什么时候应该听搭档的话。

En: Very good. Now was there anything else you'd like to discuss?
Zh: “很好。你还有什么想讨论的吗？”

En: Ah, well. Burglary is a serious violation of the right to property, but it is a *local* matter. Thus, the Moralintern need not get involved.
Zh: “呃，好吧。入室盗窃是对所有权的严重侵害，但这只是*地方*事务。因此，国际道德伦理委员会不需要介入。”

En: Now, was there something else you wanted to discuss?
Zh: “现在，你还有什么想讨论的吗？”

En: Pin it on someone else.
Zh: 嫁祸给其他人。

En: Abundance of suspects.
Zh: 嫌疑人众多。

En: Embarrassed by a child.
Zh: 被一个小孩羞辱。

En: Dresscode: Man of charms.
Zh: 着装要求：万人迷

En: Her name is Katarzine Alasije and she's already in police custody.\n
Zh: “她的名字叫卡特琳·艾拉洁，已经被警方拘留了。”

En: Her name is Klaasje and she's already in police custody.
Zh: “她的名字叫卡拉洁，已经被警方拘留了。”

En: I meant that as a hypothetical. I don't actually know about any corporate espionage.
Zh: “刚才是我假设的。我不知道什么企业间谍的事。”

En: It's Evrart Claire, the head of the Dockworkers' Union.
Zh: “是艾弗拉特·克莱尔，码头工人工会的会长。”

En: Now's your chance, ssssire. Which of your enemies would you most like to make trouble for?
Zh: 现在您的机会来了，大人。您最希望给哪个敌人制造麻烦呢？

En: Of all the names stored in the rusty filing cabinet that is your hippocampus, the one you pull out is...
Zh: 在你海马体里所有生锈的档案柜中，你抽出的那一份名字是……

En: What about that gremlin child from behind the Whirling-in-Ragssss?
Zh: 那褴褛飞旋背后的那个熊孩子呢？

En: Cuno. He's this punk-ass kid who hangs out behind the Whirling-in-Rags. He's your guy.
Zh: “坤诺，一个经常在褴褛飞旋背后瞎晃的小阿飞。他就是你要找的人。”

En: There's this ugly-ass kid who hangs out behind the Whirling-in-Rags. He's your guy.
Zh: “褴褛飞旋背后经常有一个小屁孩在瞎晃。他就是你要找的人。”

En: His name is Garte, and he's working at the Whirling-in-Rags disguised as a cafeteria manager.
Zh: “他的名字叫加尔特，伪装成了褴褛飞旋的餐厅经理。”

En: You'll find him hanging out by the roundabout. He's disguised as a really racist lorry driver.
Zh: “他经常在环岛附近出现。伪装身份是一名真正的种族主义货車司机。”

En: He'll tell you his name is Raphael, but his real name is Herve, so make sure you call him that.
Zh: “他会告诉你自己叫拉斐尔，但是他的真名是赫维，一定要记得这么称呼他。”

En: There are a bunch of speedfreaks lurking around the church. They confessed while high on drugs.
Zh: “教堂附近有一群鬼鬼祟祟的思必得瘾君子。他们嗑药嗑嗨了之后就会坦白。”

En: Very good. What else then?
Zh: “很好。还有什么吗？”

En: A bunch of *what*, detective?
Zh: “一群*什么*，警探？”

En: Kipts. You know.
Zh: “吉皮特。你懂得。”

En: Nothing, sorry.
Zh: “没什么，抱歉。”

En: Yes, well. As I was saying: Colonialism has left a dark legacy in many places, one we must move past, but also never forget.
Zh: “是的，好吧。我也说过了：殖民主义在很多地方留下了黑暗的遗赠，我们必须越过它继续前进，但也永远不能忘记。”

En: There seems to be plenty of room for proxy wars waged by multinational corporations, though.
Zh: 不过，跨国公司发起的代理人战争似乎还有不少空间。

En: That's because they're all a bunch of squares who like to sit around with their fingers up each other's assholes.
Zh: 那是因为他们全都是一群老古董，喜欢围坐在一起，把手指塞进对方的后门。

En: Ah, that's right. You did say this was all just a *hypothetical* scenario...
Zh: “啊，没错。你确实说过这全都是*假设*的情节……”

En: Whew! For a moment there I was concerned that we had *quite* the situation on our hands...
Zh: “呼！有那么一会儿，我还担心我们手上要处理*相当不得了*的状况呢……”

En: What do you mean 'as a hypothetical'? I don't mean to imply anything, officer, but there's no chance you're *harbouring* a fugitive, is there?
Zh: “你说‘假设’是什么意思？我不想暗示什么，警官，但是你应该没有在*窝藏*逃犯吧？”

En: A moment, if you don't mind, sir. The lieutenant pulls you aside.
Zh: “请等一下，如果你不介意的话，先生。”警督把你拉到一边。

En: You do realize what you're doing, don't you? He whispers to you, *sotto voce*.
Zh: “你明白自己在做什么吧？”他*低声*地问你。

En: Yeah, I'm turning over a dangerous fugitive.
Zh: “是的，我正在移交一名非常危险的逃犯。”

En: Oh, shit, Klaasje could really get in real trouble if I tell him about her, huh?
Zh: “该死，如果我把她的事告诉他，她真的会有大麻烦的，是吗？”

En: *Very* real trouble...
Zh: “*非常*大的麻烦……”

En: The lieutenant looks you straight in the eye, trying to gauge your seriousness.
Zh: 警督直视你的双眼，试着衡量你到底有多认真。

En: I won't tell you how to do your job. But remember, this is an RCM matter. Make sure you weigh your next words carefully.
Zh: “我不会告诉你该怎么做事。但是要记住，这是RCM的事务。一定要仔细斟酌接下来要说的话。”

En: When you turn back the man is looking at you expectantly.
Zh: 你转回身，男人正期待着看着你。

En: A what... a *child*? He seems confused at first, then disappointed.
Zh: “什么……一个*孩子*？”他一开始有些困惑，接着有些失望。

En: As a common lorry driver, you say? Interesting. Are you sure?
Zh: “你说，一个普通的货車司机？有点意思。你确定吗？”

En: Much as I'm inclined to believe you, I'm not sure my colleagues at the ICP will...
Zh: “虽然我很想相信你，但是我不确定ICP的同事会不会信……”

En: Evrart Claire? Surely you're mistaken.
Zh: “艾弗拉特·克莱尔？你肯定是搞错了。”

En: What do you mean? Have you *met* Evrart?
Zh: “什么意思？你*见过*艾弗拉特吗？”

En: He's already planning to go to war with Wild Pines. It's definitely the kind of thing he'd do.
Zh: “他已经打算跟野松公司开战了。他绝对能做出这种事情。”

En: Besides, in certain less-advanced nations the Moralintern may defer to local custom...
Zh: “再说了，在某些欠发达国家，国际道德伦理委员会可能会尊重当地的习俗……”

En: I'll make note of your claims in my report, but I'm sorry to say it's likely to end up in the back of a filing cabinet somewhere in Mirova, unread and forgotten.
Zh: “我会在报告中记下你的主张，不过我非常抱歉的说，它最后很可能会被丢在米诺瓦某处的档案柜后面，没人看，也没人记得。”

En: He may not look like an corporate spy, but that's what makes him so effective.
Zh: “他看起来可能不像是企业间谍，但这也是效果拔群的原因。”

En: The lieutenant makes a strange sound that he quickly smothers.
Zh: 警督发出一个奇怪的声音，然后又很快咽了回去。

En: Was that... did the lieutenant just *snort*?
Zh: 难道……警督刚刚*哼哼*了？

En: A cafeteria manager, you say? How strange. Normally fugitives don't take on such *public* roles.
Zh: “你是说，一个餐厅经理？

En: He's extremely clever like that. Careful, though, he'll also try to convince you that you owe him money.
Zh: “他这样做非常聪明。不过要小心，他会尝试让你相信你欠他钱。”

En: A drug-addled *spy-ring*! That sounds rather *strange*, don't you think?
Zh: “一个吸毒成瘾的*特务组织*！听起来实在*太奇怪*了，你不觉得吗？”

En: Not nearly as strange as the anodic dance music they're into.
Zh: “跟他们喜欢的阳极舞曲相比，根本算不上奇怪。”

En: Drugs make people do *crazy* things sometimes, like steal corporate secrets.
Zh: “毒品有时会让人做出*疯狂*的事情，比如窃取企业机密。”

En: I *have*. The only thing Evrart is a fugitive from is the *exercise machine*...
Zh: “我*见过*。艾弗拉特唯一逃避的只有*健身器械*……”

En: No, no, no. Evrart would have no need to dirty his hands in such a way...
Zh: “不不不，艾弗拉特没必要像这样脏了自己的手……”

En: He was a motherfucker and a killer.
Zh: “他是个混账，也是个杀手。”

En: Officer, you shouldn't speak ill of the dead!
Zh: “警官，你不应该说死人的坏话！”

En: It's okay, he told me so.
Zh: “没事的，他这么告诉我的。”

En: You're right. That was disrespectful of me.
Zh: “你说得对，我实在是太失礼了。”

En: He... so you spoke with the victim *before* he died?
Zh: “他……所以说在被害人去世*之前*，你跟他说过话？”

En: No, this was after he died.
Zh: “不，是在他去世之后。”

En: Yeah, it was a wild coincidence. Let's move on, though.
Zh: “没错，这确实是个疯狂的巧合。不过我们还是先继续吧。”

En: The man gives the lieutenant a nervous look.
Zh: 男人紧张地看了警督一眼。

En: The lieutenant raises his eyebrows as though he'd stopped paying attention.
Zh: 警督扬起眉头，好像已经不再注意了。

En: Your partner's doing you a favour by pretending not to have heard your insane ramblings.
Zh: 你的搭档假装没有听见你的疯言疯语，这是在帮你。

En: I... see. Well, you must be a very thorough detective. So what else did you learn?
Zh: “我……明白了。好吧，你一定是个很周密的警探。所以你还了解到什么信息？”

En: The man seems slightly relieved, as though he's just narrowly survived a perilous bend in the road.
Zh: 男人似乎有些放松，就好像刚从路上一个危险的拐角死里逃生。

En: He was a poor foster kid before the Oranjese government put him in a military academy.
Zh: “在奥兰治政府把他送入军事学院前，他只是一个可怜的寄养儿童。”

En: He was a poor kid before the Oranjese government put him in a military academy.
Zh: “在奥兰治政府把他送入军事学院前，他只是一个可怜的小孩。”

En: The man nods. "It's the responsibility of any good welfare state to take care of its most unfortunate citizens."
Zh: 男人点点头。“任何高福利国家都有责任照顾那些最不幸的公民。”

En: They turned him into a psychopathic killing machine!
Zh: “他们把他变成了一台精神错乱的杀人机器！”

En: Yeah, he should have been grateful for the help.
Zh: “是啊，他应该感激自己得到的帮助。”

En: They gave him a successful *career*. The man holds up a finger as though he's made a brilliant point.
Zh: “他们给了他一份成功的*事业*。”男人举起一只手指，仿佛自己提出了一个绝妙的观点。

En: Every one of us has our role to play.
Zh: “我们每个人都有自己的角色要扮演。”

En: YES. Killing was his business and business was *good*.
Zh: 是的。杀人是他的生意，而且这是份*好*差事。

En: Did he love it before they made him do it again and again and again?
Zh: 他们让他一次又一次地杀人之前，他喜欢这样做吗？

En: What else has your investigation uncovered?
Zh: “你在调查过程中还发现了什么？”

En: 'Customs' like blatant, systematic corruption?
Zh: ‘习俗’，比如厚颜无耻的系统性腐败吗？

En: That's a shame.
Zh: “这是一种耻辱。”

En: That's totally fine.
Zh: “其实真的没什么。”

En: Yes, it rather is. As much as I'm a true believer in international institutions, there are times when even I grow frustrated with the bureaucracy...
Zh: “是的，确实如此。就像我这种国际制度的真正信徒，有时也会对官僚机构越来越失望……”

En: You think it's 'fine' that Martinaise is a safe haven for international fugitives? You know, concern for the rule of law is one of the Moralintern's main criteria for admission into EPIS...
Zh: “马丁内斯是国际逃犯的避风港，你觉得这样完全*没问题*吗？你知道吗，对法治的关注是国际道德伦理委员会认可EPIS入会资格的主要衡量标准之一……”

En: Well, no matter. I should thank you for bringing this to our attention. Your efforts have been noted.
Zh: “好吧，没关系的。我应该谢谢你提醒我们注意这一点。你的努力已经被注意到了。”

En: Now, was there anything else you wanted to discuss?
Zh: “现在，你还有什么想讨论的吗？”

En: What proof do you have that this *child* is involved in corporate espionage?
Zh: “你有什么证据能证明这个*孩子*涉及企业间谍行为吗？”

En: He's a ginger. What else do you need to know?
Zh: “他是个红发小鬼，所以还有什么需要知道的吗？”

En: His dad's a major drug kingpin in Martinaise.
Zh: “他的父亲是马丁内斯的大毒枭。”

En: He's been stealing locusts from some cryptozoologists.
Zh: “他一直从神秘动物学家那里偷蝗虫。”

En: He's been throwing rocks at a corpse.
Zh: “他一直朝尸体扔石头。”

En: He used reactionary slurs on my crime scene.
Zh: “他曾经在我的犯罪现场使用过反动的污言秽语。”

En: Selling drugs is certainly unfortunate, but what does that have to do with corporate espionage?
Zh: “贩毒确实很不可取，但是这跟企业间谍有什么关系吗？”

En: Stealing from misguided pseudo-scientists is unfortunate, but what does that have to do with corporate espionage?
Zh: “从误入歧途的伪科学家那里偷东西确实很不可取，但是这跟企业间谍有什么关系吗？”

En: Tsk-tsk. Racial slurs certainly have no place in a pluralistic society, but what does that have to do with corporate espionage?
Zh: “啧啧。种族歧视性发言在多元化的社会肯定没有一席之地，但是这跟企业间谍有什么关系吗？”

En: Corpse desecration is morally repugnant, but what does that have to do with corporate espionage?
Zh: “亵渎尸体在道德上是令人厌恶的，但是这跟企业间谍有什么关系吗？”

En: What do you mean by that, officer?
Zh: “你这话是什么意思，警官？”

En: I'm saying it establishes a *pattern* of criminality. Do I need to get a cork board and some yarn here?
Zh: “我想说他建立了某种犯罪*模式*。我需要在这里架块软木板，再拉点纱线吗？”

En: It's a slippery slope, man... (Make a sliding gesture with your hand.) "Real slippery."
Zh: “这是滑坡谬误……”（用手做出滑动的样子。）“真正的滑坡。”

En: Hmm, that does seem kinda *tenuous* now that you mention it.
Zh: “嗯，听你这么一提，确实好像有点*薄弱*。”

En: There's no way I can submit this report to the ICP -- they'd have my head examined!
Zh: “我是不可能把这份报告交给ICP的——他们会把我拖去做大脑检查的！”

En: *Extremely* tenuous...
Zh: “*极其*薄弱……”

En: I suggest you work on your *investigative skills* a bit more before you go around accusing children of crimes.
Zh: “我建议你再多锻炼一下自己的*侦察技巧*，然后再四处指控儿童犯罪。”

En: Now that that's settled, was there anything else you'd like to discuss?
Zh: “现在这一点也说清楚了，你还有什么想讨论的吗？”

En: Ah, good to hear. Excellent. On behalf of the Moralintern, let me thank you for this service...
Zh: “啊，很高兴听到你这么说。太棒了。我谨此代表国际道德伦理委员会，对你的服务表示感谢……”

En: I'll be sure I make a note of your contributions in my report. Revachol is fortunate to have police officers like you.
Zh: “我肯定会在报告中记下你的贡献。瑞瓦肖有你这样的警官真的很幸运。”

En: You hear that Kim? Revachol's lucky to have me.
Zh: “你听见了吗，金？瑞瓦肖有我很幸运。”

En: Just doing my job, sir.
Zh: “只是做好我的本职工作罢了，先生。”

En: For almighty Revachol.
Zh: “为了伟大的瑞瓦肖。”

En: My name is Charles Villedrouin and I'm an official with the Coalition Government. I work for the Institute of Price Stabilité, on assignment from Sur-la-Clef.
Zh: “我叫查尔斯·维尔德鲁安，是联盟政府的一名官员。我代表苏拉菲为物价稳定研究所工作。”

En: I heard you talking to my friend outside... Very good. Super. I am here to assist you in any way possible. Ask me about the hanging.
Zh: “我听见你在外面跟我的朋友说话……很好。非常好。我会尽一切可能来协助你。问问我绞刑的事吧。”

En: Oh yes, my friend has a great eye for these things. He refuses to tell me where it came from -- it's a mystery. Hehehe. He smiles. "I believe they call this type of frame *industrial*. It's very comfy."
Zh: “是啊，我的朋友对这些东西很有眼光。他拒绝告诉我它的出处——这是个秘密。呵呵呵。”他笑了。“我相信他们把这种类型的框架称为*工业化*。非常的舒适。”

En: Of course, you're turning over a dangerous LAWBREAKER.
Zh: 当然，你正在检举一个危险的违法者。

En: What was that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: I said 'For almighty Revachol' you deaf frog-eater.
Zh: “我说‘为了伟大的瑞瓦肖’，你这个吃青蛙的聋哑人。”

En: Nothing. Just doing my job.
Zh: “没什么。只是做好我的本职工作而已。”

En: What the detective means is that he's proud to serve the RCM.
Zh: “这位警探的意思是，能为RCM效劳，他感到很自豪。”

En: Ah, of course. Carry on then.
Zh: “啊，当然。那就再接再厉吧。”

En: The lieutenant gives you a sharp look. He's bailed you out, again, but he's none too pleased about it.
Zh: 警督狠狠瞪了你一眼。他再次帮你圆了回来，但是对此他一点也不开心。

En: The man nods. "You are a credit to the RCM. If more Revacholians were like you, they might be part of EPIS by now."
Zh: 男人点点头。“你是RCM的光荣。如果能有更多像你一样的瑞瓦肖人，他们现在可能已经是EPIS的成员了。”

En: Somehow the man thinks of this as a compliment rather than an expression of patronizing condescension.
Zh: 不知怎么的，这个男人觉得这是一种恭维，而不是一种纡尊降贵的表现。

En: Now that that's settled, was there anything else you'd like to discuss?
Zh: “那就这样说定了，你还有什么想讨论的吗？”

En: You should have heeded your partner's warning. Now it's too late.
Zh: 你应该留心搭档的警告。现在已经太迟了。

En: We're still looking for the fugitive. We'll report back if we learn anything more.
Zh: “我们还在寻找这位逃犯。如果掌握了更多信息的话，我们会回报的。”

En: I'm still looking for the fugitive. I'll report back if I learn anything more.
Zh: “我还在寻找这位逃犯。如果掌握了更多信息的话，我会回报的。”

En: I'm afraid I must *insist* that you tell me what you know. Or I'll be forced to make a *very* unfavourable report to my superiors at the Moralintern.
Zh: “恐怕我必须*坚持*你把知道的都告诉我。否则我只能被迫向我在国际道德伦理委员会的上司提交一份*非常*不利的报告。”

En: A bullet deftly dodged, ssssire.
Zh: 您巧妙地躲过了一劫，大人。

En: Honour demanded it.
Zh: “这是荣誉的诉求。”

En: 'Customs' like complete disregard for the *Rule of Law*?
Zh: ‘习俗’，比如完全不尊重*法治*的那些人吗？

En: Corporate espionage may technically be legal in *Revachol*, but elsewhere it's a very high-level offence. It violates a number of international agreements, in particular the First La Cherte Accord...
Zh: “从技术层面上讲，企业间谍在*瑞瓦肖*可能是合法的，但在别处却是一种很严重的犯罪行为。它违反了一系列国际协议，特别是第一高价协议……”

En: I understand that you're here to investigate a *murder*, the man says, his face grave, "but I must impress upon you the *seriousness* of this matter..."
Zh: “我能理解，你来这里是为了调查一起*凶杀案*的，”男人一脸严肃地说到，“但是我必须让你明白此类事件的*严重性*……”

En: *Thousands* of corporate secrets may be at stake here. I shudder to think what damage that might do to the international markets...
Zh: “*数千个*企业机密可能正身处险境。我都不敢想象它可能会对国际市场造成怎样的伤害……”

En: The man sighs. "I only pray that the ICP apprehends this fugitive before more corporate secrets are lost..."
Zh: 男人叹了口气。“我只能祈祷ICP能逮捕这位逃犯，以免泄露更多的企业机密……”

En: It's certainly serious, sir. But there's also the question of jurisdiction. The Wayfarer Act places certain *strictures* on what matters the RCM may involve itself in.
Zh: “确实很严重，先生。但是这里还涉及到了司法管辖权的问题。《旅客法令》对RCM可以干涉的问题做出了一定的*限制*。”

En: You're right, of course. What use are protocols if you don't follow them?
Zh: “当然了，你说得对。如果不遵守的话，那协议还有什么用？”

En: Now, was there anything else you wished to discuss?
Zh: “现在，你还有什么想讨论的吗？”

En: I suspect he doesn't know the half of it.
Zh: “我怀疑他了解的情况还不到一半。”

En: Her name is Katarzine Alasije and she's at the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: “她的名字叫卡特琳·艾拉洁，她就在褴褛飞旋。”

En: Her name is Klaasje and she's at the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: “她的名字叫卡拉洁，她就在褴褛飞旋。”

En: Right. That should be enough for the ICP. Very good. On behalf of the Moralintern, let me thank you for this service...
Zh: “好的。这对ICP来说应该够了。很好。我谨此代表国际道德伦理委员会，向你的服务表示感谢……”

En: Now that that's settled, was there anything else you'd like to discuss?
Zh: “那就这样说定了，你还有什么想讨论的吗？”

En: Now that that's settled, was there anything else you'd like to discuss?
Zh: “那就这样说定了，你还有什么想讨论的吗？”

En: Aha, he says, his voice suddenly cool. "A *nationalist*, I see. In other circumstances I might be compelled to report you for *sedition*..."
Zh: “啊哈，”他的声音突然变得很冷静。“一个*民族主义者*，我明白了。在其他情况下，我可能必须举报你发表了*煽动性言论*……”

En: Well, no matter. You've done the Moralintern a great service regardless of your *personal* views...
Zh: “不要紧。不论你的*个人*观点是怎么样的，你都为国际道德伦理委员会做出了巨大的贡献……”

En: Ah, very good, then. Your remark seems to have completely vanished from his mind.
Zh: “啊，那很好啊。”你的言辞好像完全从他的脑海中消失了。

En: What? Are you intimidated by this sissy *bureaucrat*? Mediocre!
Zh: 什么？你被这个娘娘腔的*官僚主义者*恐吓到了吗？太逊了吧！

En: He won't, though, because he's nothing but a sissy *bureaucrat*.
Zh: 但是他不会的，他只是个娘娘腔的*官僚主义者*而已。

En: For some reason he gives you a discount if you trash the cafeteria.
Zh: “出于某种原因，如果你把餐厅弄脏的话，他会给你打折。”

En: Well, that wasn't pretty, but you do seem to have taken the heat off Klaasje, for now.
Zh: 呃，虽然有些狼狈，不过卡拉洁的怀疑似乎是解除了，暂时而已。

En: You're right, sir. I'll be sure to give the detective an overview of proper police procedure. The lieutenant darts a quick glance in your direction.
Zh: “你说得对，先生。我一定会给这位警探好好概述一下警察办事的正常程序。”警督飞快地朝你的方向瞥了一眼。

En: That was a wink. Your good cop/idiot cop routine seems to have done the trick.
Zh: 他向你使了个眼色。你那个唱黑脸红脸的套路好像是奏效了。

En: You're dooming a young woman in her hour of desperation.
Zh: 一个年轻女子正处于绝望之中，而你把她推向了厄运。

En: Stop! Before you do anything, consider the consequences.
Zh: 停下来！在你做任何事之前，应该先考虑一下后果。

En: What do you mean?
Zh: 你什么意思？

En: Have you thought about what this might mean for Klaasje?
Zh: 你有没有想过这对卡拉洁来说意味着什么？

En: Don't concern yourself with the feelings of a LAWBREAKER.
Zh: 不要因为对一个违法者的感情影响到自己。

En: Oh, right. She could get in big trouble if I turn her over to the ICP.
Zh: 哦，没错。如果我把她交给ICP，她一定会遇到大麻烦的。

En: What happens to her is no business of mine.
Zh: 她发生什么跟我无关。

En: The man looks at you expectantly.
Zh: 男人期盼地看着你。

En: It's your call.
Zh: 由你决定了。

En: Turn her in now and you'll never get the chance to feel her sweet young flesh against yours.
Zh: 现在把她供出去的话，你永远也不会有机会感觉到她那具年轻又甜美的肉体了。

En: The lieutenant does his level best to suppress a deep sigh.
Zh: 警督用尽全力，忍住了一个深深的叹息。

En: That's it. She's done for. Au revoir, Klaasje.
Zh: 就这样吧。她完蛋了。再见，卡拉洁。

En: This is a great relief. The sanctity of corporate secrets is a cornerstone of the international order...
Zh: “这真是一个巨大的解脱。企业机密的尊严是国际秩序的奠基石……”

En: The guy who got canned for trying to arrest that diplomat? the bald man asks. "Well, *technically* he got canned for being shit-faced on the job. But yeah, trying to arrest the ambassador from Gottwald *while* shit-faced was probably a contributing factor..."
Zh: “你是说那个企图逮捕外交官而被开除的人吗？”秃头男人问到。“呃，从*技术*层面上讲，他被解雇是因为工作期间烂醉如泥。不过，企图逮捕戈特伍德的大使，*同时*又烂醉如泥，可能是一个影响因素……”

En: The bald man squints at a file, then returns it to the cabinet. A new drawer opens with a grinding screech. He turns back to the redhead in the doorway. "What made you think of him now?"
Zh: 秃头男人斜着眼睛看了会手中的文件，然后又把它塞回档案柜。伴随着一阵尖利刺耳的声音，一个新抽屉打开了。他回头望向门口的红发人。“你怎么会现在想到他的？”

En: Don't know. He just kinda popped into my mind. "Ahem, excuse me, Chester," says a female officer who threads her way into the file room. "Where is old Ace now?" the bald officer asks.
Zh: “不知道。他就是突然从我脑子里蹦出来了。”“呃哼，抱歉，切斯特，”一个女警官慢慢走进档案室。“老埃斯在哪？”秃头警官问到。

En: Here the female officer chimes in: "I believe he's part of a certain bureaucrat's security detail." "No fucking way!" The redhead is dumbfounded. "How'd he pull that off?"
Zh: 就在这时，女警官插话了：“我相信他是某个官僚安保特遣队的一员。”“绝对他妈的不可能！”红发有些慌乱。“他是怎么做到的？”

En: Family connections, I'd imagine? His father-in-law works for the Office of Standards and Best Practices. With that, the woman exits, a grey file folder tucked under her arm.
Zh: “我猜，是家族关系吧？他的岳父为标准和最佳实践办公室工作。”说完，女人走了出去，胳膊下还夹着一份灰色的文件。

En: Doing one's job doesn't automatically make one anyone's *bitch*. Besides, there are more nefarious powers to work for than the Moralintern.
Zh: “完成自己的本职工作不会让一个人自然而然地成为任何人的*姘头*。再说了，相比国际道德伦理委员会，还有更多穷凶极恶的势力。”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "So when I said I was a lackey of capital, what I should have said is that I'm a Moralintern bitch?"
Zh: （转向金。）“所以我说自己是资本主义的走狗的时候，其实更应该说自己是国际道德伦理委员会的姘头？”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "So when I said I was a thrall to foreign interests, what I should have said is that I'm a Moralintern bitch?"
Zh: （转向金。）“所以我说自己是外国利益的奴隶的时候，其实更应该说自己是国际道德伦理委员会的姘头？”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "So what I'm hearing is that we're Moralintern bitches?"
Zh: （转向金。）“所以，我听到的是，我们是国际道德伦理委员会的姘头？”

En: Oooh... (Turn to Kim.) "So we're actually working for the Moralintern? That doesn't seem so bad."
Zh: “哦……”（转向金。）“所以我们其实是在为国际道德伦理委员会做事？好像也没那么糟糕的样子。”

En: He nods. "There are more nefarious powers to work for than the Moralintern."
Zh: （他点点头。）“相比国际道德伦理委员会，还有更多穷凶极恶的势力。”

En: (Turn back to Sunday Friend.) "Are you a moralist?"
Zh: （转向周日朋友。）“你是道德家吗？”

En: Consequences? I don't care about consequences!
Zh: 后果？我不在乎什么后果！

En: Do you not care for anyone other than yourself? Have you thought about what this might mean for Klaasje?
Zh: 除了自己，难道你不会关心其他人吗？你有没有想过这对卡拉洁来说意味着什么？

En: He nods. "Anything else?"
Zh: 他点点头。“还有别的事吗？”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: Governmental issues take me all over Revachol as you can see.
Zh: “你可以看到，政府问题带着我走遍了整个瑞瓦肖。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: Someone is always watching.
Zh: “总会有人看着的。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: None of z'is is weird.
Zh: “没有什么好奇怪的。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: In a *wider* sense z'is is actually my apartment. And yours.
Zh: “从*广义*上讲，这里确实是我的公寓。也是你的。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: If we can ensure *stabilité*, progress will naturally follow.
Zh: “如果我们可以保证*稳定性*，进步自然就会随之到来。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: *Transparence* has always been our highest priority.
Zh: “*透明度*一直都是我们的最高优先级。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: All of z'is is perfectly normal. A bit mundane if anything.
Zh: “这一切都很正常。非要说的话，确实有些平凡。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: I don't z'ink conclusions should be rushed at z'is point.
Zh: “我觉得现在下结论有些为时过早。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: A *politicien* is never off z'e clock.
Zh: “一名*政客*从来不会下班。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: Moderacy in everything -- even in moderacy.
Zh: “一切都要节制——就算节制本身也是。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: Everyone needs to share z'e *responsabilité*.
Zh: “大家都需要分担自己的*责任*。”

ConvTitle: APT / TOMAS THE SPONSOR barks apt
En: If *stabilité* is ensured, progress will naturally follow.
Zh: “如果能保证*稳定*，进展自然会随之而来。””

En: I'm alright, you can leave now...
Zh: “我没事，现在你可以离开了……”

En: Please just leave...
Zh: “请离开吧……”

En: Hello, detective.
Zh: “你好，警探。”

En: Good advice.
Zh: 这是个好建议。

En: This only made it harder. Here comes the pain.
Zh: 这样才会变得更困难。痛苦来了。

En: Tidying up. Nervously.
Zh: 正在收拾。紧张不安。

En: Look at the lieutenant first.
Zh: 先看看警督。

En: He gives you a short encouraging nod.
Zh: 他朝你微微点了点头，以示鼓励。

En: No one answers the door.
Zh: 没人应门。

En: It's too late, I think everyone's already asleep. We should try again tomorrow.
Zh: “现在太晚了，我觉得大家都已经睡着了。我们还是明天再来一次吧。”

En: A weathered brown door. The number reads '20'.
Zh: 一扇风化的棕色大门。号码是“20”。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Knock.
Zh: 敲门。

En: Hello! Who is it? A voice calls out from the other side of the door.
Zh: “你好！请问是谁？”一个声音从大门另一侧传了出来。

En: Is this Billie Méjean's home? This is the police, please open the door.
Zh: “这里是比莉·梅让家吗？我是警察，请开门。”

En: The police? A moment, please, give us a moment. You hear shuffling inside.
Zh: “警察？请等等，给我们一点时间。”你听见里面有些响动。

En: No one answers the door. They must be sleeping at this hour. Better try again tomorrow.
Zh: 没人应门。这个时间他们一定是睡着了。最好明天再试一次。

En: And someone turns down the radio.
Zh: 还有人调低了收音机的声音。

En: She unlocks the door. "Come in, the door is open."
Zh: 她打开门。“请进，门打开了。”

En: This is the door, you already know it's the right door. This is going to be *so hard*.
Zh: 就是这扇门，你知道这就是正确的那扇门。肯定会*相当困难*的。

En: Get yourself together. It's just police work.
Zh: 镇定一点。这只是警察的工作而已。

En: Don't knock, just leave. You don't need this. You don't need to *sad it up*.
Zh: 别敲门，直接离开吧。你不需要这样。你不需要*悲伤*。

En: There's fear in her voice.
Zh: 她的声音里有些恐惧的意味。

En: The lieutenant motions you to go ahead and knock.
Zh: 警督示意你上前敲门。

En: Hold on, Kim, we should discuss this before we move on. What should we expect?
Zh: “等一下，金。继续之前我们应该先讨论一下。我们可能会遇到什么状况？”

En: We have our first preliminary identification -- in all likelihood the deceased is the husband of Billie Méjean. We need to confirm this, as well as deliver the death notification to Billie herself.
Zh: “根据我们的初步鉴定结果——死者十分可能是比莉·梅让的丈夫。我们需要确定这一点，同时还要向比莉本人传达他的死讯。”

En: Chances are that whoever's going to greet us behind that door is somehow related to the deceased. This means that our job here is to deliver the death notification, as well as identify the body.
Zh: “不管是谁在门口迎接我们，都可能跟死者有所关联。这就意味着我们的工作是传达死讯，还有确认死者身份。”

En: Now, delivering a death notification is never an easy task. There's a reason why it's often called the most stressful part of our job. This is why it's usually done in pairs. You got this. I'll be monitoring reactions, ready to act if necessary.
Zh: “传达死讯从来都不是一件容易的工作。它经常被称作是我们工作中压力最大的部分，其中是有一定原因的。所以这种工作经常会交给两个人完成。你来吧。我会负责观察对方的反应，如果有必要的话随时做出反应。”

En: You're right, let's talk this through. He looks at the apartment door and lowers his voice a bit...
Zh: “你说的没错，我们先聊聊。”他看着公寓大门，稍微压低了声音……

En: Do you have any advice on how to tell her he's...?
Zh: “你有什么建议吗，该怎么告诉她说他已经……？”

En: What if I don't want to do this?
Zh: “如果我不想这样做怎么办？”

En: Alright, I think I'm ready. (Proceed.)
Zh: “好吧，我想我已经准备好了。”（继续。）

En: Yes, it's hard. He stares at the front door for a moment, his face still. "But there is no easy way to handle this information. It just has to happen, as soon as possible."
Zh: “是的，这很难。”他盯着前门看了一会儿，面容毫无波澜。“但是恐怕没有什么简单的方法来处理这种信息。必须把消息传达出去，越快越好。”

En: Dead? Just don't say that you know how they feel. You don't.
Zh: “死了？只是千万别说你明白他们的感受。你不明白。”

En: The lieutenant motions towards the door.
Zh: 警督朝门口走去。

En: Kim, I don't want to do this, let's turn around. (Try to bail out.)
Zh: “金，我不想这么做，我们转回去吧。”（试着放弃。）

En: We can't. Someone has to deliver the news. He motions towards the door. "Let's go."
Zh: “不可以。必须有人传达这个消息。”他朝着门口走去。“我们走吧。”

En: You hear some light footsteps and what appears to be a late-night talk show playing on the radio.
Zh: 你听到一阵轻微的脚步声，收音机里像是在播放一出深夜脱口秀节目。

En: You hear light footsteps passing by the door and some folk music playing on the radio.
Zh: 你听到门口有轻微的脚步声经过，收音机里正播放着民族音乐。

En: You hear some light footsteps and what appears to be a daily weather forecast playing on the radio.
Zh: 你听到一阵轻微的脚步声，收音机里像是在播放每日天气预报。

En: Something smells good -- soupe à l'oignon?
Zh: 里面飘出很好闻的味道——洋葱汤吗？

En: She swallows visibly.
Zh: 她明显咽了下口水。

En: Central Jamrock Public Library?
Zh: “加姆洛克公共图书馆？”

En: I think, yes. If that's the one on Meroe.
Zh: “我想是的。如果是麦罗埃街上那家的话。”

En: Central Jamrock Public Library?
Zh: “加姆洛克公共图书馆？”

En: Do something? Break something? It's a fool's hope.
Zh: 做点什么？打破点什么？这是一个愚蠢的希望。

En: It reminded him of his teenage years.
Zh: 它让他想起自己的青少年时期。

En: Here it comes.
Zh: 要来了。

En: God, do I just say he's *dead* like that...
Zh: 天啦，我是不是要直接说他*死了*……

En: Yes. That's the most important thing. Use that word.
Zh: 是的。那才是最重要的事情。用那个词。

En: No euphemisms, this is what you came here for.
Zh: 没有委婉的说法，你就是为这个来的。

En: A great and terrible spike. The blood freezes in her veins.
Zh: 一根巨大而恐怖的尖刺。血液在她的血管里冻结成冰。

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Two days, maybe.
Zh: “也许两天吧。”

En: If you say 'Two days, maybe', it will be etched in her mind forever.
Zh: 如果你说出‘也许两天’，它就会永远铭刻在她的脑海里。

En: But he was just here. Alive.
Zh: 他前不久还在这里，活生生的。

En: No. A day.
Zh: 不。一天。

En: God, should I call them? Should I tell them to come home?
Zh: “天啦，我该打给她们吗？我应该让她们回家吗？”

En: It burns like acid.
Zh: 它像酸液一样灼烧着。

En: Unreal.
Zh: 那么的不真实。

En: She can't take much more. Her stomach is churning. Soon she will have to go to the bathroom and scream.
Zh: 她再也不能言语，胃里在不停翻搅。很快她就不得不冲进浴室尖叫。

En: A very good call.
Zh: 您的判断非常正确。

En: A very good call. You must leave now, sire.
Zh: 您的判断非常正确。大人，现在您必须离开了。

En: These are her last reserves of strength. Her muscles will give in soon. Already she starts to shake.
Zh: 这是她仅存的一点力气。很快，她的肌肉会投降。她已经开始颤抖了。

En: These are her last reserves of strength. Her muscles will give in soon. Already she starts to shake.
Zh: 这是她仅存的一点力气。很快，她的肌肉会投降。她已经开始颤抖了。

En: It is.
Zh: 是的。

En: There has been enough pain.
Zh: 痛苦已经够多了。

En: *Force* yourself. Slap yourself.
Zh: 强迫你自己。扇自己巴掌。

En: She's in pain. She's in so much pain and so are you, your chest is burning.
Zh: 她很痛苦。她跟你一样痛苦，你的胸膛在燃烧。

En: The lieutenant steps in. "We're very sorry to inform you, but your husband, Victor Méjean, was found dead this morning on the Martinaise boardwalk."
Zh: 警督插了进来。“我们非常抱歉的通知你，你的丈夫维克托·梅让今早被发现死在了马丁内斯的木板道。”

En: He was just here. Alive.
Zh: 他就在这里。活生生的。

En: You stupid... *why* did you say that?!
Zh: 你这个白痴……*为什么*要这么说？！

En: God, should I call them? Should I tell them to come home?
Zh: “天啦，我该打给她们吗？我应该让她们回家吗？”

En: No, no... She breathes in. "I just need to tell my girls..." The air gets sucked out of her lungs suddenly.
Zh: “不，不……”她深吸一口气。“我只是需要告诉我的女儿们……”突然，她肺里的空气全被抽了出来。

En: It burns like acid.
Zh: 它像酸液一样灼烧着。

En: Unreal.
Zh: 那么的不真实。
