En: She can't take much more. Her stomach is churning. Soon she will have to go to the bathroom and scream.
Zh: 她再也不能言语，胃里在不停翻搅。很快她就不得不冲进浴室尖叫。

En: For god's sake. Lie.
Zh: 看在神明的面上，撒谎吧。

En: Yes. You're a total fucking horror show.
Zh: 是的。你他妈就是一场彻头彻尾的恐怖秀。

En: No-no! Stop now.
Zh: 不！停下来，现在。

En: These are her last reserves of strength. Her muscles will give in soon. To a scream.
Zh: 这是她仅存的一点力气。很快，她的肌肉就会屈服。向尖叫屈服。

En: She looks up, too anxious to say anything.
Zh: 她抬起头，由于太过紧张，她一句话都说不出来。

En: The lieutenant nods at the woman, then looks back at you, his voice lowered: "We should step outside and talk."
Zh: 警督对女人点点头，然后把目光转向你，压低了声音：“我们应该出去聊几句。”

En: Again, I'm very sorry, ma'am.
Zh: “再次向你表达我的遗憾，夫人。”

En: So... how have you been?
Zh: “所以……你最近怎么样？”

En: How have I been? she shakes her head. "You're not here to discuss *me* -- what is this about, officer?"
Zh: “我最近怎么样？”她摇摇头。“我肯定你来这里不是为了讨论*我*的事——怎么回事，警官？”

En: I noticed you were cooking something.
Zh: “我看到你好像在做饭。”

En: People gotta eat. She rubs her hands together, anxious. "What is this about? Why are you suddenly asking me about my cooking?"
Zh: “大家都要吃饭的。”她紧张地摩擦双手。“这是要干什么？为什么突然问我做饭的事？”

En: [Set the library card by her. Leave the room.]
Zh: [把借书证放在她身边。离开房间。]

En: Very long, ma'am. I'm sorry.
Zh: “很久了，夫人。我很抱歉。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: No, we *have*.
Zh: “不，我们*有*。”

En: I'll take it from here. Thank you. She points at the front door, breathing fast now. Holding herself back.
Zh: “接下来就交给我吧。谢谢你。”她指了指大门的方向，呼吸变得有些急促。她正在努力控制自己。

En: Can't write a scene without knowing the actors. Ask more, get comfortable around her.
Zh: 要是不了解演员的话，怎么能写得出一幕场景呢。再多问一些，让自己在她身边舒服一些。

En: She's afraid -- and so are you. Man up and cut to the point.
Zh: 她很害怕——你也一样。爷们一点，直奔主题吧。

En: It's like ripping off a bandage. The least painful way is to do it straight away, without stalling.
Zh: 这就像是撕绷带。痛苦最少的方式就是最直接的方式，不要拖拉。

En: How about some small talk before you break the news?
Zh: 不如在宣布消息之前先闲聊一下？

En: Definitely no small talk, this isn't the time or place.
Zh: 绝对不要闲聊，时间不对，地点也不对。

En: [Set the library card by her. Leave the room.]
Zh: [把借书证放在她身边。离开房间。]

En: She's crying, too heartbroken to notice you standing next to her.
Zh: 她在哭泣，太过悲伤的她没有注意到站在身旁的你。

En: What are you still doing here?! She needs time to be alone now.
Zh: 你还在这里做什么？！现在她需要些时间独处。

En: Hello, I'm Billie. Would you like something to drink? Tea, lemonade? she asks nervously. "We're out of coffee, I was supposed to get some tomorrow..."
Zh: “你好，我是比莉。你想来点喝的吗？茶，柠檬水？”她紧张地问到。“我们的咖啡喝完了，我本来应该明天去买的……”

En: Don't give her the lieutenant's handkerchief, it's too special.
Zh: 不要把警督的手帕交给她，它太特别了。

En: The woman wipes her face with a sleeve and manages to conjure up a small terrified smile. She looks disoriented.
Zh: 女人用袖子擦了把脸，勉强挤出了一丝惊恐的微笑。她看起来有些混乱。

En: Thanks, but I'm alright.
Zh: “谢谢，我还好。”

En: I'm not really here for the tea.
Zh: “我真的不是来这里喝茶的。”

En: Not now. [Leave.]
Zh: 现在不行。[离开。]

En: Is this about Victor, my husband? Is he in some kind of trouble again? I can come pick him up in the station if that's what... She stops, her eyes trying to read answers from your face.
Zh: “是关于我的丈夫维克托吗？他是不是又惹上什么麻烦了？我可以去警局接他，如果是这么回事的话……”她停了下来，眼睛似乎想从你的脸上找到答案。

En: Something changes in her expression. "No, this is something much worse... Is he in a hospital? How bad is it?"
Zh: 她的表情有了些变化。“不，情况更糟糕是不是……他住院了吗？伤的多重？”

En: You live here with Victor, your husband, right?
Zh: “你跟你丈夫维克托一起住在这里，是吗？”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: I don't think I introduced myself properly -- I'm Billie. Would you like something to drink? She looks around the kitchen. "Tea, lemonade? We're out of coffee..."
Zh: “我还没有好好做个自我介绍——我叫比莉。你想来点喝的吗？”她四下看了看厨房。“茶，柠檬水？我们家没有咖啡了……”

En: It's you. From the bookstand. For a moment she seems paralysed, just looking at you and the lieutenant. Then she snaps out of it.
Zh: “是你。书摊的那个人。”有那么一会儿，她似乎愣住了，只是呆呆地看着你和金。然后，她强迫自己摆脱了那种状态。

En: It's you. From the bookstand. For a moment she seems paralysed, just looking at you. Then she forces herself out of it.
Zh: “是你。书摊的那个人。”有那么一会儿，她似乎愣住了，只是呆呆地看着你。然后，她强迫自己摆脱了那种状态。

En: She must know you're here because of her husband.
Zh: 她一定知道你是为她丈夫来的。

En: It's you from the bookstand... She just looks at you for a moment without knowing what to do.
Zh: “你是书摊的那个人……”她只是呆呆地看了你一会儿，不知道该怎么做。

En: It's you from the bookstand... Did you come to bring my cockatoo back? She smiles nervously, before the smile vanishes from her face.
Zh: “你是书摊的那个人……你把我的凤头鹦鹉带回来了吗？”她的笑容有些紧张，接着就从脸上消失了。

En: It's not about the cockatoo.
Zh: “不是凤头鹦鹉的事。”

En: She just nods.
Zh: 她只是点点头。

En: Keep it together. You don't want your body language to tell her the news.
Zh: 沉着一点。你可不希望自己的肢体语言向她传达消息。

En: Sorry, I'm rambling... She shakes her head and tries to laugh it off. "It's just that Victor often gets into all kinds of trouble. So, how can I help you?"
Zh: “抱歉，我的话有些不着边际……”她摇摇头，试着一笑置之。“只是维克托经常惹上各种各样的麻烦。所以，我有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Hold on... *You're* Billie? I thought Billie was a man...
Zh: “等等……*你是*比莉？我还以为比莉是个男人……”

En: No, I'm Billie. She narrows her eyes. "Is this about my husband, Victor? I can come pick him up in the station if that's what..." She stops, her eyes trying to read answers from your face.
Zh: “不，我是比莉。”她眯起眼睛。“是关于我的丈夫维克托的吗？我可以去警局接他，如果是这么回事的话……”她停了下来，眼睛似乎想从你的脸上找到答案。

En: Keep it together. Your body language shouldn't give it away.
Zh: 沉着一点。不要让肢体语言暴露了。

En: It's me, Victor, and the kids here, she nods. "We have two daughters, Jennie and Jolie."
Zh: “我，维克托还有孩子们，”她点点头。“我们有两个女儿，珍妮和朱莉。”

En: (Look around.) "And where is everybody? I don't see anyone home."
Zh: （环顾四周。）“那其他人呢？我好像没看见其他人在家。”

En: The girls are staying at their friend's place tonight, and Victor is... out.
Zh: “女孩们今晚在朋友家里过夜，维克托……出去了。”

En: Out where?
Zh: “去哪了？”

En: He still hasn't come home?
Zh: “他还没有回家？”

En: No. She stops.
Zh: “没有。”她停了下来。

En: Why, do you have any news?
Zh: “为什么这么问，你有什么消息吗？”

En: As I already told you, he has a problem with drinking. And so he... disappears every now and then.
Zh: “我已经告诉过你了，他有点酗酒的问题，而且他……时不时会失踪。”

En: He... has a problem with drinking. And so he... disappears every now and then.
Zh: “他……有点酗酒的问题。而且他……时不时会失踪。”

En: He's probably in the Pox, drinking with his friends. I sent him to the library a few days ago, but I guess something came up... She smiles sourly.
Zh: “他可能在帕克斯，跟他的朋友一起喝酒。几天前我让他去一趟图书馆，但是我猜有什么东西上来了……”她的笑容有些酸涩。

En: Remind me again, what was he wearing when you last saw him?
Zh: “再提醒我一次，你最后一次看见他的时候，他穿的是什么衣服？”

En: Do you remember what he was wearing when you last saw him?
Zh: “你还记得最后一次看见他的时候，他穿的是什么衣服吗？”

En: To your knowledge, did he make it to the library?
Zh: “据你所知，他去图书馆了吗？”

En: There's something else I have to tell you...
Zh: “我还有些事要告诉你……”

En: No. I guess I'll find out when he decides to come back home. She looks at the front door.
Zh: “不。我猜他决定回家的时候，我就知道了吧。”她看着大门的方向。

En: It shouldn't be long from now. He's never gone for more than a few days.
Zh: “应该用不了太久。他从没离开过几天的。”

En: His old leather jacket.
Zh: “他的旧皮夹克。”

En: Can you describe it?
Zh: “你能描述一下吗？”

En: She folds her hands across her chest.
Zh: 她把两只手交叠着放在胸前。

En: Tell her about the dead body.
Zh: 告诉她尸体的事。

En: Kim is here.
Zh: 金在这里。

En: Identified the leather jacket.
Zh: 辨认皮夹克。

En: Identified the library card.
Zh: 辨认借书证。

En: Kept your composure.
Zh: 保持冷静。

En: Discussed things through with Kim.
Zh: 跟金讨论问题。

En: Lieutenant's handkerchief.
Zh: 警督的手帕。

En: Too much small talk.
Zh: 闲聊太多了。

En: Made her anxious.
Zh: 让她紧张。

En: Dresscode: RCM official.
Zh: 着装要求：RCM警官。

En: I don't want to.
Zh: 我不想。

En: (Turn to Kim.) "I don't want to do this."
Zh: （转向金。）“我不想这样。”

En: How old are your daughters?
Zh: “你的女儿们多大了？”

En: What was the name of the library?
Zh: “图书馆叫什么名字？”

En: Oh, it's in Jamrock. The one at Meroe street. I don't know the official name.
Zh: “哦，在加姆洛克。就是麦罗埃街上那个。我不知道官方名称是什么。”

En: Wait, what?
Zh: 等等，什么？

En: But we *have to*.
Zh: 但是我们*必须*这样做。

En: I don't want to do this.
Zh: 我不想这么做。

En: (Turn to Kim.) "It seems like there's a part of me that refuses to do this."
Zh: （转向金。）“看来有部分我拒绝这样做。”

En: (Slap your cheek.) "Stupid... stupid... just say it..."
Zh: （自己打脸。）“笨蛋……笨蛋……直接说啊……”

En: (Slap your cheek.) "Stupid... stupid... just say it..."
Zh: （自己打脸。）“笨蛋……笨蛋……直接说啊……”

En: (Turn to Billie.) "It seems like there's a part of me that refuses to do this."
Zh: （转向比莉。）“看来有部分我拒绝这样做。”

En: (Turn to Billie.) "I don't want to do this."
Zh: （转向比莉。）“我不想这么做。”

En: Say what? She looks frightened.
Zh: “说什么？”她似乎吓到了。

En: Your husband's dead.
Zh: “你丈夫死了。”

En: Too late, you ruined it, just say it now.
Zh: 太迟了，你毁了它，话语就这么脱口而出。

En: Do what?
Zh: “做什么？”

En: Officer... the lieutenant warns you.
Zh: “警官……”警督向你发出警告。

En: Excuse me, what? She blinks. "What-did-you-say?"
Zh: “请原谅，你说什么？”她眨眨眼。“你说什么？”

En: Do what?
Zh: “做什么？”

En: Too late, you ruined it, just say it now.
Zh: 太迟了，你毁了它，话语就这么脱口而出。

En: Say what? She looks frightened.
Zh: “说什么？”她似乎吓到了。

En: Officer... the lieutenant warns you.
Zh: “警官……”警督向你发出警告。

En: We're very sorry to inform you, but your husband, Victor Méjean, was found dead this morning on the Martinaise boardwalk.
Zh: “我们非常抱歉的通知你，你的丈夫维克托·梅让今早被发现死在了马丁内斯的木板道。”

En: Your husband has expired.
Zh: “你的丈夫到期了。”

En: He's in a better place now.
Zh: “他现在去了一个更好的地方。”

En: I said that he's dead.
Zh: “我说他死了。”

En: You sense that the lieutenant is ready to say something -- don't let him. Fix this yourself, you did this.
Zh: 你感觉到警督准备说些什么——别让他这样做。自己搞定，你可以的。

En: (Raise your palm.) "Kim, I can handle this. Let me."
Zh: （举起手。）“金，我可以处理好的。让我来吧。”

En: Let him talk.
Zh: 让他说。

En: The lieutenant closes his mouth, but he doesn't look pleased.
Zh: 警督闭上嘴，但是看起来不太高兴。

En: Expired? She narrows her eyes. "Like a *milk carton*?"
Zh: “到期？”她眯起眼睛。“就像*牛奶盒*吗？”

En: Where? Did he run *away*?
Zh: “哪里？他*跑路*了吗？”

En: No, he's dead.
Zh: “不，他死了。”

En: Oh.
Just dead.
Zh: “只是死了。”

En: Just tell me, what happened to Victor? She looks first at the lieutenant, then back at you.
Zh: “告诉我，维克托怎么了？”她先看了一眼警督，然后把目光转回到你身上。

En: Yes, I... She chokes on something, clutching on to her neck.
Zh: “是的，我……”她被什么东西噎住了，紧紧抓住自己的脖子。

En: I'm sorry, I just need a... I don't know what to say. She rests her hand on her forehead, eyes pale like pearls in seawater.
Zh: “抱歉，我需要……我不知道该说什么。”她双手捂住额头，双眼像海水里的珍珠一样苍白。

En: Oh. She touches her neck, eyes pale like pearls in seawater.
Zh: “哦。”她的手抚上自己的脖子，双眼像海水里的珍珠一样苍白。

En: Oh, she says again, "But he was just..." She looks at the kitchen table, where two cigarette butts are still in the tray.
Zh: “哦，”她又说了一次，“但他只是……”她看着厨房的桌子，上面的烟灰缸里还留着两只烟头。

En: I understand that this comes as a huge shock. I want you to know that me and my partner, he points at you, "are here for you if you have any questions. We're very sorry for your loss, ma'am."
Zh: “我能理解，这对你来说是一个巨大的冲击。我想让你知道，我和我的搭档，”他指向你，“专程过来回答你的所有问题，我们对你丈夫的死深表遗憾，夫人。”

En: What happened to him? Tell me. Her neck and cheeks are covered with red blotches, her double chin is shaking.
Zh: “他出了什么事？告诉我。”她的脖子和脸颊被红疹斑覆盖，双下巴在颤抖。

En: He drank himself to death.
Zh: “酗酒过量。”

En: He fell and smashed his head against the bench.
Zh: “他摔了一跤，头撞到了长凳。”

En: Trust me, ma'am, you *don't* want to know.
Zh: “相信我，夫人，你*不想*知道的。”

En: Why, it's *that bad*?
Zh: “为什么，真有*那么糟糕*吗？”

En: And you just... found him there? Lying in the cold? She shakes her head.
Zh: “你就这么……在那里找到他的？冰冷的躺在那里？”她摇摇头。

En: How long had he *been* there?
Zh: “他在那里*多久*了？”

En: It's hard to say.
Zh: “很难说。”

En: For a few days at least.
Zh: “至少几天了。”

En: She doesn't reply. Her eyes well up with tears, as she struggles to keep it together.
Zh: 她没有回应。她的眼里满是泪水，却拼命忍着不让它们落下来。

En: Is there anyone we could call for you? A friend, a family member, someone who could be here for you?
Zh: “需要我们帮你打给谁吗？朋友，家人，或者可以来陪你的人？”

En: Just tell me, what do I need to do next? Where is he? Can I see him?
Zh: “告诉我，接下来我该怎么做？他在哪？我能看看他吗？”

En: He was taken to the city morgue. The local coroner will be contacting you shortly to arrange the funeral. Here's his number in case you want to contact him earlier. He hands her a leaflet with the morgue's contact information.
Zh: “我们已经把死者送到市停尸房了。本地验尸官很快会联系你，安排葬礼事宜。如果你想提前联系他的话，这里是他的电话号码。”

En: Is there anything else that the RCM could do for you?
Zh: 他递给女人一张传单，上面有停尸房的联系方式。“RCM还能为你做些什么吗？”

En: No. I'll call you if something comes up, I'm still a bit... She rubs her face, blotchy and numb.
Zh: “没了。如果有什么事我会打给你，我还是有点……”她摩挲着自己的脸颊，上面满是斑点，有些木然。

En: I don't want to do this.
Zh: “我不想这么做。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: What? She stares at you with hazy eyes.
Zh: “什么？”她用空洞的眼睛盯着你。

En: I guess what I want to know is... how did he die?
Zh: “我想知道……他是怎么死的？”

En: Please don't cry.
Zh: “请不要哭。”

En: (Mumble.) "I don't want to do this..."
Zh: （嘟囔）“我不想这么做……”

En: Smack your lips.
Zh: 砸吧嘴唇。

En: Alright, I'll call them... She breathes in again, trying to steady herself.
Zh: “好吧，我会打给他们……”她再次深吸一口气，试着稳定自己的情绪。

En: Look away.
Zh: 看向别处。

En: He's still there, on the boardwalk.
Zh: “他还在那里，在木板道上。”

En: He's in the morgue.
Zh: “他在停尸房。”

En: You haven't even taken him away?! There's alarm in her voice.
Zh: “你还没有把他带走吗？！”她的声音里有一股警告的意味。

En: And who should I contact...?
Zh: “那我应该联系谁……？”

En: RCM? I wish Kim was here...
Zh: RCM？真希望金在这里……

En: I really don't know.
Zh: “我真的不知道。”

En: RCM?
Zh: “RCM？”

En: Alright, I'll call the RCM then, she nods to herself.
Zh: “好吧，我会打给RCM，”她自顾自的点点头。

En: No, but we will. We will take him to the city morgue.
Zh: “没有，不过我们会做的。我们会把他送到市停尸房。”

En: Sorry I couldn't do this, I have a brain condition... (Tap on your head.)
Zh: “抱歉我做不到，我的脑袋稍微有点问题……”（拍拍自己的头。）

En: Alright, that's it then.
Zh: “好吧，那就这样吧。”

En: A brain condition?
Zh: “脑子有问题？”

En: I drank too much and now I can't do anything right anymore.
Zh: “我喝的太多，现在什么事都做不太好。”

En: You know what, it doesn't matter. I'll just... leave.
Zh: “你知道吗，无所谓了。我还是……离开吧。”

En: Yes, she says sharply, "that's what happened to Victor too."
Zh: “是的，”她尖锐地说到，“维克托也是这样。”

En: It looks like he slipped on the boardwalk -- fatally, I'm afraid. It was just a very unfortunate accident, although alcohol may have played a role.
Zh: “看起来他像是在木板道上滑了一跤——恐怕这一下是致命的。这只是一起非常不幸的事故，不过其中可能也有酒精的影响。”

En: I can't... She covers her mouth. "I can't believe he's still *there*, this is *madness*."
Zh: “我不能……”她捂住嘴巴。“我不能相信他居然还在*那里*，这真是太*疯狂*了。”

En: I don't know where they go...
Zh: “我不知道他们去了哪里……”

En: No, I meant -- where is *his body*?!
Zh: “不，我是说——*他的尸体*在哪？”

En: I'll take it from here. Thank you. She points at the front door, breathing fast now. Holding herself back.
Zh: “接下来就交给我吧。谢谢你。”她指了指大门的方向，呼吸变得有些急促。她正在努力控制自己。

En: Apologies, my partner, the lieutenant steps in, giving you a sideways glance, "did *not* mean to make light of the situation."
Zh: “很抱歉，我的搭档，”警督斜了你一眼，插了进来，“我的搭档*并不是*有意轻视这种状况的。”

En: Again, if there's anything we could do for you, then don't hesitate to call the RCM, ma'am.
Zh: “重新声明一次，如果有什么我们能为你做的事，请不要犹豫，直接打给RCM，夫人。”

En: You hear the clock ticking in the children's room.
Zh: 你听见孩子们的房间传来钟表的滴答声。

En: She just nods, distant and inconsolable. The bed springs rattle beneath her as she begins to shake.
Zh: 她只是点点头，冷漠疏离、伤心欲绝。她开始颤抖，身下床垫的弹簧发出吱呀吱呀的声响。

En: It's still there, on the boardwalk.
Zh: “他还在那里，在木板道上。”

En: It's in the morgue.
Zh: “他在停尸房里。”

En: You've done this before. Just keep your focus.
Zh: 你之前做过的。只要集中注意力就好。

En: Ma'am, I'm very sorry to say, but your husband, Victor Méjean, was found dead on the Martinaise boardwalk.
Zh: “夫人，我很抱歉的通知你，你的丈夫维克托·梅让被发现死在了马丁内斯的木板道上。”

En: Oh. She touches her neck, eyes pale like pearls in seawater.
Zh: “哦。”她的手抚上自己的脖子，双眼像海水里的珍珠一样苍白。

En: Oh, she says again, "But he was just..." She looks at the kitchen table, where two cigarette butts are still in the tray.
Zh: “哦，”她又说了一次，“但他只是……”她看着厨房的桌子，上面的烟灰缸里还留着两只烟头。

En: We understand this comes as a huge shock. I want you to know that me and my partner, he points at you, "are here for you if you have any questions. Take your time, ma'am."
Zh: “我们能理解，这对你来说是一个巨大的冲击。我想让你知道，我和我的搭档，”他指向你，“专程过来回答你的所有问题，慢慢来，夫人。”

En: What happened to him? She turns to you. Her neck and cheeks are covered with red blotches, her double chin is shaking.
Zh: “他出了什么事？告诉我。”她的脖子和脸颊被红疹斑覆盖，双下巴在颤抖。

En: She blinks. "What-did-you-say?"
Zh: 她眨眨眼。“你说什么？”

En: Your husband, Victor Méjean, is dead. I'm very sorry for your loss, ma'am.
Zh: “你的丈夫维克托·梅让死了。对你的丈夫的死，我深表遗憾，夫人。”

En: I understand that this may come as a huge shock. I want you to know that I'm here for you if you have any questions. Take your time, ma'am, I'm not going anywhere.
Zh: “我能理解，这对你来说是一个巨大的冲击。我想让你知道，我这次来会回答你的所有问题。慢慢来，夫人，我哪里也不去。”

En: It's still early to say, but at first glance it seems like he slipped and hit his head.
Zh: “现在说还有些太早，但是第一眼看上去，他好像是滑倒，然后撞到了头。”

En: It was an accident -- he fell through a hole in the boardwalk and hit his head.
Zh: “这是一起事故——他从木板道的洞里摔了下去，然后撞到了头。”

En: Was he drunk?
Zh: “他喝醉了吗？”

En: Alcohol may have played a role, yes.
Zh: “酒精可能是起了点作用，没错。”

En: We don't know yet, but we will let you know when the results arrive.
Zh: “我们还不知道，等结果出来了我们会告诉你的。”

En: I see... She withdraws, trying to picture the scene. "And you just... found him there? Lying in the cold?"
Zh: “我明白了……”她后退几步，试着想象那个画面。“你就这么……在那里找到他的吗？冰冷的躺在那里？”

En: How long had he been there?
Zh: “他在那多久了？”

En: It's hard to say at this point.
Zh: “现在还很难说。”

En: It couldn't have been long.
Zh: “不是很久。”

En: She blinks, eyes welling up with tears, as her hands start searching for something from the pockets of her dress.
Zh: 她眨眨眼，眼中满是泪水，手开始在裙子的口袋里翻找着什么。

En: The handkerchief!
Zh: 手帕！

En: Here. The lieutenant takes out his handkerchief and offers it to the woman. She nods and slowly wipes away her tears.
Zh: “给你。”金掏出自己的手帕，递给了女人。她点点头，开始轻轻擦拭着眼泪。

En: A small, terrified smile quivers on her face as she takes the handkerchief and wipes away the tears. She looks disoriented.
Zh: 当她掏出手帕擦拭眼泪的时候，脸上流露出一丝惊恐的微笑。她看起来有些混乱。

En: Here, take this. (Give her the lieutenant's handkerchief.)
Zh: “给你，拿着吧。”（把警督的手帕递给她。）

En: (No words. Just give her the lieutenant's handkerchief.)
Zh: （不说话。只是把警督的手帕递给她。）

En: Yes, thank you. She smiles a small, terrified smile, then wipes away her tears. She looks disoriented.
Zh: “是的，谢谢你。”她露出一丝惊恐的微笑，轻轻擦拭着眼泪。她看起来有些混乱。

En: Is there anyone we could call for you? A friend, a family member, someone who could be here for you?
Zh: “需要我们帮你打给谁吗？朋友，家人，或者可以来陪你的人？”

En: No, no... She breathes in. "I just need to tell my girls..." The air gets sucked out of her lungs suddenly.
Zh: “不，不……”她深吸一口气。“我只是需要告诉我的女儿们……”突然，她肺里的空气全被抽了出来。

En: Yes, they should know. Do you want us to call them and ask them here?
Zh: “是的，她们应该知道。你希望我们打电话叫她们过来吗？”

En: No, take a day to recover. You'll be better prepared when they come home tomorrow.
Zh: “不，用一天恢复一下，这样明天她们回家的时候，你就准备好了。”

En: No, I'll do it myself, I just... She breathes again, holding on to her left side like she's in pain.
Zh: “不，我自己来，我只是……”她又吸了一口气，紧握着身体左侧，好像非常痛苦。

En: Good, that's probably the right thing, thank you... She nods, but with a wretched expression.
Zh: “好吧，这样做可能是对的，谢谢……”她点点头，但脸上的表情很难受。

En: Is there anyone we could call for you? A friend, a family member, someone who could be here for you?
Zh: “需要我们帮你打给谁吗？朋友，家人，或者可以来陪你的人？”

En: My handkerchief... She tries to smile through the tears, as she rubs her face with hands like a child.
Zh: “我的手帕……”她像孩子一样用手摩挲着哭泣的面庞，想要挤出一点笑容。

En: Just tell me, what do I need to do next? Where is he? Can I see him?
Zh: “告诉我，接下来我该怎么做？他在哪？我能看看他吗？”

En: No, I'll call you if something comes up, I'm still... She rubs her face, runs her fingers over cheeks that have become numb.
Zh: “没了，如果有什么事我会打给你，我还是有点……”她摩挲着自己的脸庞，手指抚摸着早已麻木的面颊。

En: We've taken him to the city morgue. The local coroner will be contacting you shortly to arrange the funeral. Here's his number in case you want to contact him earlier. He hands her a leaflet with the morgue's contact information.
Zh: “我们已经把他送到市停尸房了。本地验尸官很快会联系你，安排葬礼事宜。如果你想提前联系他的话，这里是他的电话号码。”

En:
Is there anything else that the RCM could do for you?	他递给女人一张传单，上面有停尸房的联系方式。“RCM还能为你做些什么吗？”
0x0100009200003229	Dialogue Text	APT / WCW MAIN	Working Class Woman	Thank you." She looks down. "Thank you for telling me. I'll call, if..." She runs out of breath.
Zh: “谢谢你。”她低下头。“感谢你告诉我这个消息。我会打电话的，如果……”她有些喘不过气。

En: Again, I'm very sorry, ma'am. [Set the library card by her. Leave the room.]
Zh: “再次向你表达我的遗憾，夫人。”[把借书证放在她身边。离开房间。]

En: We've taken him to the city morgue. The local coroner will be contacting you shortly to arrange the funeral. In the meantime, you can always call the RCM if you have any questions.
Zh: “我们已经把他送到市停尸房了。本地验尸官很快会联系你，安排葬礼事宜。同时，如果你有什么问题的话，可以随时打给RCM。”

En: You know the answer to this. Oddly, you just do.
Zh: 你知道答案。很奇怪，但你就是知道。

En: I see. Well, thank you for... telling me. She rubs her face, runs her fingers over cheeks that have become numb.
Zh: “我明白了。呃，谢谢你……告诉我这个消息。”她摩挲着自己的脸庞，手指抚摸着早已麻木的面颊。

En: Thank you for telling, me officer... She looks down.
Zh: “感谢你告诉我，警官……”她低下头。

En: Jolie is sixteen and Jennie is turning eighteen next month.
Zh: “朱莉16岁，珍妮下个月18岁。”

En: What was he supposed to do in the library?
Zh: “他打算去图书馆做什么？”

En: Just to return a book of mine, why? She looks at you quizzically. "Why all those questions, detective? Did he..."
Zh: “帮我还本书，为什么这么问？”她疑惑地看着你。“为什么要问这些问题，警探？他是不是……”

En: What was the name of the book?
Zh: “书的名字是什么？”

En: Was it 'The Glinting Curve' by M. Thibault?
Zh: “是M·蒂博的‘闪耀曲线’吗？”

En: I think it was Thibault's 'The Glinting Curve'. She tucks a strand of hair behind her ear. "It was pretty good..."
Zh: “我想应该是蒂博的‘闪耀曲线’。”她把一缕头发拨到耳后。“挺不错的……”

En: Yes. She becomes unsettled as she says that, her forehead scrunching up into a worried expression.
Zh: “是的。”她的话语里有一些不稳定的情绪，前额皱成了一副忧虑的神情。

En: Um, it's just your average brown leather jacket. But he bought it as a teenager, so...
Zh: “呃，只是一件普通的深棕色皮夹克。不过衣服是他十几岁的时候买的，所以……”

En: No distinctive features?
Zh: “没有什么显著的特征吗？”

En: No, well... She stops. "The lining is hand-sewn. It's blue; I tried to make the thing more weatherproof since he's running around with it in the middle of winter."
Zh: “没有，呃……”她停了下来。“内衬是手工缝制的，蓝色；我想让夹克变得更防风雨一些，因为他总是在隆冬的时候穿着它到处跑。”

En: What about the inner lining?
Zh: “那内衬呢？”

En: Yes... She stops. "The lining is hand-sewn. It's blue; I tried to make the thing more weatherproof since he's running around with it in the middle of winter."
Zh: “是的……”她停了下来。“内衬是手工缝制的，蓝色；我想让夹克变得更防风雨一些，因为他总是在隆冬的时候穿着它到处跑。”

En: Yes, I remember, you mentioned them at the bookstand.
Zh: “是的，我记得，你在书摊前面提到过。”

En: Mhmh, they came home later. They're good girls.
Zh: “嗯，后来她们回来了。她们都是好姑娘。”

En: The lieutenant has taken off his foggy glasses and is busy cleaning them in his handkerchief for now. You're on your own here.
Zh: 警督取下满是雾气的眼镜，正忙着用手帕擦拭。这里你只能靠自己了。

En: Condensation -- from stepping inside.
Zh: 冷凝——因为刚走进屋内。

En: He must feel vulnerable without his glasses. Is this why he's letting you take the lead?
Zh: 没有眼镜的他一定感觉很脆弱。这就是他让你主导的原因吗？

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: A phone book lies open on the table, covering a stack of utility bills. Right next to it, in plain sight, sits a small bottle of amphetamine, conveniently equipped with a straw.
Zh: 一本电话簿躺在桌子上，压在一叠物业账单上面。就在它旁边，一小瓶安非他命就摆在十分显眼的地方，还方便地配了一根吸管。

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: Lieutenant, I've located psychoactive substances on this table.
Zh: “警督，我在桌子上发现了精神活性物质。”

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: Take the speed from the table.
Zh: 从桌子上拿走思必得。

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: Good, confiscate it.
Zh: “很好，没收了。”

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: You pocket the bottle as if it were the most natural thing in the world.
Zh: 你把瓶子塞进口袋，好像这是世界上最自然的一件事情。

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: Don't wait, celebrate! Blast that shit right here! Take inventory of it once this boring table shit is done!
Zh: 别等了，现在就庆祝吧！把这玩意一口气干个精光！等搜查完这张无聊的桌子，马上就去清点一下存货！

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: You could slip the bottle right into your pocket -- the lieutenant wouldn't even notice...
Zh: 你可以把瓶子直接塞进口袋——警督甚至都不会注意到……

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: Blast it before you face the beast, de Ruyter. You're going to need the encouragement.
Zh: 在你面对那头野兽，德鲁伊特之前，一口气干了它。你需要一点鼓励。

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: Score! It's right there, baby. Today's your lucky day.
Zh: 找到了！它就在那里，宝贝。今天是你的幸运日。

ConvTitle: APT / WRITING DESK IN CUNO'S APT
En: The minuscule amount of amphetamine doesn't interest the lieutenant in the slightest. He listens instead to something in the other room.
Zh: 这么少量的安非他命，警督压根就不会感兴趣的。他在听其他房间的动静。
